Document write path: add, replace, and delete documents.\n//!\n//! Implements P2.2 write path:\n//! - Primary key extraction on the hot path\n//! - `_miroir_shard` injection\n//! - Reserved field rejection\n//! - Two-rule quorum\n//!\n//! Implements P2.5 task reconciliation:\n//! - Collects per-node task UIDs\n//! - Registers Miroir task ID (mtask-<uuid>)\n//! - Returns mtask ID to client\n//!\n//! Implements §13.6 session pinning:\n//! - Records writes with session header\n//! - Tracks pinned group (first to reach quorum)\n\nuse axum::extract::{Extension, Path, Query};\nuse axum::response::{IntoResponse, Response};\nuse axum::http::{StatusCode, header};\nuse axum::{Json, Router};\nuse miroir_core::api_error::{MiroirCode, MeilisearchError};\nuse miroir_core::router::{shard_for_key, write_targets_with_migration};\nuse miroir_core::scatter::{DeleteByIdsRequest, DeleteByFilterRequest, NodeClient, WriteRequest, WriteResponse};\nuse miroir_core::topology::{Topology, NodeId};\nuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\nuse serde_json::Value;\nuse std::collections::HashMap;\nuse std::sync::Arc;\nuse tracing::{debug, info, instrument};\n\nuse crate::client::HttpClient;\nuse crate::middleware::OptionalSessionId;\nuse crate::routes::admin_endpoints::AppState;\n\n/// Document write parameters from query string.\n#[derive(Debug, Deserialize)]\npub struct DocumentsParams {\n    primaryKey: Option<String>,\n}\n\n/// Task response (Meilisearch-compatible).\n#[derive(Debug, Serialize)]\npub struct TaskResponse {\n    taskUid: u64,\n    indexUid: String,\n    status: String,\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    error: Option<Value>,\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    #[serde(rename = \"type\")]\n    error_type: Option<String>,\n}\n\n/// Response for write operations.\n#[derive(Debug, Serialize)]\npub struct DocumentsWriteResponse {\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    taskUid: Option<String>, // Changed to String to hold mtask-<uuid>\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    indexUid: Option<String>,\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    status: Option<String>,\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    error: Option<String>,\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    #[serde(rename = \"type\")]\n    error_type: Option<String>,\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    code: Option<String>,\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    link: Option<String>,\n}\n\n/// Header name for degraded write responses.\npub const HEADER_MIROIR_DEGRADED: &str = \"X-Miroir-Degraded\";\n\n/// Quorum tracking state for write operations.\n#[derive(Debug, Default)]\nstruct QuorumState {\n    /// Per-group ACK counts: group_id -> successful_ack_count\n    group_acks: HashMap<u32, usize>,\n    /// Per-group total node counts: group_id -> total_nodes_attempted\n    group_totals: HashMap<u32, usize>,\n    /// Groups that met quorum: group_id -> true\n    groups_met_quorum: HashMap<u32, bool>,\n    /// Total degraded groups count\n    degraded_groups: u32,\n}\n\nimpl QuorumState {\n    /// Record a write attempt to a node.\n    fn record_attempt(&mut self, group_id: u32, _node_id: &NodeId) {\n        *self.group_totals.entry(group_id).or_insert(0) += 1;\n    }\n\n    /// Record a successful write ACK from a node.\n    fn record_success(&mut self, group_id: u32, _node_id: &NodeId) {\n        *self.group_acks.entry(group_id).or_insert(0) += 1;\n    }\n\n    /// Record a failed write attempt from a node.\n    fn record_failure(&mut self, _group_id: u32) {\n        // Track that this group had a failure\n        // Degraded is determined after checking quorum\n    }\n\n    /// Check if a group has met quorum: floor(RF/2) + 1 ACKs required.\n    fn check_group_quorum(&mut self, group_id: u32, rf: usize) -> bool {\n        let acks = *self.group_acks.get(&group_id).unwrap_or(&0);\n        let quorum = (rf / 2) + 1;\n        let met = acks >= quorum;\n        *self.groups_met_quorum.entry(group_id).or_insert(false) = met;\n        met\n    }\n\n    /// Count how many groups met quorum.\n    fn count_quorum_groups(&self) -> usize {\n        self.groups_met_quorum.values().filter(|&&v| v).count()\n    }\n\n    /// Count degraded groups (groups that exist but didn't meet quorum).\n    fn count_degraded_groups(&mut self, replica_group_count: u32, rf: usize) -> u32 {\n        let mut degraded = 0u32;\n        for group_id in 0..replica_group_count {\n            if !self.check_group_quorum(group_id, rf) {\n                // Only count as degraded if we attempted to write to this group\n                if self.group_totals.contains_key(&group_id) {\n                    degraded += 1;\n                }\n            }\n        }\n        degraded\n    }\n}\n\n/// Build router for document endpoints.\npub fn router<S>() -> Router<S>\nwhere\n    S: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,\n{\n    Router::new()\n        .route(\"/\", axum::routing::post(post_documents))\n        .route(\"/\", axum::routing::put(put_documents))\n        .route(\"/\", axum::routing::delete(delete_documents))\n        .route(\"/:id\", axum::routing::delete(delete_document_by_id))\n}\n\n/// POST /indexes/{uid}/documents - Add documents.\n#[instrument(skip_all, fields(index = %index))]\nasync fn post_documents(\n    Path(index): Path<String>,\n    Query(params): Query<DocumentsParams>,\n    Extension(state): Extension<Arc<AppState>>,\n    session_id_wrapper: OptionalSessionId,\n    headers: axum::http::HeaderMap,\n    Json(documents): Json<Vec<Value>>,\n) -> std::result::Result<Response, MeilisearchError> {\n    // Extract session ID from OptionalSessionId extractor (set by session_pinning_middleware)\n    let sid: Option<String> = session_id_wrapper.0.and_then(|sid| {\n        if sid.0.is_empty() { None } else { Some(sid.0.clone()) }\n    });\n\n    // Extract idempotency key (plan §13.10)\n    let idempotency_key = headers\n        .get(\"Idempotency-Key\")\n        .and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok())\n        .map(|s| s.to_string());\n\n    write_documents_impl(index, params.primaryKey, documents, &state, sid, idempotency_key).await\n}\n\n/// PUT /indexes/{uid}/documents - Replace documents.\n#[instrument(skip_all, fields(index = %index))]\nasync fn put_documents(\n    Path(index): Path<String>,\n    Query(params): Query<DocumentsParams>,\n    Extension(state): Extension<Arc<AppState>>,\n    OptionalSessionId(session_id): OptionalSessionId,\n    headers: axum::http::HeaderMap,\n    Json(documents): Json<Vec<Value>>,\n) -> std::result::Result<Response, MeilisearchError> {\n    let sid: Option<String> = session_id.and_then(|sid| {\n        if sid.0.is_empty() { None } else { Some(sid.0.clone()) }\n    });\n\n    // Extract idempotency key (plan §13.10)\n    let idempotency_key = headers\n        .get(\"Idempotency-Key\")\n        .and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok())\n        .map(|s| s.to_string());\n\n    write_documents_impl(index, params.primaryKey, documents, &state, sid, idempotency_key).await\n}\n\n/// DELETE /indexes/{uid}/documents - Delete by IDs or filter.\nasync fn delete_documents(\n    Path(index): Path<String>,\n    Extension(state): Extension<Arc<AppState>>,\n    OptionalSessionId(session_id): OptionalSessionId,\n    headers: axum::http::HeaderMap,\n    Json(body): Json<Value>,\n) -> std::result::Result<Response, MeilisearchError> {\n    let sid: Option<String> = session_id.and_then(|sid| {\n        if sid.0.is_empty() { None } else { Some(sid.0.clone()) }\n    });\n\n    // Extract idempotency key (plan §13.10)\n    let idempotency_key = headers\n        .get(\"Idempotency-Key\")\n        .and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok())\n        .map(|s| s.to_string());\n\n    // Try to parse as delete by filter first\n    if let Some(filter) = body.get(\"filter\") {\n        let req = DeleteByFilterRequest {\n            index_uid: index.clone(),\n            filter: filter.clone(),\n            origin: None, // Client write\n        };\n        return delete_by_filter_impl(index, req, &state, sid, idempotency_key).await;\n    }\n\n    // Try to parse as delete by IDs\n    if let Some(ids) = body.get(\"ids\").and_then(|v| v.as_array()) {\n        let ids: Vec<String> = ids\n            .iter()\n            .filter_map(|v| v.as_str().map(|s| s.to_string()))\n            .collect();\n        if !ids.is_empty() {\n            let req = DeleteByIdsRequest {\n                index_uid: index.clone(),\n                ids,\n                origin: None, // Client write\n            };\n            return delete_by_ids_impl(index, req, &state, sid, idempotency_key).await;\n        }\n    }\n\n    // If we get here, the request body is malformed\n    Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n        MiroirCode::ReservedField,\n        \"delete request must include either 'filter' or 'ids' field\",\n    ))\n}\n\n/// DELETE /indexes/{uid}/documents/{id} - Delete single document by ID.\nasync fn delete_document_by_id(\n    Path((index, id)): Path<(String, String)>,\n    Extension(state): Extension<Arc<AppState>>,\n    OptionalSessionId(session_id): OptionalSessionId,\n    headers: axum::http::HeaderMap,\n) -> std::result::Result<Response, MeilisearchError> {\n    let sid: Option<String> = session_id.and_then(|sid| {\n        if sid.0.is_empty() { None } else { Some(sid.0.clone()) }\n    });\n\n    // Extract idempotency key (plan §13.10)\n    let idempotency_key = headers\n        .get(\"Idempotency-Key\")\n        .and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok())\n        .map(|s| s.to_string());\n\n    let req = DeleteByIdsRequest {\n        index_uid: index.clone(),\n        ids: vec![id],\n        origin: None, // Client write\n    };\n    delete_by_ids_impl(index, req, &state, sid, idempotency_key).await\n}\n\n/// Implementation for write documents (POST/PUT).\n#[instrument(skip_all, fields(index = %index, session_id))]\nasync fn write_documents_impl(\n    index: String,\n    primary_key: Option<String>,\n    mut documents: Vec<Value>,\n    state: &AppState,\n    session_id: Option<String>,\n    idempotency_key: Option<String>,\n) -> std::result::Result<Response, MeilisearchError> {\n    if documents.is_empty() {\n        return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n            MiroirCode::PrimaryKeyRequired,\n            \"cannot write empty document batch\",\n        ));\n    }\n\n    // 0.5. Check idempotency cache (plan §13.10)\n    if state.config.idempotency.enabled {\n        if let Some(ref key) = idempotency_key {\n            // Compute SHA256 hash of the request body\n            use sha2::{Digest, Sha256};\n            let body_hash = format!(\"{:x}\", Sha256::digest(serde_json::to_string(&documents).unwrap_or_default()));\n\n            // Check cache\n            match state.idempotency_cache.check(key, &body_hash).await {\n                Ok(Some(cached_mtask_id)) => {\n                    // Idempotency hit: return cached mtask ID\n                    state.metrics.inc_idempotency_hit(\"dedup\");\n                    return build_response_with_degraded_header(\n                        DocumentsWriteResponse {\n                            taskUid: Some(cached_mtask_id),\n                            indexUid: Some(index.clone()),\n                            status: Some(\"enqueued\".to_string()),\n                            error: None,\n                            error_type: None,\n                            code: None,\n                            link: None,\n                        },\n                        0, // No degraded groups for cached response\n                    );\n                }\n                Ok(None) => {\n                    // Cache miss - proceed with processing\n                    state.metrics.inc_idempotency_hit(\"miss\");\n                }\n                Err(miroir_core::error::MiroirError::IdempotencyKeyReused) => {\n                    // Key exists but body hash differs\n                    state.metrics.inc_idempotency_hit(\"conflict\");\n                    return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n                        MiroirCode::IdempotencyKeyReused,\n                        \"idempotency key was already used with a different request body\",\n                    ));\n                }\n                Err(e) => {\n                    // Other error - log but proceed (best-effort caching)\n                    tracing::warn!(error = %e, \"idempotency cache check failed, proceeding with write\");\n                    state.metrics.inc_idempotency_hit(\"miss\");\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    // 1. Resolve alias to concrete index UID (plan §13.7)\n    // Aliases are resolved before any processing; writes to multi-target aliases are rejected\n    let index_uid = if state.config.aliases.enabled {\n        // Check if the index is an alias\n        let resolved = state.alias_registry.resolve(&index).await;\n        if resolved != vec![index.clone()] {\n            // It was an alias\n            // Check if it's a multi-target alias (read-only, ILM-managed)\n            if state.alias_registry.is_multi_target_alias(&index).await {\n                return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n                    MiroirCode::MultiAliasNotWritable,\n                    format!(\n                        \"alias '{}' is a multi-target alias and is read-only (managed by ILM); writes must go to the concrete index or the write alias\",\n                        index\n                    ),\n                ));\n            }\n            // Single-target alias: record resolution metric and use the target\n            state.metrics.inc_alias_resolution(&index);\n            resolved.into_iter().next().unwrap_or_else(|| index.clone())\n        } else {\n            // Not an alias, use the index name as-is\n            index.clone()\n        }\n    } else {\n        index.clone()\n    };\n\n    // 1. Extract primary key from first document if not provided\n    let primary_key = primary_key.or_else(|| {\n        documents\n            .first()\n            .and_then(|doc| extract_primary_key(doc))\n    });\n\n    let primary_key = primary_key.ok_or_else(|| {\n        MeilisearchError::new(\n            MiroirCode::PrimaryKeyRequired,\n            format!(\"primary key required for index `{}`\", index),\n        )\n    })?;\n\n    // 2. Validate all documents have the primary key and check for reserved field\n    let anti_entropy_enabled = state.config.anti_entropy.enabled;\n    let updated_at_field = &state.config.anti_entropy.updated_at_field;\n    let ttl_enabled = state.config.ttl.enabled;\n    let expires_at_field = &state.config.ttl.expires_at_field;\n\n    for (i, doc) in documents.iter().enumerate() {\n        // Check for reserved field BEFORE checking primary key (per acceptance criteria)\n        // _miroir_shard is ALWAYS reserved (plan §5)\n        if doc.get(\"_miroir_shard\").is_some() {\n            return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n                MiroirCode::ReservedField,\n                \"document contains reserved field `_miroir_shard`\",\n            ));\n        }\n\n        // _miroir_updated_at is reserved ONLY when anti_entropy.enabled: true (plan §5, §13.8)\n        if anti_entropy_enabled && doc.get(updated_at_field).is_some() {\n            return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n                MiroirCode::ReservedField,\n                format!(\"document contains reserved field `{}` (reserved when anti_entropy.enabled: true)\", updated_at_field),\n            ));\n        }\n\n        // _miroir_expires_at is reserved ONLY when ttl.enabled: true (plan §5, §13.14)\n        // When reserved, clients cannot SET it; the orchestrator controls it. When disabled,\n        // client values pass through end-to-end.\n        if ttl_enabled && doc.get(expires_at_field).is_some() {\n            return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n                MiroirCode::ReservedField,\n                format!(\"document contains reserved field `{}` (reserved when ttl.enabled: true)\", expires_at_field),\n            ));\n        }\n\n        if doc.get(&primary_key).is_none() {\n            return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n                MiroirCode::PrimaryKeyRequired,\n                format!(\n                    \"document at index {} missing primary key field `{}`\",\n                    i, primary_key\n                ),\n            ));\n        }\n    }\n\n    // 3. Inject _miroir_shard and _miroir_updated_at into each document\n    let topology = state.topology.read().await;\n    let shard_count = topology.shards;\n    let rf = topology.rf();\n    let replica_group_count = topology.replica_group_count();\n\n    // Get current timestamp in milliseconds since epoch for _miroir_updated_at stamping\n    let now_ms = if anti_entropy_enabled {\n        Some(\n            std::time::SystemTime::now()\n                .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)\n                .unwrap_or_default()\n                .as_millis() as u64\n        )\n    } else {\n        None\n    };\n\n    for doc in &mut documents {\n        if let Some(pk_value) = doc.get(&primary_key).and_then(|v| v.as_str()) {\n            let shard_id = shard_for_key(pk_value, shard_count);\n            doc[\"_miroir_shard\"] = serde_json::json!(shard_id);\n        }\n\n        // Stamp _miroir_updated_at when anti_entropy is enabled (plan §13.8)\n        // This happens AFTER reserved field validation, so orchestrator-controlled injection is allowed\n        if let Some(timestamp) = now_ms {\n            doc[updated_at_field] = serde_json::json!(timestamp);\n        }\n    }\n\n    // 4. Group documents by target nodes (per-batch grouping for efficient fan-out)\n    let node_documents = group_documents_by_shard(&documents, &primary_key, &topology)?;\n\n    // 5. Fan out to nodes and track quorum\n    let client = HttpClient::new(\n        state.config.node_master_key.clone(),\n        state.config.scatter.node_timeout_ms,\n    );\n\n    let mut quorum_state = QuorumState::default();\n    let mut node_task_uids: HashMap<String, u64> = HashMap::new();\n\n    // For each shard, write to all RF nodes in each replica group (with dual-write support)\n    for (shard_id, docs) in node_documents {\n        // Get migration coordinator reference for dual-write detection\n        let migration_coordinator = state.migration_coordinator.as_ref().map(|c| {\n            // We need a read lock on the coordinator\n            tokio::task::block_in_place(|| {\n                tokio::runtime::Handle::current().block_on(async {\n                    c.read().await\n                })\n            })\n        });\n\n        // Use migration-aware routing\n        let targets = write_targets_with_migration(\n            shard_id,\n            &topology,\n            migration_coordinator.as_deref(),\n        );\n\n        if targets.is_empty() {\n            return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n                MiroirCode::ShardUnavailable,\n                format!(\"no available nodes for shard {}\", shard_id),\n            ));\n        }\n\n        // Track which groups we're targeting for this shard\n\n        for node_id in targets {\n            let node = topology\n                .node(&node_id)\n                .ok_or_else(|| MeilisearchError::new(MiroirCode::ShardUnavailable, \"node not found in topology\"))?;\n\n            let group_id = node.replica_group;\n            quorum_state.record_attempt(group_id, &node_id);\n\n            let req = WriteRequest {\n                index_uid: index_uid.clone(),\n                documents: docs.clone(),\n                primary_key: Some(primary_key.clone()),\n                origin: None, // Client write\n            };\n\n            match client.write_documents(&node_id, &node.address, &req).await {\n                Ok(resp) if resp.success => {\n                    quorum_state.record_success(group_id, &node_id);\n                    if let Some(task_uid) = resp.task_uid {\n                        node_task_uids.insert(node_id.as_str().to_string(), task_uid);\n                    }\n                }\n                Ok(resp) => {\n                    // Non-success response (validation error, etc.)\n                    return Ok(build_json_error_response(build_error_response(resp)));\n                }\n                Err(_) => {\n                    quorum_state.record_failure(group_id);\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    // 6. Apply two-rule quorum logic\n    let degraded_groups = quorum_state.count_degraded_groups(replica_group_count, rf);\n    let quorum_groups = quorum_state.count_quorum_groups();\n\n    // Write success if at least one group met quorum\n    if quorum_groups == 0 {\n        return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n            MiroirCode::NoQuorum,\n            \"no replica group met quorum\",\n        ));\n    }\n\n    // 6.5. Find the first group to reach quorum (for session pinning, plan §13.6)\n    // Groups are checked in ascending order, so the first one with quorum is the first\n    let first_quorum_group = (0..replica_group_count)\n        .find(|&group_id| {\n            let acks = *quorum_state.group_acks.get(&group_id).unwrap_or(&0);\n            let quorum = (rf / 2) + 1;\n            acks >= quorum\n        })\n        .unwrap_or(0); // Default to group 0 if somehow no quorum (shouldn't happen here)\n\n    // 7. Register Miroir task with collected node task UIDs\n    let miroir_task = state\n        .task_registry\n        .register_with_metadata(\n            node_task_uids.clone(),\n            Some(index_uid.clone()),\n            Some(\"documentAdditionOrUpdate\".to_string()),\n        )\n        .map_err(|e| MeilisearchError::new(\n            MiroirCode::ShardUnavailable,\n            format!(\"failed to register task: {}\", e),\n        ))?;\n\n    // 7.5. Record session pinning if session header present (plan §13.6)\n    if let (Some(ref sid), true) = (&session_id, state.session_manager.is_enabled()) {\n        if let Err(e) = state.session_manager.record_write_with_quorum(\n            sid,\n            miroir_task.miroir_id.clone(),\n            first_quorum_group,\n        ).await {\n            // Log error but don't fail the write - session pinning is best-effort\n            tracing::error!(\n                session_id = %sid,\n                error = %e,\n                \"failed to record session pinning for write\"\n            );\n        }\n    }\n\n    // 7.6. Insert into idempotency cache if key was provided (plan §13.10)\n    if state.config.idempotency.enabled {\n        if let Some(ref key) = idempotency_key {\n            use sha2::{Digest, Sha256};\n            let body_hash = format!(\"{:x}\", Sha256::digest(serde_json::to_string(&documents).unwrap_or_default()));\n            state.idempotency_cache.insert(\n                key.clone(),\n                body_hash,\n                miroir_task.miroir_id.clone(),\n            ).await;\n            // Update cache size metric\n            state.metrics.set_idempotency_cache_size(state.idempotency_cache.size().await as u64);\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Build success response with degraded header and mtask ID\n    build_response_with_degraded_header(\n        DocumentsWriteResponse {\n            taskUid: Some(miroir_task.miroir_id),\n            indexUid: Some(index_uid.clone()),\n            status: Some(\"enqueued\".to_string()),\n            error: None,\n            error_type: None,\n            code: None,\n            link: None,\n        },\n        degraded_groups,\n    )\n}\n\n/// Implementation for delete by IDs.\nasync fn delete_by_ids_impl(\n    index: String,\n    req: DeleteByIdsRequest,\n    state: &AppState,\n    session_id: Option<String>,\n    idempotency_key: Option<String>,\n) -> std::result::Result<Response, MeilisearchError> {\n    if req.ids.is_empty() {\n        return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n            MiroirCode::PrimaryKeyRequired,\n            \"cannot delete empty ID list\",\n        ));\n    }\n\n    // 0.5. Check idempotency cache (plan §13.10)\n    if state.config.idempotency.enabled {\n        if let Some(ref key) = idempotency_key {\n            use sha2::{Digest, Sha256};\n            let body_hash = format!(\"{:x}\", Sha256::digest(serde_json::to_string(&req).unwrap_or_default()));\n\n            match state.idempotency_cache.check(key, &body_hash).await {\n                Ok(Some(cached_mtask_id)) => {\n                    state.metrics.inc_idempotency_hit(\"dedup\");\n                    return build_response_with_degraded_header(\n                        DocumentsWriteResponse {\n                            taskUid: Some(cached_mtask_id),\n                            indexUid: Some(index.clone()),\n                            status: Some(\"enqueued\".to_string()),\n                            error: None,\n                            error_type: None,\n                            code: None,\n                            link: None,\n                        },\n                        0,\n                    );\n                }\n                Ok(None) => {\n                    state.metrics.inc_idempotency_hit(\"miss\");\n                }\n                Err(miroir_core::error::MiroirError::IdempotencyKeyReused) => {\n                    state.metrics.inc_idempotency_hit(\"conflict\");\n                    return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n                        MiroirCode::IdempotencyKeyReused,\n                        \"idempotency key was already used with a different request body\",\n                    ));\n                }\n                Err(e) => {\n                    tracing::warn!(error = %e, \"idempotency cache check failed, proceeding with delete\");\n                    state.metrics.inc_idempotency_hit(\"miss\");\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Resolve alias to concrete index UID (plan §13.7)\n    let index_uid = if state.config.aliases.enabled {\n        let resolved = state.alias_registry.resolve(&index).await;\n        if resolved != vec![index.clone()] {\n            // It was an alias - check if it's a multi-target alias (read-only)\n            if state.alias_registry.is_multi_target_alias(&index).await {\n                return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n                    MiroirCode::MultiAliasNotWritable,\n                    format!(\n                        \"alias '{}' is a multi-target alias and is read-only (managed by ILM); deletes must go to the concrete index or the write alias\",\n                        index\n                    ),\n                ));\n            }\n            // Single-target alias: record resolution metric and use the target\n            state.metrics.inc_alias_resolution(&index);\n            resolved.into_iter().next().unwrap_or_else(|| index.clone())\n        } else {\n            index.clone()\n        }\n    } else {\n        index.clone()\n    };\n\n    let topology = state.topology.read().await;\n    let rf = topology.rf();\n    let replica_group_count = topology.replica_group_count();\n\n    // Group IDs by target shard for independent per-shard routing\n    let mut shard_ids: HashMap<u32, Vec<String>> = HashMap::new();\n    for id in &req.ids {\n        let shard_id = shard_for_key(id, topology.shards);\n        shard_ids.entry(shard_id).or_default().push(id.clone());\n    }\n\n    let client = HttpClient::new(\n        state.config.node_master_key.clone(),\n        state.config.scatter.node_timeout_ms,\n    );\n\n    let mut quorum_state = QuorumState::default();\n    let mut node_task_uids: HashMap<String, u64> = HashMap::new();\n\n    // For each shard, write to all RF nodes in each replica group\n    for (shard_id, ids) in shard_ids {\n        let targets = miroir_core::router::write_targets(shard_id, &topology);\n\n        if targets.is_empty() {\n            return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n                MiroirCode::ShardUnavailable,\n                format!(\"no available nodes for shard {}\", shard_id),\n            ));\n        }\n\n        for node_id in targets {\n            let node = topology\n                .node(&node_id)\n                .ok_or_else(|| MeilisearchError::new(MiroirCode::ShardUnavailable, \"node not found in topology\"))?;\n\n            let group_id = node.replica_group;\n            quorum_state.record_attempt(group_id, &node_id);\n\n            let delete_req = DeleteByIdsRequest {\n                index_uid: index_uid.clone(),\n                ids: ids.clone(),\n                origin: None, // Client write\n            };\n\n            match client.delete_documents(&node_id, &node.address, &delete_req).await {\n                Ok(resp) if resp.success => {\n                    quorum_state.record_success(group_id, &node_id);\n                    if let Some(task_uid) = resp.task_uid {\n                        node_task_uids.insert(node_id.as_str().to_string(), task_uid);\n                    }\n                }\n                Ok(resp) => {\n                    return Ok(build_json_error_response(build_error_response(resp)));\n                }\n                Err(_) => {\n                    quorum_state.record_failure(group_id);\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Apply two-rule quorum logic\n    let degraded_groups = quorum_state.count_degraded_groups(replica_group_count, rf);\n    let quorum_groups = quorum_state.count_quorum_groups();\n\n    // Write success if at least one group met quorum\n    if quorum_groups == 0 {\n        return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n            MiroirCode::NoQuorum,\n            \"no replica group met quorum\",\n        ));\n    }\n\n    // Find the first group to reach quorum (for session pinning, plan §13.6)\n    let first_quorum_group = (0..replica_group_count)\n        .find(|&group_id| {\n            let acks = *quorum_state.group_acks.get(&group_id).unwrap_or(&0);\n            let quorum = (rf / 2) + 1;\n            acks >= quorum\n        })\n        .unwrap_or(0);\n\n    // Register Miroir task with collected node task UIDs\n    let miroir_task = state\n        .task_registry\n        .register_with_metadata(\n            node_task_uids.clone(),\n            Some(index.clone()),\n            Some(\"documentDeletion\".to_string()),\n        )\n        .map_err(|e| MeilisearchError::new(\n            MiroirCode::ShardUnavailable,\n            format!(\"failed to register task: {}\", e),\n        ))?;\n\n    // Record session pinning if session header present (plan §13.6)\n    if let (Some(ref sid), true) = (&session_id, state.session_manager.is_enabled()) {\n        if let Err(e) = state.session_manager.record_write_with_quorum(\n            sid,\n            miroir_task.miroir_id.clone(),\n            first_quorum_group,\n        ).await {\n            tracing::error!(\n                session_id = %sid,\n                error = %e,\n                \"failed to record session pinning for delete\"\n            );\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Insert into idempotency cache if key was provided (plan §13.10)\n    if state.config.idempotency.enabled {\n        if let Some(ref key) = idempotency_key {\n            use sha2::{Digest, Sha256};\n            let body_hash = format!(\"{:x}\", Sha256::digest(serde_json::to_string(&req).unwrap_or_default()));\n            state.idempotency_cache.insert(\n                key.clone(),\n                body_hash,\n                miroir_task.miroir_id.clone(),\n            ).await;\n            state.metrics.set_idempotency_cache_size(state.idempotency_cache.size().await as u64);\n        }\n    }\n\n    build_response_with_degraded_header(\n        DocumentsWriteResponse {\n            taskUid: Some(miroir_task.miroir_id),\n            indexUid: Some(index.clone()),\n            status: Some(\"enqueued\".to_string()),\n            error: None,\n            error_type: None,\n            code: None,\n            link: None,\n        },\n        degraded_groups,\n    )\n}\n\n/// Implementation for delete by filter (broadcast to all nodes).\nasync fn delete_by_filter_impl(\n    index: String,\n    req: DeleteByFilterRequest,\n    state: &AppState,\n    session_id: Option<String>,\n    idempotency_key: Option<String>,\n) -> std::result::Result<Response, MeilisearchError> {\n    // 0.5. Check idempotency cache (plan §13.10)\n    if state.config.idempotency.enabled {\n        if let Some(ref key) = idempotency_key {\n            use sha2::{Digest, Sha256};\n            let body_hash = format!(\"{:x}\", Sha256::digest(serde_json::to_string(&req).unwrap_or_default()));\n\n            match state.idempotency_cache.check(key, &body_hash).await {\n                Ok(Some(cached_mtask_id)) => {\n                    state.metrics.inc_idempotency_hit(\"dedup\");\n                    return build_response_with_degraded_header(\n                        DocumentsWriteResponse {\n                            taskUid: Some(cached_mtask_id),\n                            indexUid: Some(index.clone()),\n                            status: Some(\"enqueued\".to_string()),\n                            error: None,\n                            error_type: None,\n                            code: None,\n                            link: None,\n                        },\n                        0,\n                    );\n                }\n                Ok(None) => {\n                    state.metrics.inc_idempotency_hit(\"miss\");\n                }\n                Err(miroir_core::error::MiroirError::IdempotencyKeyReused) => {\n                    state.metrics.inc_idempotency_hit(\"conflict\");\n                    return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n                        MiroirCode::IdempotencyKeyReused,\n                        \"idempotency key was already used with a different request body\",\n                    ));\n                }\n                Err(e) => {\n                    tracing::warn!(error = %e, \"idempotency cache check failed, proceeding with delete by filter\");\n                    state.metrics.inc_idempotency_hit(\"miss\");\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    let topology = state.topology.read().await;\n    let rf = topology.rf();\n    let replica_group_count = topology.replica_group_count();\n\n    let client = HttpClient::new(\n        state.config.node_master_key.clone(),\n        state.config.scatter.node_timeout_ms,\n    );\n\n    let mut quorum_state = QuorumState::default();\n    let mut node_task_uids: HashMap<String, u64> = HashMap::new();\n\n    // Broadcast to all nodes (cannot shard-route for filters)\n    for node in topology.nodes() {\n        let group_id = node.replica_group;\n        quorum_state.record_attempt(group_id, &node.id);\n\n        match client\n            .delete_documents_by_filter(&node.id, &node.address, &req)\n            .await\n        {\n            Ok(resp) if resp.success => {\n                quorum_state.record_success(group_id, &node.id);\n                if let Some(task_uid) = resp.task_uid {\n                    node_task_uids.insert(node.id.as_str().to_string(), task_uid);\n                }\n            }\n            Ok(resp) => {\n                return Ok(build_json_error_response(build_error_response(resp)));\n            }\n            Err(_) => {\n                quorum_state.record_failure(group_id);\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Apply two-rule quorum logic\n    let degraded_groups = quorum_state.count_degraded_groups(replica_group_count, rf);\n    let quorum_groups = quorum_state.count_quorum_groups();\n\n    // Write success if at least one group met quorum\n    if quorum_groups == 0 {\n        return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n            MiroirCode::NoQuorum,\n            \"no replica group met quorum\",\n        ));\n    }\n\n    // Find the first group to reach quorum (for session pinning, plan §13.6)\n    let first_quorum_group = (0..replica_group_count)\n        .find(|&group_id| {\n            let acks = *quorum_state.group_acks.get(&group_id).unwrap_or(&0);\n            let quorum = (rf / 2) + 1;\n            acks >= quorum\n        })\n        .unwrap_or(0);\n\n    // Register Miroir task with collected node task UIDs\n    let miroir_task = state\n        .task_registry\n        .register_with_metadata(\n            node_task_uids.clone(),\n            Some(index.clone()),\n            Some(\"documentDeletion\".to_string()),\n        )\n        .map_err(|e| MeilisearchError::new(\n            MiroirCode::ShardUnavailable,\n            format!(\"failed to register task: {}\", e),\n        ))?;\n\n    // Record session pinning if session header present (plan §13.6)\n    if let (Some(ref sid), true) = (&session_id, state.session_manager.is_enabled()) {\n        if let Err(e) = state.session_manager.record_write_with_quorum(\n            sid,\n            miroir_task.miroir_id.clone(),\n            first_quorum_group,\n        ).await {\n            tracing::error!(\n                session_id = %sid,\n                error = %e,\n                \"failed to record session pinning for delete by filter\"\n            );\n        }\n    }\n\n    build_response_with_degraded_header(\n        DocumentsWriteResponse {\n            taskUid: Some(miroir_task.miroir_id),\n            indexUid: Some(index.clone()),\n            status: Some(\"enqueued\".to_string()),\n            error: None,\n            error_type: None,\n            code: None,\n            link: None,\n        },\n        degraded_groups,\n    )\n}\n\n/// Extract primary key from a document by checking common field names.\n///\n/// Tries fields in order: id, pk, key, _id.\nfn extract_primary_key(doc: &Value) -> Option<String> {\n    [\"id\", \"pk\", \"key\", \"_id\"]\n        .iter()\n        .find(|&&key| doc.get(key).is_some())\n        .map(|&s| s.to_string())\n}\n\n/// Group documents by their target shard for fan-out optimization.\n///\n/// Returns a map of shard_id -> documents to send to that shard.\n/// The caller then fans out each shard's documents to all RF nodes in each group.\n///\n/// This per-batch grouping minimizes HTTP fan-out count (critical at scale).\nfn group_documents_by_shard(\n    documents: &[Value],\n    primary_key: &str,\n    topology: &Topology,\n) -> std::result::Result<HashMap<u32, Vec<Value>>, MeilisearchError> {\n    let mut shard_documents: HashMap<u32, Vec<Value>> = HashMap::new();\n\n    for doc in documents {\n        let pk_value = doc\n            .get(primary_key)\n            .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n            .ok_or_else(|| {\n                MeilisearchError::new(\n                    MiroirCode::PrimaryKeyRequired,\n                    \"primary key value must be a string\",\n                )\n            })?;\n\n        let shard_id = shard_for_key(pk_value, topology.shards);\n        shard_documents\n            .entry(shard_id)\n            .or_default()\n            .push(doc.clone());\n    }\n\n    Ok(shard_documents)\n}\n\n/// Build an error response from a node error.\nfn build_error_response(resp: WriteResponse) -> DocumentsWriteResponse {\n    DocumentsWriteResponse {\n        taskUid: resp.task_uid.map(|uid| uid.to_string()),\n        indexUid: None,\n        status: None,\n        error: resp.message,\n        error_type: resp.error_type,\n        code: resp.code,\n        link: None,\n    }\n}\n\n/// Build a success response with optional X-Miroir-Degraded header.\nfn build_response_with_degraded_header(\n    response: DocumentsWriteResponse,\n    degraded_groups: u32,\n) -> std::result::Result<Response, MeilisearchError> {\n    let body = serde_json::to_string(&response).map_err(|e| {\n        MeilisearchError::new(\n            MiroirCode::ShardUnavailable,\n            format!(\"failed to serialize response: {}\", e),\n        )\n    })?;\n\n    let mut builder = Response::builder()\n        .status(StatusCode::OK)\n        .header(header::CONTENT_TYPE, \"application/json\");\n\n    // Add X-Miroir-Degraded header if any groups were degraded\n    if degraded_groups > 0 {\n        builder = builder.header(HEADER_MIROIR_DEGRADED, format!(\"groups={}\", degraded_groups));\n    }\n\n    Ok(builder\n        .body(axum::body::Body::from(body))\n        .map_err(|e| MeilisearchError::new(\n            MiroirCode::ShardUnavailable,\n            format!(\"failed to build response: {}\", e),\n        ))?)\n}\n\n/// Build an error response as JSON (for forwarded node errors).\nfn build_json_error_response(resp: DocumentsWriteResponse) -> Response {\n    (\n        StatusCode::OK,\n        [(header::CONTENT_TYPE, \"application/json\")],\n        Json(resp),\n    )\n        .into_response()\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n    use miroir_core::api_error::{MiroirCode, MeilisearchError};\n    use serde_json::json;\n\n    #[test]\n    fn Some(\"id\".to_string()));\n\n        let doc_with_pk = serde_json::json!({\"pk\": \"test456\", \"name\": \"Test\"});\n        assert_eq!(extract_primary_key(&doc_with_pk), Some(\"pk\".to_string()));\n\n        let doc_with_key = serde_json::json!({\"key\": \"test789\", \"name\": \"Test\"});\n        assert_eq!(extract_primary_key(&doc_with_key), Some(\"key\".to_string()));\n\n        let doc_with__id = serde_json::json!({\"_id\": \"test000\", \"name\": \"Test\"});\n        assert_eq!(extract_primary_key(&doc_with__id), {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_24ad41d60ccd4f9583b2356b","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t//! Result merger: combines shard results into a single response.\n2\t//!\n3\t//! Supports pluggable merge strategies via the [`MergeStrategy`] trait.\n4\t//! The default strategy is Reciprocal Rank Fusion (RRF) with k=60.\n5\t\n6\tuse crate::Result;\n7\tuse serde_json::{Map, Value};\n8\tuse std::collections::BTreeMap;\n9\tuse std::cmp::Ordering;\n10\t\n11\t/// Input to the merge operation.\n12\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n13\tpub struct MergeInput {\n14\t    /// One response page per node in the covering set.\n15\t    pub shard_hits: Vec<ShardHitPage>,\n16\t\n17\t    /// Original offset from the client request.\n18\t    pub offset: usize,\n19\t\n20\t    /// Original limit from the client request.\n21\t    pub limit: usize,\n22\t\n23\t    /// Whether the client requested scores in the response.\n24\t    pub client_requested_score: bool,\n25\t\n26\t    /// Facet names requested (for filtering which facets to return).\n27\t    pub facets: Option<Vec<String>>,\n28\t\n29\t    /// Failed shard IDs (for X-Miroir-Degraded header).\n30\t    pub failed_shards: Vec<u32>,\n31\t}\n32\t\n33\t/// Response from a single shard (node).\n34\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n35\tpub struct ShardHitPage {\n36\t    /// Raw JSON response from the node.\n37\t    pub body: Value,\n38\t}\n39\t\n40\t/// Merged search result.\n41\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, serde::Serialize)]\n42\tpub struct MergedSearchResult {\n43\t    /// Merged hits (globally sorted, offset/limit applied).\n44\t    pub hits: Vec<Value>,\n45\t\n46\t    /// Aggregated facet distribution.\n47\t    pub facet_distribution: Option<BTreeMap<String, BTreeMap<String, u64>>>,\n48\t\n49\t    /// Estimated total hits (sum of shard totals).\n50\t    pub estimated_total_hits: u64,\n51\t\n52\t    /// Processing time in milliseconds (max across covering set).\n53\t    pub processing_time_ms: u64,\n54\t\n55\t    /// Whether the response is degraded (some shards had errors).\n56\t    pub degraded: bool,\n57\t\n58\t    /// Failed shard IDs (for X-Miroir-Degraded header).\n59\t    pub failed_shards: Vec<u32>,\n60\t}\n61\t\n62\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n63\t// Merge strategy trait\n64\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n65\t\n66\t/// Pluggable merge strategy for combining shard results.\n67\t///\n68\t/// Implementations define how hits from multiple shards are combined\n69\t/// into a single globally-ranked response. The default strategy is\n70\t/// [`RrfStrategy`] (Reciprocal Rank Fusion).\n71\tpub trait MergeStrategy: Send + Sync {\n72\t    /// Merge search results from multiple shards into a single response.\n73\t    fn merge(&self, input: MergeInput) -> Result<MergedSearchResult>;\n74\t\n75\t    /// Strategy name (for logging and `/explain` output).\n76\t    fn name(&self) -> &'static str;\n77\t}\n78\t\n79\t/// Box reference to a merge strategy (for polymorphic dispatch).\n80\tpub type DynMergeStrategy = dyn MergeStrategy;\n81\t\n82\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n83\t// RRF strategy\n84\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n85\t\n86\t/// Default RRF constant k.\n87\t///\n88\t/// The value 60 is recommended in the RRF literature and used by\n89\t/// OpenSearch for hybrid search. Smaller values amplify rank\n90\t/// differences; larger values flatten them.\n91\tpub const DEFAULT_RRF_K: u32 = 60;\n92\t\n93\t/// Reciprocal Rank Fusion merge strategy.\n94\t///\n95\t/// Each document's contribution from a shard is `1 / (k + rank + 1)`\n96\t/// where rank is the 0-based position. Documents appearing in\n97\t/// multiple shards have their contributions summed. Results are\n98\t/// sorted by total RRF score descending, with deterministic\n99\t/// tie-breaking on primary key.\n100\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n101\tpub struct RrfStrategy {\n102\t    k: u32,\n103\t}\n104\t\n105\timpl RrfStrategy {\n106\t    /// Create a new RRF strategy with the given k constant.\n107\t    pub fn new(k: u32) -> Self {\n108\t        Self { k: k.max(1) }\n109\t    }\n110\t\n111\t    /// Create with the default k=60.\n112\t    pub fn default_strategy() -> Self {\n113\t        Self::new(DEFAULT_RRF_K)\n114\t    }\n115\t\n116\t    /// Return the configured k value.\n117\t    pub fn k(&self) -> u32 {\n118\t        self.k\n119\t    }\n120\t}\n121\t\n122\timpl Default for RrfStrategy {\n123\t    fn default() -> Self {\n124\t        Self::default_strategy()\n125\t    }\n126\t}\n127\t\n128\timpl MergeStrategy for RrfStrategy {\n129\t    fn merge(&self, input: MergeInput) -> Result<MergedSearchResult> {\n130\t        rrf_merge(&self.k, input)\n131\t    }\n132\t\n133\t    fn name(&self) -> &'static str {\n134\t        \"rrf\"\n135\t    }\n136\t}\n137\t\n138\t/// A document with its accumulated RRF score.\n139\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n140\tstruct RRFDocument {\n141\t    /// Accumulated RRF score across all shards.\n142\t    rrf_score: f64,\n143\t\n144\t    /// Primary key for tie-breaking.\n145\t    primary_key: String,\n146\t\n147\t    /// The hit document (JSON object) from the highest-ranking shard.\n148\t    hit: Map<String, Value>,\n149\t}\n150\t\n151\timpl PartialEq for RRFDocument {\n152\t    fn eq(&self, other: &Self) -> bool {\n153\t        self.rrf_score == other.rrf_score && self.primary_key == other.primary_key\n154\t    }\n155\t}\n156\t\n157\timpl Eq for RRFDocument {}\n158\t\n159\timpl PartialOrd for RRFDocument {\n160\t    fn partial_cmp(&self, other: &Self) -> Option<Ordering> {\n161\t        Some(self.cmp(other))\n162\t    }\n163\t}\n164\t\n165\timpl Ord for RRFDocument {\n166\t    fn cmp(&self, other: &Self) -> Ordering {\n167\t        // Primary sort: RRF score descending (higher score = better rank)\n168\t        match self.rrf_score.partial_cmp(&other.rrf_score) {\n169\t            Some(Ordering::Equal) => {\n170\t                // Secondary sort: primary key ascending for deterministic tie-breaking\n171\t                self.primary_key.cmp(&other.primary_key)\n172\t            }\n173\t            Some(ord) => ord.reverse(),\n174\t            None => {\n175\t                // NaN case: treat as lowest score\n176\t                if self.rrf_score.is_nan() && !other.rrf_score.is_nan() {\n177\t                    Ordering::Less\n178\t                } else if !self.rrf_score.is_nan() && other.rrf_score.is_nan() {\n179\t                    Ordering::Greater\n180\t                } else {\n181\t                    Ordering::Equal\n182\t                }\n183\t            }\n184\t        }\n185\t    }\n186\t}\n187\t\n188\t/// Merge search results using the default RRF strategy (k=60).\n189\t///\n190\t/// This is a convenience wrapper around [`RrfStrategy`] for callers\n191\t/// that don't need to customise the strategy.\n192\tpub fn merge(input: MergeInput) -> Result<MergedSearchResult> {\n193\t    rrf_merge(&DEFAULT_RRF_K, input)\n194\t}\n195\t\n196\t/// Merge search results with a specific strategy.\n197\t///\n198\t/// Use this when the strategy is selected from config or when you\n199\t/// need a non-default RRF k value.\n200\tpub fn merge_with_strategy(\n201\t    strategy: &dyn MergeStrategy,\n202\t    input: MergeInput,\n203\t) -> Result<MergedSearchResult> {\n204\t    strategy.merge(input)\n205\t}\n206\t\n207\t/// Core RRF merge implementation.\n208\tfn rrf_merge(k: &u32, input: MergeInput) -> Result<MergedSearchResult> {\n209\t    let mut estimated_total_hits = 0u64;\n210\t    let mut max_processing_time = 0u64;\n211\t    let mut degraded = false;\n212\t\n213\t    // Collect all hits with their ranks from all shards.\n214\t    // Use a map to aggregate RRF scores for documents appearing in multiple shards.\n215\t    let mut rrf_map: std::collections::HashMap<String, RRFDocument> = std::collections::HashMap::new();\n216\t\n217\t    for shard_page in &input.shard_hits {\n218\t        let body = &shard_page.body;\n219\t\n220\t        // Check for degraded response.\n221\t        if let Some(serde_json::Value::Bool(false)) = body.get(\"success\") {\n222\t            degraded = true;\n223\t            continue;\n224\t        }\n225\t\n226\t        // Extract estimated total hits.\n227\t        if let Some(Value::Number(n)) = body.get(\"estimatedTotalHits\") {\n228\t            if let Some(n) = n.as_u64() {\n229\t                estimated_total_hits = estimated_total_hits.saturating_add(n);\n230\t            }\n231\t        }\n232\t\n233\t        // Extract processing time.\n234\t        if let Some(Value::Number(n)) = body.get(\"processingTimeMs\") {\n235\t            if let Some(n) = n.as_u64() {\n236\t                max_processing_time = max_processing_time.max(n);\n237\t            }\n238\t        }\n239\t\n240\t        // Extract hits with ranks (position in shard's results).\n241\t        if let Some(Value::Array(hits)) = body.get(\"hits\") {\n242\t            for (rank, hit) in hits.iter().enumerate() {\n243\t                if let Value::Object(ref map) = hit {\n244\t                    let map = map.clone();\n245\t\n246\t                    // Extract primary key for deduplication.\n247\t                    let primary_key = map\n248\t                        .get(\"id\")\n249\t                        .or_else(|| map.get(\"pk\"))\n250\t                        .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n251\t                        .unwrap_or(\"\")\n252\t                        .to_string();\n253\t\n254\t                    // RRF contribution: 1 / (k + rank + 1)\n255\t                    // rank is 0-based, so +1 converts to 1-based position.\n256\t                    let rrf_contribution = 1.0 / ((*k as f64) + (rank as f64) + 1.0);\n257\t\n258\t                    // Aggregate RRF scores across shards.\n259\t                    use std::collections::hash_map::Entry;\n260\t                    match rrf_map.entry(primary_key.clone()) {\n261\t                        Entry::Vacant(e) => {\n262\t                            e.insert(RRFDocument {\n263\t                                rrf_score: rrf_contribution,\n264\t                                primary_key,\n265\t                                hit: map,\n266\t                            });\n267\t                        }\n268\t                        Entry::Occupied(mut e) => {\n269\t                            // Document appears in multiple shards: sum RRF contributions.\n270\t                            e.get_mut().rrf_score += rrf_contribution;\n271\t                        }\n272\t                    }\n273\t                }\n274\t            }\n275\t        }\n276\t    }\n277\t\n278\t    // Convert map to vec and sort by RRF score descending.\n279\t    let mut merged_docs: Vec<_> = rrf_map.into_values().collect();\n280\t    merged_docs.sort();\n281\t\n282\t    // Apply offset + limit.\n283\t    let skip = input.offset;\n284\t    let take = input.limit;\n285\t    let paginated_hits: Vec<_> = merged_docs\n286\t        .into_iter()\n287\t        .skip(skip)\n288\t        .take(take)\n289\t        .collect();\n290\t\n291\t    // Strip reserved fields and rebuild hits.\n292\t    let mut hits = Vec::with_capacity(paginated_hits.len());\n293\t    for mut doc in paginated_hits {\n294\t        // Strip _rankingScore if not requested (RRF doesn't use original scores).\n295\t        if !input.client_requested_score {\n296\t            doc.hit.remove(\"_rankingScore\");\n297\t        }\n298\t\n299\t        // Always strip _miroir_* fields.\n300\t        doc.hit.retain(|k, _| !k.starts_with(\"_miroir_\"));\n301\t\n302\t        hits.push(Value::Object(doc.hit));\n303\t    }\n304\t\n305\t    // Merge facets.\n306\t    let facet_distribution = merge_facets(&input.shard_hits, input.facets.as_deref());\n307\t\n308\t    Ok(MergedSearchResult {\n309\t        hits,\n310\t        facet_distribution,\n311\t        estimated_total_hits,\n312\t        processing_time_ms: max_processing_time,\n313\t        degraded,\n314\t        failed_shards: input.failed_shards,\n315\t    })\n316\t}\n317\t\n318\t/// Score-based merge strategy (OP#4 global-IDF).\n319\t///\n320\t/// This merge strategy is correct **only when** the preflight phase has\n321\t/// provided global IDF so that scores are comparable across shards. It sorts\n322\t/// all hits globally by `_rankingScore` descending, with deterministic\n323\t/// tie-breaking on primary key.\n324\t///\n325\t/// Without global IDF, this strategy will produce incorrect rankings because\n326\t/// shard-local scores are not comparable across shards with different document\n327\t/// distributions.\n328\t///\n329\t/// Use with [`dfs_query_then_fetch_search`] in the scatter module.\n330\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy)]\n331\tpub struct ScoreMergeStrategy;\n332\t\n333\timpl ScoreMergeStrategy {\n334\t    /// Create a new score-based merge strategy.\n335\t    pub fn new() -> Self {\n336\t        Self\n337\t    }\n338\t}\n339\t\n340\timpl Default for ScoreMergeStrategy {\n341\t    fn default() -> Self {\n342\t        Self::new()\n343\t    }\n344\t}\n345\t\n346\timpl MergeStrategy for ScoreMergeStrategy {\n347\t    fn merge(&self, input: MergeInput) -> Result<MergedSearchResult> {\n348\t        score_merge(input)\n349\t    }\n350\t\n351\t    fn name(&self) -> &'static str {\n352\t        \"score\"\n353\t    }\n354\t}\n355\t\n356\t/// Core score-based merge implementation (OP#4 global-IDF).\n357\t///\n358\t/// This merge strategy is correct when the preflight phase has provided\n359\t/// global IDF so that scores are comparable across shards. It sorts all\n360\t/// hits globally by `_rankingScore` descending, with deterministic tie-breaking\n361\t/// on primary key.\n362\t///\n363\t/// Without global IDF, this strategy will produce incorrect rankings because\n364\t/// shard-local scores are not comparable across shards with different document\n365\t/// distributions.\n366\tfn score_merge(input: MergeInput) -> Result<MergedSearchResult> {\n367\t    let mut estimated_total_hits = 0u64;\n368\t    let mut max_processing_time = 0u64;\n369\t    let mut degraded = false;\n370\t    let mut seen_pks = std::collections::HashSet::new();\n371\t    let mut all_hits = Vec::new();\n372\t\n373\t    // Collect all hits from all shards, deduplicating by primary key.\n374\t    // Dedup is needed because execute_scatter may push the same node response\n375\t    // once per shard when multiple shards map to the same node.\n376\t    for shard_page in &input.shard_hits {\n377\t        let body = &shard_page.body;\n378\t\n379\t        // Check for degraded response.\n380\t        if let Some(serde_json::Value::Bool(false)) = body.get(\"success\") {\n381\t            degraded = true;\n382\t            continue;\n383\t        }\n384\t\n385\t        // Extract estimated total hits.\n386\t        if let Some(Value::Number(n)) = body.get(\"estimatedTotalHits\") {\n387\t            if let Some(n) = n.as_u64() {\n388\t                estimated_total_hits = estimated_total_hits.saturating_add(n);\n389\t            }\n390\t        }\n391\t\n392\t        // Extract processing time.\n393\t        if let Some(Value::Number(n)) = body.get(\"processingTimeMs\") {\n394\t            if let Some(n) = n.as_u64() {\n395\t                max_processing_time = max_processing_time.max(n);\n396\t            }\n397\t        }\n398\t\n399\t        // Extract hits.\n400\t        if let Some(Value::Array(hits)) = body.get(\"hits\") {\n401\t            for hit in hits {\n402\t                if let Value::Object(map) = hit {\n403\t                    let pk = map\n404\t                        .get(\"id\")\n405\t                        .or_else(|| map.get(\"pk\"))\n406\t                        .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n407\t                        .unwrap_or(\"\")\n408\t                        .to_string();\n409\t                    if seen_pks.insert(pk) {\n410\t                        all_hits.push(map.clone());\n411\t                    }\n412\t                }\n413\t            }\n414\t        }\n415\t    }\n416\t\n417\t    // Sort by score descending, then by primary key ascending for tie-breaking.\n418\t    all_hits.sort_by(|a, b| {\n419\t        let score_a = a.get(\"_rankingScore\")\n420\t            .and_then(|v| v.as_f64())\n421\t            .unwrap_or(0.0);\n422\t        let score_b = b.get(\"_rankingScore\")\n423\t            .and_then(|v| v.as_f64())\n424\t            .unwrap_or(0.0);\n425\t\n426\t        // Extract primary keys for tie-breaking.\n427\t        let pk_a = a.get(\"id\")\n428\t            .or_else(|| a.get(\"pk\"))\n429\t            .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n430\t            .unwrap_or(\"\");\n431\t        let pk_b = b.get(\"id\")\n432\t            .or_else(|| b.get(\"pk\"))\n433\t            .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n434\t            .unwrap_or(\"\");\n435\t\n436\t        // Primary sort: score descending (higher score = better rank)\n437\t        match score_a.partial_cmp(&score_b) {\n438\t            Some(Ordering::Equal) => {\n439\t                // Secondary sort: primary key ascending for deterministic tie-breaking\n440\t                pk_a.cmp(pk_b)\n441\t            }\n442\t            Some(ord) => ord.reverse(),\n443\t            None => {\n444\t                // NaN case: treat as lowest score\n445\t                if score_a.is_nan() && !score_b.is_nan() {\n446\t                    Ordering::Less\n447\t                } else if !score_a.is_nan() && score_b.is_nan() {\n448\t                    Ordering::Greater\n449\t                } else {\n450\t                    Ordering::Equal\n451\t                }\n452\t            }\n453\t        }\n454\t    });\n455\t\n456\t    // Apply offset + limit.\n457\t    let paginated_hits: Vec<_> = all_hits\n458\t        .into_iter()\n459\t        .skip(input.offset)\n460\t        .take(input.limit)\n461\t        .collect();\n462\t\n463\t    // Strip reserved fields and rebuild hits.\n464\t    let mut hits = Vec::with_capacity(paginated_hits.len());\n465\t    for mut hit in paginated_hits {\n466\t        // Strip _rankingScore if not requested.\n467\t        if !input.client_requested_score {\n468\t            hit.remove(\"_rankingScore\");\n469\t        }\n470\t\n471\t        // Always strip _miroir_* fields.\n472\t        hit.retain(|k, _| !k.starts_with(\"_miroir_\"));\n473\t\n474\t        hits.push(Value::Object(hit));\n475\t    }\n476\t\n477\t    // Merge facets.\n478\t    let facet_distribution = merge_facets(&input.shard_hits, input.facets.as_deref());\n479\t\n480\t    Ok(MergedSearchResult {\n481\t        hits,\n482\t        facet_distribution,\n483\t        estimated_total_hits,\n484\t        processing_time_ms: max_processing_time,\n485\t        degraded,\n486\t        failed_shards: input.failed_shards,\n487\t    })\n488\t}\n489\t\n490\t/// Merge facet distributions from multiple shards.\n491\t///\n492\t/// Uses BTreeMap for stable ordering (deterministic serialization).\n493\tfn merge_facets(\n494\t    shard_pages: &[ShardHitPage],\n495\t    requested_facets: Option<&[String]>,\n496\t) -> Option<BTreeMap<String, BTreeMap<String, u64>>> {\n497\t    let mut merged: BTreeMap<String, BTreeMap<String, u64>> = BTreeMap::new();\n498\t\n499\t    for shard_page in shard_pages {\n500\t        let body = &shard_page.body;\n501\t\n502\t        // Meilisearch uses \"facetDistribution\" for facet results.\n503\t        if let Some(Value::Object(facets)) = body.get(\"facetDistribution\") {\n504\t            for (facet_name, facet_values) in facets {\n505\t                // Skip if not requested (if a filter was provided).\n506\t                if let Some(requested) = requested_facets {\n507\t                    if !requested.iter().any(|f| f == facet_name) {\n508\t                        continue;\n509\t                    }\n510\t                }\n511\t\n512\t                if let Value::Object(values_map) = facet_values {\n513\t                    let merged_facet = merged.entry(facet_name.clone()).or_default();\n514\t                    for (value, count) in values_map {\n515\t                        if let Value::Number(n) = count {\n516\t                            if let Some(n) = n.as_u64() {\n517\t                                *merged_facet.entry(value.clone()).or_insert(0) += n;\n518\t                            }\n519\t                        }\n520\t                    }\n521\t                }\n522\t            }\n523\t        }\n524\t    }\n525\t\n526\t    if merged.is_empty() {\n527\t        None\n528\t    } else {\n529\t        Some(merged)\n530\t    }\n531\t}\n532\t\n533\t#[cfg(test)]\n534\tmod tests {\n535\t    use super::*;\n536\t    use serde_json::json;\n537\t\n538\t    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n539\t    // Trait / strategy tests\n540\t    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n541\t\n542\t    #[test]\n543\t    fn test_rrf_strategy_default_matches_free_function() {\n544\t        let input = MergeInput {\n545\t            shard_hits: vec![make_shard_response(\n546\t                vec![make_hit(\"doc1\", 0.9, 0), make_hit(\"doc2\", 0.7, 0)],\n547\t                100,\n548\t                15,\n549\t            )],\n550\t            offset: 0,\n551\t            limit: 10,\n552\t            client_requested_score: false,\n553\t            facets: None,\n554\t        failed_shards: Vec::new(),\n555\t        };\n556\t\n557\t        let strategy = RrfStrategy::default_strategy();\n558\t        let via_trait = strategy.merge(input.clone()).unwrap();\n559\t        let via_free = merge(input).unwrap();\n560\t\n561\t        assert_eq!(via_trait.hits, via_free.hits);\n562\t        assert_eq!(via_trait.estimated_total_hits, via_free.estimated_total_hits);\n563\t    }\n564\t\n565\t    #[test]\n566\t    fn test_rrf_strategy_custom_k() {\n567\t        // With k=1, rank 0 gets 1/(1+0+1) = 0.5\n568\t        // With k=60, rank 0 gets 1/(60+0+1) ≈ 0.0164\n569\t        // Both should produce the same ordering for a single shard.\n570\t        let input = MergeInput {\n571\t            shard_hits: vec![make_shard_response(\n572\t                vec![make_hit(\"a\", 0.9, 0), make_hit(\"b\", 0.5, 0)],\n573\t                50,\n574\t                10,\n575\t            )],\n576\t            offset: 0,\n577\t            limit: 10,\n578\t            client_requested_score: false,\n579\t            facets: None,\n580\t        failed_shards: Vec::new(),\n581\t        };\n582\t\n583\t        let strategy_k1 = RrfStrategy::new(1);\n584\t        let result = strategy_k1.merge(input).unwrap();\n585\t        assert_eq!(result.hits[0].get(\"id\").unwrap(), \"a\");\n586\t        assert_eq!(result.hits[1].get(\"id\").unwrap(), \"b\");\n587\t    }\n588\t\n589\t    #[test]\n590\t    fn test_merge_with_strategy_dispatches() {\n591\t        let input = MergeInput {\n592\t            shard_hits: vec![make_shard_response(\n593\t                vec![make_hit(\"doc1\", 0.9, 0)],\n594\t                50,\n595\t                10,\n596\t            )],\n597\t            offset: 0,\n598\t            limit: 10,\n599\t            client_requested_score: false,\n600\t            facets: None,\n601\t        failed_shards: Vec::new(),\n602\t        };\n603\t\n604\t        let strategy = RrfStrategy::default_strategy();\n605\t        let result = merge_with_strategy(&strategy, input).unwrap();\n606\t        assert_eq!(result.hits.len(), 1);\n607\t        assert_eq!(strategy.name(), \"rrf\");\n608\t    }\n609\t\n610\t    #[test]\n611\t    fn test_rrf_strategy_k_clamped_to_one() {\n612\t        let strategy = RrfStrategy::new(0);\n613\t        assert_eq!(strategy.k(), 1);\n614\t    }\n615\t\n616\t    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n617\t    // Core merge tests\n618\t    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n619\t\n620\t    fn make_hit(id: &str, score: f64, shard: u32) -> Value {\n621\t        json!({\n622\t            \"id\": id,\n623\t            \"title\": format!(\"Document {}\", id),\n624\t            \"_rankingScore\": score,\n625\t            \"_miroir_shard\": shard,\n626\t        })\n627\t    }\n628\t\n629\t    #[allow(dead_code)]\n630\t    fn make_hit_ranked(id: &str, shard: u32) -> Value {\n631\t        json!({\n632\t            \"id\": id,\n633\t            \"title\": format!(\"Document {}\", id),\n634\t            \"_rankingScore\": 0.5, // RRF ignores score, only rank matters\n635\t            \"_miroir_shard\": shard,\n636\t        })\n637\t    }\n638\t\n639\t    fn make_shard_response(\n640\t        hits: Vec<Value>,\n641\t        total_hits: u64,\n642\t        processing_time: u64,\n643\t    ) -> ShardHitPage {\n644\t        ShardHitPage {\n645\t            body: json!({\n646\t                \"hits\": hits,\n647\t                \"estimatedTotalHits\": total_hits,\n648\t                \"processingTimeMs\": processing_time,\n649\t                \"facetDistribution\": {},\n650\t            }),\n651\t        }\n652\t    }\n653\t\n654\t    #[test]\n655\t    fn test_merge_basic() {\n656\t        let input = MergeInput {\n657\t            shard_hits: vec![make_shard_response(\n658\t                vec![\n659\t                    make_hit(\"doc1\", 0.9, 0),\n660\t                    make_hit(\"doc2\", 0.7, 0),\n661\t                ],\n662\t                100,\n663\t                15,\n664\t            )],\n665\t            offset: 0,\n666\t            limit: 10,\n667\t            client_requested_score: false,\n668\t            facets: None,\n669\t        failed_shards: Vec::new(),\n670\t        };\n671\t\n672\t        let result = merge(input).unwrap();\n673\t        assert_eq!(result.hits.len(), 2);\n674\t        assert_eq!(result.estimated_total_hits, 100);\n675\t        assert_eq!(result.processing_time_ms, 15);\n676\t        assert!(!result.degraded);\n677\t\n678\t        // Score should be stripped.\n679\t        let hit = &result.hits[0];\n680\t        assert!(hit.get(\"_rankingScore\").is_none());\n681\t        assert!(hit.get(\"_miroir_shard\").is_none());\n682\t        assert_eq!(hit.get(\"id\").unwrap(), \"doc1\");\n683\t    }\n684\t\n685\t    #[test]\n686\t    fn test_merge_global_sort() {\n687\t        let input = MergeInput {\n688\t            shard_hits: vec![\n689\t                make_shard_response(vec![make_hit(\"doc1\", 0.5, 0)], 50, 10),\n690\t                make_shard_response(vec![make_hit(\"doc2\", 0.9, 1)], 50, 10),\n691\t                make_shard_response(vec![make_hit(\"doc3\", 0.7, 2)], 50, 10),\n692\t            ],\n693\t            offset: 0,\n694\t            limit: 10,\n695\t            client_requested_score: true,\n696\t            facets: None,\n697\t        failed_shards: Vec::new(),\n698\t        };\n699\t\n700\t        let result = merge(input).unwrap();\n701\t        assert_eq!(result.hits.len(), 3);\n702\t\n703\t        // RRF: all docs are at rank 0 in their respective shards.\n704\t        // With equal ranks, tie-break by primary key (alphabetically).\n705\t        assert_eq!(result.hits[0].get(\"id\").unwrap(), \"doc1\");\n706\t        assert_eq!(result.hits[1].get(\"id\").unwrap(), \"doc2\");\n707\t        assert_eq!(result.hits[2].get(\"id\").unwrap(), \"doc3\");\n708\t    }\n709\t\n710\t    #[test]\n711\t    fn test_merge_rrf_rank_ordering() {\n712\t        // Test RRF: higher rank (lower position) contributes more to RRF score.\n713\t        // shard0: [low_rank_doc (rank 0), mid_rank_doc (rank 10)]\n714\t        // shard1: [high_rank_doc (rank 0)]\n715\t        // shard2: [mid_rank_doc (rank 0) - same doc appears in two shards!]\n716\t        //\n717\t        // Expected RRF scores:\n718\t        // - low_rank_doc: 1/(60+0+1) = 1/61 (only in shard0)\n719\t        // - high_rank_doc: 1/(60+0+1) = 1/61 (only in shard1)\n720\t        // - mid_rank_doc: 1/(60+10+1) + 1/(60+0+1) = 1/71 + 1/61 (rank 10 in shard0, rank 0 in shard2)\n721\t        //\n722\t        // mid_rank_doc should win because it appears in multiple shards.\n723\t\n724\t        let mut shard0_hits = vec![];\n725\t        let mut shard1_hits = vec![];\n726\t        let mut shard2_hits = vec![];\n727\t\n728\t        // Build shard0: low_rank_doc at position 0, mid_rank_doc at position 10\n729\t        shard0_hits.push(make_hit(\"low_rank_doc\", 0.1, 0));\n730\t        for i in 0..9 {\n731\t            shard0_hits.push(make_hit(&format!(\"filler_0_{}\", i), 0.5, 0));\n732\t        }\n733\t        shard0_hits.push(make_hit(\"mid_rank_doc\", 0.2, 0));\n734\t\n735\t        // shard1: high_rank_doc at position 0\n736\t        shard1_hits.push(make_hit(\"high_rank_doc\", 0.3, 1));\n737\t\n738\t        // shard2: mid_rank_doc at position 0 (same doc appears again!)\n739\t        shard2_hits.push(make_hit(\"mid_rank_doc\", 0.4, 2));\n740\t\n741\t        let input = MergeInput {\n742\t            shard_hits: vec![\n743\t                make_shard_response(shard0_hits, 100, 10),\n744\t                make_shard_response(shard1_hits, 100, 10),\n745\t                make_shard_response(shard2_hits, 100, 10),\n746\t            ],\n747\t            offset: 0,\n748\t            limit: 10,\n749\t            client_requested_score: false,\n750\t            facets: None,\n751\t        failed_shards: Vec::new(),\n752\t        };\n753\t\n754\t        let result = merge(input).unwrap();\n755\t\n756\t        // mid_rank_doc should be first because it appears in multiple shards\n757\t        // (RRF contributions sum: rank 10 in shard0 + rank 0 in shard2)\n758\t        assert_eq!(result.hits[0].get(\"id\").unwrap(), \"mid_rank_doc\");\n759\t\n760\t        // low_rank_doc and high_rank_doc both at rank 0 in their shards\n761\t        // Tie-break by primary key alphabetically\n762\t        assert_eq!(result.hits[1].get(\"id\").unwrap(), \"high_rank_doc\");\n763\t        assert_eq!(result.hits[2].get(\"id\").unwrap(), \"low_rank_doc\");\n764\t    }\n765\t\n766\t    #[test]\n767\t    fn test_merge_rrf_duplicate_handling() {\n768\t        // Test that the same document appearing in multiple shards\n769\t        // gets its RRF score summed.\n770\t        //\n771\t        // doc1 appears at rank 0 in shard0 and rank 5 in shard1\n772\t        // doc2 appears at rank 0 in shard2\n773\t        //\n774\t        // RRF(doc1) = 1/(60+0+1) + 1/(60+5+1) = 1/61 + 1/66\n775\t        // RRF(doc2) = 1/(60+0+1) = 1/61\n776\t        //\n777\t        // doc1 should rank higher.\n778\t\n779\t        let shard0_hits = vec![make_hit(\"doc1\", 0.1, 0)];\n780\t        let mut shard1_hits = vec![];\n781\t        let shard2_hits = vec![make_hit(\"doc2\", 0.9, 2)];\n782\t\n783\t        // Add filler hits to shard1 to make doc1 appear at rank 5\n784\t        for i in 0..5 {\n785\t            shard1_hits.push(make_hit(&format!(\"filler_1_{}\", i), 0.5, 1));\n786\t        }\n787\t        shard1_hits.push(make_hit(\"doc1\", 0.2, 1));\n788\t\n789\t        let input = MergeInput {\n790\t            shard_hits: vec![\n791\t                make_shard_response(shard0_hits, 50, 10),\n792\t                make_shard_response(shard1_hits, 50, 10),\n793\t                make_shard_response(shard2_hits, 50, 10),\n794\t            ],\n795\t            offset: 0,\n796\t            limit: 10,\n797\t            client_requested_score: false,\n798\t            facets: None,\n799\t        failed_shards: Vec::new(),\n800\t        };\n801\t\n802\t        let result = merge(input).unwrap();\n803\t\n804\t        // doc1 should rank higher despite lower score because\n805\t        // it appears in multiple shards and gets RRF contribution boost\n806\t        assert_eq!(result.hits[0].get(\"id\").unwrap(), \"doc1\");\n807\t        assert_eq!(result.hits[1].get(\"id\").unwrap(), \"doc2\");\n808\t    }\n809\t\n810\t    #[test]\n811\t    fn // Test that RRF ordering is based on rank, not score.\n864\t        // Even though doc3 has highest score, it's ranked lower in its shard.\n865\t        //\n866\t        // shard0: doc2 at rank 0 with score 0.9 (same rank, tie-break by id)\n867\t        // shard1: doc1 at rank 0 with score 0.1 (same rank, tie-break by id)\n868\t        // shard2: doc3 at rank 2 (position 2) with score 1.0 (lower rank)\n869\t        //\n870\t        // RRF scores:\n871\t        // - doc1: 1/61 (rank 0)\n872\t        // - doc2: 1/61 (rank 0)\n873\t        // - filler: 1/62 (rank 1)\n874\t        // - doc3: 1/63 (rank 2)\n875\t        //\n876\t        // Ordering: doc1, doc2 (tie-break alphabetically), filler, then doc3\n877\t\n878\t        let shard0_hits = vec![make_hit(\"doc2\", 0.9, 0)]; // High score, rank 0\n879\t        let shard1_hits = vec![make_hit(\"doc1\", 0.1, 0)]; // Low score, rank 0\n880\t        let shard2_hits = vec![\n881\t            make_hit(\"filler\", 0.5, 2),\n882\t            make_hit(\"filler2\", 0.5, 2),\n883\t            make_hit(\"doc3\", 1.0, 2),  // Highest score, but rank 2\n884\t        ];\n885\t\n886\t        let input = MergeInput {\n887\t            shard_hits: vec![\n888\t                make_shard_response(shard0_hits, 50, 10),\n889\t                make_shard_response(shard1_hits, 50, 10),\n890\t                make_shard_response(shard2_hits, 50, 10),\n891\t            ],\n892\t            offset: 0,\n893\t            limit: 10,\n894\t            client_requested_score: true,\n895\t            facets: None,\n896\t        failed_shards: Vec::new(),\n897\t        };\n898\t\n899\t        let result = merge(input).unwrap();\n900\t\n901\t        // doc1 and doc2 both at rank 0, tie-break alphabetically\n902\t        assert_eq!(result.hits[0].get(\"id\").unwrap(), \"doc1\");\n903\t        assert_eq!(result.hits[1].get(\"id\").unwrap(), \"doc2\");\n904\t        // filler and filler2 at rank 1\n905\t        assert_eq!(result.hits[2].get(\"id\").unwrap(), \"filler\");\n906\t        assert_eq!(result.hits[3].get(\"id\").unwrap(), \"filler2\");\n907\t        // doc3 at rank 2, comes last despite highest score\n908\t        assert_eq!(result.hits[4].get(\"id\").unwrap(), \"doc3\");\n909\t    }\n910\t\n911\t    #[test]\n912\t    fn test_merge_strips_miroir_fields() {\n913\t        let input = limit=50 result.\n1170\t        let mut all_hits = Vec::new();\n1171\t        for i in 0..50 {\n1172\t            all_hits.push(make_hit(&format!(\"doc{:02}\", i), (50 - i) as f64 / 100.0, 0));\n1173\t        }\n1174\t\n1175\t        let input = MergeInput {\n1176\t            shard_hits: vec![make_shard_response(all_hits, 50, 10)],\n1177\t            offset: 0,\n1178\t            limit: 50,\n1179\t            client_requested_score: false,\n1180\t            facets: None,\n1181\t        failed_shards: Vec::new(),\n1182\t        };\n1183\t\n1184\t        let full_result = merge(input.clone()).unwrap();\n1185\t        assert_eq!(full_result.hits.len(), 50);\n1186\t\n1187\t        // Now fetch in pages of 10 and verify they match.\n1188\t        for page in 0..5 {\n1189\t            let page_input = MergeInput {\n1190\t                shard_hits: input.shard_hits.clone(),\n1191\t                offset: page * 10,\n1192\t                limit: 10,\n1193\t
