             \"brand\": {\"apple\": 20},\n                },\n            }),\n        };\n\n        let input = MergeInput {\n            shard_hits: vec![shard],\n            offset: 0,\n            limit: 10,\n            client_requested_score: false,\n            facets: Some(vec![\"category\".to_string()]),\n            failed_shards: Vec::new(),\n        };\n\n        let result = merge(input).unwrap();\n        let facets = result.facet_distribution.unwrap();\n\n        assert!(facets.contains_key(\"category\"));\n        assert!(!facets.contains_key(\"brand\"));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_merge_estimated_total_hits_sum() {\n        let input = MergeInput {\n            shard_hits: vec![\n                make_shard_response(vec![], 100, 10),\n                make_shard_response(vec![], 150, 15),\n                make_shard_response(vec![], 200, 20),\n            ],\n            offset: 0,\n            limit: 10,\n            client_requested_score: false,\n            facets: None,\n        failed_shards: Vec::new(),\n        };\n\n        let result = merge(input).unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(result.estimated_total_hits, 450);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_merge_processing_time_max() {\n        let input = MergeInput {\n            shard_hits: vec![\n                make_shard_response(vec![], 100, 10),\n                make_shard_response(vec![], 100, 25),\n                make_shard_response(vec![], 100, 15),\n            ],\n            offset: 0,\n            limit: 10,\n            client_requested_score: false,\n            facets: None,\n        failed_shards: Vec::new(),\n        };\n\n        let result = merge(input).unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(result.processing_time_ms, 25);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_merge_tie_breaking() {\n        let input = MergeInput {\n            shard_hits: vec![\n                make_shard_response(vec![make_hit(\"zebra\", 0.5, 0)], 50, 10),\n                make_shard_response(vec![make_hit(\"apple\", 0.5, 1)], 50, 10),\n            ],\n            offset: 0,\n            limit: 10,\n            client_requested_score: false,\n            facets: None,\n        failed_shards: Vec::new(),\n        };\n\n        let result = merge(input).unwrap();\n        // RRF: both docs at rank 0 in their shards, equal RRF scores.\n        // Tie-break by primary key (apple < zebra lexicographically).\n        assert_eq!(result.hits[0].get(\"id\").unwrap(), \"apple\");\n        assert_eq!(result.hits[1].get(\"id\").unwrap(), \"zebra\");\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_merge_degraded_flag() {\n        let failed_shard = ShardHitPage {\n            body: json!({\n                \"success\": false,\n                \"message\": \"node unavailable\",\n            }),\n        };\n\n        let input = MergeInput {\n            shard_hits: vec![\n                make_shard_response(vec![make_hit(\"doc1\", 0.9, 0)], 50, 10),\n                failed_shard,\n            ],\n            offset: 0,\n            limit: 10,\n            client_requested_score: false,\n            facets: None,\n        failed_shards: Vec::new(),\n        };\n\n        let result = merge(input).unwrap();\n        assert!(result.degraded);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_stable_serialization() {\n        let shard1 = ShardHitPage {\n            body: json!({\n                \"hits\": [make_hit(\"doc1\", 0.9, 0)],\n                \"estimatedTotalHits\": 100,\n                \"processingTimeMs\": 10,\n                \"facetDistribution\": {\n                    \"category\": {\"electronics\": 50, \"books\": 30},\n                },\n            }),\n        };\n\n        let shard2 = ShardHitPage {\n            body: json!({\n                \"hits\": [make_hit(\"doc2\", 0.8, 1)],\n                \"estimatedTotalHits\": 100,\n                \"processingTimeMs\": 15,\n                \"facetDistribution\": {\n                    \"brand\": {\"apple\": 20},\n                },\n            }),\n        };\n\n        let input = MergeInput {\n            shard_hits: vec![shard1.clone(), shard2.clone()],\n            offset: 0,\n            limit: 10,\n            client_requested_score: false,\n            facets: None,\n        failed_shards: Vec::new(),\n        };\n\n        let result1 = merge(input.clone()).unwrap();\n        let result2 = merge(input).unwrap();\n\n        // Serialize both to JSON.\n        let json1 = serde_json::to_value(&result1).unwrap();\n        let json2 = serde_json::to_value(&result2).unwrap();\n\n        // Byte-identical.\n        assert_eq!(\n            serde_json::to_vec(&json1).unwrap(),\n            serde_json::to_vec(&json2).unwrap()\n        );\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_page_reconstruction() {\n        // Test that pages of 10 reconstruct a single limit=50 result.\n        let mut all_hits = Vec::new();\n        for i in 0..50 {\n            all_hits.push(make_hit(&format!(\"doc{:02}\", i), (50 - i) as f64 / 100.0, 0));\n        }\n\n        let input = MergeInput {\n            shard_hits: vec![make_shard_response(all_hits, 50, 10)],\n            offset: 0,\n            limit: 50,\n            client_requested_score: false,\n            facets: None,\n        failed_shards: Vec::new(),\n        };\n\n        let full_result = merge(input.clone()).unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(full_result.hits.len(), 50);\n\n        // Now fetch in pages of 10 and verify they match.\n        for page in 0..5 {\n            let page_input = MergeInput {\n                shard_hits: input.shard_hits.clone(),\n                offset: page * 10,\n                limit: 10,\n                client_requested_score: false,\n                facets: None,\n        failed_shards: Vec::new(),\n            };\n            let page_result = merge(page_input).unwrap();\n\n            let start = page * 10;\n            let end = start + 10;\n            assert_eq!(\n                page_result.hits,\n                full_result.hits[start..end],\n                \"Page {} mismatch\",\n                page\n            );\n        }\n    }\n\n    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n    // RRF correctness properties\n    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_rrf_cross_shard_replication_boost() {\n        // Key RRF property: a document appearing in multiple shards\n        // gets a higher merged score than a single-shard document,\n        // even if it ranks lower within each shard.\n        //\n        // doc-replicated: rank 5 in shard0, rank 5 in shard1\n        //   RRF = 1/(60+5+1) + 1/(60+5+1) = 2/66 ≈ 0.0303\n        // doc-single: rank 0 in shard2\n        //   RRF = 1/(60+0+1) = 1/61 ≈ 0.0164\n        //\n        // doc-replicated should rank higher.\n\n        let mut shard0 = vec![];\n        let mut shard1 = vec![];\n        for i in 0..5 {\n            shard0.push(make_hit(&format!(\"filler0-{}\", i), 0.5, 0));\n            shard1.push(make_hit(&format!(\"filler1-{}\", i), 0.5, 1));\n        }\n        shard0.push(make_hit(\"doc-replicated\", 0.1, 0));\n        shard1.push(make_hit(\"doc-replicated\", 0.1, 1));\n\n        let shard2 = vec![make_hit(\"doc-single\", 0.99, 2)];\n\n        let strategy = RrfStrategy::default_strategy();\n        let result = strategy\n            .merge(MergeInput {\n                shard_hits: vec![\n                    make_shard_response(shard0, 100, 10),\n                    make_shard_response(shard1, 100, 10),\n                    make_shard_response(shard2, 100, 10),\n                ],\n                offset: 0,\n                limit: 20,\n                client_requested_score: false,\n                facets: None,\n        failed_shards: Vec::new(),\n            })\n            .unwrap();\n\n        let ids: Vec<_> = result.hits.iter().filter_map(|h| h.get(\"id\").and_then(|v| v.as_str())).collect();\n        let rep_pos = ids.iter().position(|&id| id == \"doc-replicated\").unwrap();\n        let single_pos = ids.iter().position(|&id| id == \"doc-single\").unwrap();\n        assert!(\n            rep_pos < single_pos,\n            \"Replicated doc at pos {} should rank above single doc at pos {}\",\n            rep_pos,\n            single_pos\n        );\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_rrf_immune_to_score_scale() {\n        // RRF uses rank only: scores of 0.001 vs 0.999 don't affect ordering.\n        // Two shards with wildly different score ranges should produce the\n        // same merge as two shards with uniform scores.\n        let shard_a = ShardHitPage {\n            body: json!({\n                \"hits\": [\n                    {\"id\": \"a1\", \"_rankingScore\": 0.001},\n                    {\"id\": \"a2\", \"_rankingScore\": 0.002},\n                ],\n                \"estimatedTotalHits\": 2,\n                \"processingTimeMs\": 5,\n            }),\n        };\n        let shard_b = ShardHitPage {\n            body: json!({\n                \"hits\": [\n                    {\"id\": \"b1\", \"_rankingScore\": 0.999},\n                    {\"id\": \"b2\", \"_rankingScore\": 0.998},\n                ],\n                \"estimatedTotalHits\": 2,\n                \"processingTimeMs\": 5,\n            }),\n        };\n\n        let strategy = RrfStrategy::default_strategy();\n        let result = strategy\n            .merge(MergeInput {\n                shard_hits: vec![shard_a, shard_b],\n                offset: 0,\n                limit: 10,\n                client_requested_score: false,\n                facets: None,\n        failed_shards: Vec::new(),\n            })\n            .unwrap();\n\n        // All at rank 0 or 1 — tie-break alphabetically within each rank tier.\n        // rank 0: a1, b1 → sort by id → a1, b1\n        // rank 1: a2, b2 → sort by id → a2, b2\n        let ids: Vec<_> = result.hits.iter().filter_map(|h| h.get(\"id\").and_then(|v| v.as_str())).collect();\n        assert_eq!(ids, vec![\"a1\", \"b1\", \"a2\", \"b2\"]);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_rrf_deterministic_with_same_input() {\n        // RRF merge is a pure function: same input always produces same output.\n        let shard = make_shard_response(\n            (0..100).map(|i| make_hit(&format!(\"doc{}\", i), (100 - i) as f64 / 100.0, 0)).collect(),\n            1000,\n            10,\n        );\n        let input = MergeInput {\n            shard_hits: vec![shard; 5],\n            offset: 0,\n            limit: 50,\n            client_requested_score: false,\n            facets: None,\n        failed_shards: Vec::new(),\n        };\n\n        let strategy = RrfStrategy::default_strategy();\n        let r1 = strategy.merge(input.clone()).unwrap();\n        let r2 = strategy.merge(input).unwrap();\n\n        assert_eq!(\n            serde_json::to_vec(&r1).unwrap(),\n            serde_json::to_vec(&r2).unwrap()\n        );\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_rrf_default_impl() {\n        let via_default: RrfStrategy = Default::default();\n        let via_constructor = RrfStrategy::default_strategy();\n        assert_eq!(via_default.k(), via_constructor.k());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_merge_pk_field_as_primary_key() {\n        let shard = ShardHitPage {\n            body: json!({\n                \"hits\": [{\"pk\": \"doc-pk\", \"title\": \"Test\"}],\n                \"estimatedTotalHits\": 1,\n                \"processingTimeMs\": 5,\n            }),\n        };\n        let input = MergeInput {\n            shard_hits: vec![shard],\n            offset: 0,\n            limit: 10,\n            client_requested_score: false,\n            facets: None,\n        failed_shards: Vec::new(),\n        };\n        let result = merge(input).unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(result.hits.len(), 1);\n        assert_eq!(result.hits[0].get(\"pk\").unwrap(), \"doc-pk\");\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_rrf_document_equality() {\n        use super::Ordering;\n        let a = super::RRFDocument {\n            rrf_score: 1.0,\n            primary_key: \"doc1\".into(),\n            hit: Map::new(),\n        };\n        let b = super::RRFDocument {\n            rrf_score: 1.0,\n            primary_key: \"doc1\".into(),\n            hit: Map::new(),\n        };\n        assert_eq!(a, b);\n\n        let c = super::RRFDocument {\n            rrf_score: 1.0,\n            primary_key: \"doc2\".into(),\n            hit: Map::new(),\n        };\n        assert_ne!(a, c);\n\n        // NaN: both NaN → Equal\n        let nan_a = super::RRFDocument {\n            rrf_score: f64::NAN,\n            primary_key: \"x\".into(),\n            hit: Map::new(),\n        };\n        let nan_b = super::RRFDocument {\n            rrf_score: f64::NAN,\n            primary_key: \"x\".into(),\n            hit: Map::new(),\n        };\n        assert_eq!(nan_a.cmp(&nan_b), Ordering::Equal);\n\n        // NaN vs real: NaN is Less\n        let real = super::RRFDocument {\n            rrf_score: 1.0,\n            primary_key: \"x\".into(),\n            hit: Map::new(),\n        };\n        assert_eq!(nan_a.cmp(&real), Ordering::Less);\n        assert_eq!(real.cmp(&nan_a), Ordering::Greater);\n    }\n\n    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n    // Score-based merge tests (OP#4 global-IDF)\n    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_score_merge_strategy_exists() {\n        let strategy = ScoreMergeStrategy::new();\n        assert_eq!(strategy.name(), \"score\");\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_score_merge_basic() {\n        let strategy = ScoreMergeStrategy::new();\n        let input = MergeInput {\n            shard_hits: vec![make_shard_response(\n                vec![\n                    make_hit(\"doc1\", 0.9, 0),\n                    make_hit(\"doc2\", 0.7, 0),\n                ],\n                100,\n                15,\n            )],\n            offset: 0,\n            limit: 10,\n            client_requested_score: false,\n            facets: None,\n        failed_shards: Vec::new(),\n        };\n\n        let result = strategy.merge(input).unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(result.hits.len(), 2);\n        assert_eq!(result.hits[0].get(\"id\").unwrap(), \"doc1\");\n        assert_eq!(result.hits[1].get(\"id\").unwrap(), \"doc2\");\n        assert_eq!(result.estimated_total_hits, 100);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_score_merge_global_sorting() {\n        // Test that score-based merge sorts globally by score.\n        // With global IDF (simulated here by consistent scores across shards),\n        // doc with highest score should rank first regardless of shard.\n        let strategy = ScoreMergeStrategy::new();\n        let input = MergeInput {\n            shard_hits: vec![\n                // Shard 0: low scores\n                make_shard_response(\n                    vec![\n                        make_hit(\"doc-low-1\", 0.3, 0),\n                        make_hit(\"doc-low-2\", 0.2, 0),\n                    ],\n                    50,\n                    10,\n                ),\n                // Shard 1: high scores (these should rank higher)\n                make_shard_response(\n                    vec![\n                        make_hit(\"doc-high-1\", 0.9, 1),\n                        make_hit(\"doc-high-2\", 0.8, 1),\n                    ],\n                    50,\n                    10,\n                ),\n            ],\n            offset: 0,\n            limit: 10,\n            client_requested_score: true,\n            facets: None,\n        failed_shards: Vec::new(),\n        };\n\n        let result = strategy.merge(input).unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(result.hits.len(), 4);\n\n        // Should be sorted by score descending globally\n        assert_eq!(result.hits[0].get(\"id\").unwrap(), \"doc-high-1\");\n        assert_eq!(result.hits[1].get(\"id\").unwrap(), \"doc-high-2\");\n        assert_eq!(result.hits[2].get(\"id\").unwrap(), \"doc-low-1\");\n        assert_eq!(result.hits[3].get(\"id\").unwrap(), \"doc-low-2\");\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_score_merge_tie_breaking() {\n        // Test deterministic tie-breaking on primary key when scores are equal.\n        let strategy = ScoreMergeStrategy::new();\n        let input = MergeInput {\n            shard_hits: vec![\n                make_shard_response(\n                    vec![\n                        make_hit(\"zebra\", 0.5, 0),\n                        make_hit(\"apple\", 0.5, 0),\n                    ],\n                    50,\n                    10,\n                ),\n            ],\n            offset: 0,\n            limit: 10,\n            client_requested_score: false,\n            facets: None,\n        failed_shards: Vec::new(),\n        };\n\n        let result = strategy.merge(input).unwrap();\n        // Both docs have same score, should tie-break alphabetically\n        assert_eq!(result.hits[0].get(\"id\").unwrap(), \"apple\");\n        assert_eq!(result.hits[1].get(\"id\").unwrap(), \"zebra\");\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_score_merge_offset_limit() {\n        let strategy = ScoreMergeStrategy::new();\n        let input = MergeInput {\n            shard_hits: vec![make_shard_response(\n                vec![\n                    make_hit(\"doc1\", 0.9, 0),\n                    make_hit(\"doc2\", 0.8, 0),\n                    make_hit(\"doc3\", 0.7, 0),\n                    make_hit(\"doc4\", 0.6, 0),\n                    make_hit(\"doc5\", 0.5, 0),\n                ],\n                100,\n                10,\n            )],\n            offset: 1,\n            limit: 2,\n            client_requested_score: false,\n            facets: None,\n        failed_shards: Vec::new(),\n        };\n\n        let result = strategy.merge(input).unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(result.hits.len(), 2);\n        assert_eq!(result.hits[0].get(\"id\").unwrap(), \"doc2\");\n        assert_eq!(result.hits[1].get(\"id\").unwrap(), \"doc3\");\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_score_merge_preserves_score_when_requested() {\n        let strategy = ScoreMergeStrategy::new();\n        let input = MergeInput {\n            shard_hits: vec![make_shard_response(\n                vec![make_hit(\"doc1\", 0.9, 0)],\n                50,\n                10,\n            )],\n            offset: 0,\n            limit: 10,\n            client_requested_score: true,\n            facets: None,\n        failed_shards: Vec::new(),\n        };\n\n        let result = strategy.merge(input).unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(\n            result.hits[0].get(\"_rankingScore\").unwrap().as_f64(),\n            Some(0.9)\n        );\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_score_merge_strips_score_when_not_requested() {\n        let strategy = ScoreMergeStrategy::new();\n        let input = MergeInput {\n            shard_hits: vec![make_shard_response(\n                vec![make_hit(\"doc1\", 0.9, 0)],\n                50,\n                10,\n            )],\n            offset: 0,\n            limit: 10,\n            client_requested_score: false,\n            facets: None,\n        failed_shards: Vec::new(),\n        };\n\n        let result = strategy.merge(input).unwrap();\n        assert!(result.hits[0].get(\"_rankingScore\").is_none());\n    }\n\n    /// Integration test: skewed corpus scenario with global-IDF preflight.\n    ///\n    /// This simulates the scenario described in P12.OP4:\n    /// - Shard 0 has 1000 docs, term \"rust\" appears in 100 (df=100)\n    /// - Shard 1 has 100 docs, term \"rust\" appears in 50 (df=50)\n    ///\n    /// Without global IDF:\n    /// - Local IDF(shard 0) = log((1000-100+0.5)/(100+0.5)+1) ≈ 2.2\n    /// - Local IDF(shard 1) = log((100-50+0.5)/(50+0.5)+1) ≈ 0.7\n    ///\n    /// With global IDF:\n    /// - Global N = 1100, global df = 150\n    /// - Global IDF = log((1100-150+0.5)/(150+0.5)+1) ≈ 1.8\n    ///\n    /// A document with tf=3 for \"rust\" in each shard:\n    /// - Shard 0 score (local IDF): ~3 * 2.2 = 6.6\n    /// - Shard 1 score (local IDF): ~3 * 0.7 = 2.1\n    ///\n    /// Without global IDF, shard 0 doc ranks higher despite shard 1 having\n    /// much higher term density (50/100 vs 100/1000).\n    ///\n    /// With global IDF, both shards use IDF=1.8, and the doc with higher\n    /// term density (normalized by document length) ranks correctly.\n    #[test]\n    fn test_score_merge_skewed_corpus_integration() {\n        // Simulate global IDF applied (scores are now comparable)\n        let strategy = ScoreMergeStrategy::new();\n\n        // Doc in large shard with high term frequency but low density\n        let doc_large_shard = json!({\n            \"id\": \"doc-large\",\n            \"title\": \"Rust in Large Shard\",\n            \"_rankingScore\": 0.75, // After global-IDF normalization\n        });\n\n        // Doc in small shard with lower term frequency but high density\n        let doc_small_shard = json!({\n            \"id\": \"doc-small\",\n            \"title\": \"Rust in Small Shard\",\n            \"_rankingScore\": 0.85, // After global-IDF normalization\n        });\n\n        // With global IDF, the small shard doc should rank higher\n        // because its term density is higher (50/100 vs 100/1000)\n        let input = MergeInput {\n            shard_hits: vec![\n                ShardHitPage {\n                    body: json!({\n                        \"hits\": [doc_large_shard],\n                        \"estimatedTotalHits\": 1000,\n                        \"processingTimeMs\": 10,\n                    }),\n                },\n                ShardHitPage {\n                    body: json!({\n                        \"hits\": [doc_small_shard],\n                        \"estimatedTotalHits\": 100,\n                        \"processingTimeMs\": 5,\n                    }),\n                },\n            ],\n            offset: 0,\n            limit: 10,\n            client_requested_score: true,\n            facets: None,\n        failed_shards: Vec::new(),\n        };\n\n        let result = strategy.merge(input).unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(result.hits.len(), 2);\n\n        // Small shard doc should rank first (higher score after global IDF)\n        assert_eq!(result.hits[0].get(\"id\").unwrap(), \"doc-small\");\n        assert_eq!(result.hits[1].get(\"id\").unwrap(), \"doc-large\");\n\n        // Scores should be preserved\n        assert_eq!(\n            result.hits[0].get(\"_rankingScore\").unwrap().as_f64(),\n            Some(0.85)\n        );\n        assert_eq!(\n            result.hits[1].get(\"_rankingScore\").unwrap().as_f64(),\n            Some(0.75)\n        );\n    }\n\n    /// Test that demonstrates the failure mode without global IDF.\n    ///\n    /// This shows what happens when scores are NOT comparable across shards:\n    /// score-based merge produces incorrect rankings, while RRF at least\n    /// produces consistent (though not optimal) results.\n    #[test]\n    fn test_score_merge_without_global_idf_fails() {\n        // Simulate the bug: shard-local IDF produces incomparable scores\n        let strategy = ScoreMergeStrategy::new();\n\n        // Shard 0: large shard, inflated local IDF (shard has term rarity)\n        let doc_shard0 = json!({\n            \"id\": \"doc-inflated\",\n            \"title\": \"Document in Large Shard\",\n            \"_rankingScore\": 0.95, // Inflated due to high local IDF\n        });\n\n        // Shard 1: small shard, deflated local IDF (term is common here)\n        let doc_shard1 = json!({\n            \"id\": \"doc-deflated\",\n            \"title\": \"Document in Small Shard\",\n            \"_rankingScore\": 0.60, // Deflated due to low local IDF\n        });\n\n        let input = MergeInput {\n            shard_hits: vec![\n                ShardHitPage {\n                    body: json!({\n                        \"hits\": [doc_shard0],\n                        \"estimatedTotalHits\": 10000,\n                        \"processingTimeMs\": 15,\n                    }),\n                },\n                ShardHitPage {\n                    body: json!({\n                        \"hits\": [doc_shard1],\n                        \"estimatedTotalHits\": 100,\n                        \"processingTimeMs\": 5,\n                    }),\n                },\n            ],\n            offset: 0,\n            limit: 10,\n            client_requested_score: true,\n            facets: None,\n        failed_shards: Vec::new(),\n        };\n\n        let result = strategy.merge(input).unwrap();\n\n        // Without global IDF, score-based merge trusts the inflated scores\n        assert_eq!(result.hits[0].get(\"id\").unwrap(), \"doc-inflated\");\n\n        // This is WRONG: doc-deflated has much higher term density but\n        // ranks lower due to shard-local IDF skew.\n        //\n        // The solution is the preflight phase (dfs_query_then_fetch_search)\n        // which computes global IDF so scores are comparable.\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_score_merge_default_impl() {\n        let via_default: ScoreMergeStrategy = Default::default();\n        let via_constructor = ScoreMergeStrategy::new();\n        assert_eq!(via_default.name(), via_constructor.name());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_score_merge_empty_input() {\n        let strategy = ScoreMergeStrategy::new();\n        let input = MergeInput {\n            shard_hits: vec![],\n            offset: 0,\n            limit: 10,\n            client_requested_score: false,\n            facets: None,\n        failed_shards: Vec::new(),\n        };\n\n        let result = strategy.merge(input).unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(result.hits.len(), 0);\n        assert_eq!(result.estimated_total_hits, 0);\n    }\n\n    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n    // P12.OP4 RRF skew validation\n    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    /// Validates the P12.OP4 finding: RRF merge with extreme shard skew\n    /// produces incorrect global rankings because it gives equal weight\n    /// to all shards regardless of their size.\n    ///\n    /// Scenario: 10 shards where shard 0 has 93K docs (93%) and shard 9\n    /// has 10 docs (0.01%). RRF assigns identical scores to rank-0 hits\n    /// from all shards, so a mediocre hit from the tiny shard ranks\n    /// equally with the best hit from the dominant shard.\n    ///\n    /// Benchmark result (10K queries, skewed corpus):\n    ///   Score merge:   τ = 0.79  (95% CI [0.787, 0.801]) — FAIL\n    ///   RRF merge:     τ = 0.14  (95% CI [0.134, 0.140]) — FAIL\n    ///   DFS preflight: τ = 0.98  (95% CI [0.982, 0.982]) — PASS\n    ///\n    /// Conclusion: RRF alone does NOT solve cross-shard comparability.\n    /// Global-IDF preflight (dfs_query_then_fetch) is required.\n    #[test]\n    fn test_rrf_skewed_shards_equal_weight_problem() {\n        // Shard 0 (dominant): doc-best should be the global #1 result.\n        // It has the highest score and appears in the shard with 93% of docs.\n        let shard_dominant = make_shard_response(\n            vec![\n                make_hit(\"doc-best\", 0.95, 0),      // True global #1\n                make_hit(\"doc-good\", 0.90, 0),       // True global #2\n                make_hit(\"doc-ok\", 0.85, 0),         // True global #3\n                make_hit(\"doc-mediocre\", 0.70, 0),   // True global #4\n                make_hit(\"doc-weak\", 0.60, 0),       // True global #5\n            ],\n            93_000,\n            10,\n        );\n\n        // Shard 9 (tiny, 10 docs): due to local IDF skew, irrelevant docs\n        // can appear at rank 0 with inflated local scores.\n        let shard_tiny = make_shard_response(\n            vec![\n                make_hit(\"doc-irrelevant\", 0.98, 9), // Inflated local IDF → high score\n                make_hit(\"doc-noise\", 0.92, 9),\n            ],\n            10,\n            2,\n        );\n\n        let strategy = RrfStrategy::default_strategy();\n        let result = strategy\n            .merge(MergeInput {\n                shard_hits: vec![shard_dominant, shard_tiny],\n                offset: 0,\n                limit: 10,\n                client_requested_score: true,\n                facets: None,\n        failed_shards: Vec::new(),\n            })\n            .unwrap();\n\n        let ids: Vec<_> = result\n            .hits\n            .iter()\n            .filter_map(|h| h.get(\"id\").and_then(|v| v.as_str()))\n            .collect();\n\n        // RRF gives equal rank weight to both shards.\n        // Rank 0 from dominant shard: 1/61 ≈ 0.0164\n        // Rank 0 from tiny shard:     1/61 ≈ 0.0164 (identical!)\n        //\n        // Tie-breaking falls to primary key (alphabetical), NOT relevance.\n        // doc-best and doc-irrelevant both get RRF score 1/61.\n        // Alphabetically: doc-best < doc-irrelevant → doc-best wins the tie.\n        //\n        // But doc-irrelevant still ranks above doc-good, doc-ok, doc-mediocre,\n        // and doc-weak — all of which are more relevant globally.\n        assert_eq!(ids[0], \"doc-best\");      // Tie-break win (alphabetical)\n        assert_eq!(ids[1], \"doc-irrelevant\"); // Tie-break loss, but still rank 2!\n\n        // doc-irrelevant (globally irrelevant) ranks ABOVE doc-good (global #2)\n        let irrelevant_pos = ids.iter().position(|&id| id == \"doc-irrelevant\").unwrap();\n        let good_pos = ids.iter().position(|&id| id == \"doc-good\").unwrap();\n        assert!(\n            irrelevant_pos < good_pos,\n            \"RRF skew bug: irrelevant doc (pos {}) ranks above doc-good (pos {})\",\n            irrelevant_pos,\n            good_pos,\n        );\n    }\n\n    /// Computes Kendall tau between two rankings (document ID lists).\n    /// Used to validate merge quality against ground truth.\n    fn kendall_tau(ranking1: &[String], ranking2: &[String]) -> f64 {\n        let pos1: std::collections::HashMap<&str, usize> = ranking1\n            .iter()\n            .enumerate()\n            .map(|(i, id)| (id.as_str(), i))\n            .collect();\n        let pos2: std::collections::HashMap<&str, usize> = ranking2\n            .iter()\n            .enumerate()\n            .map(|(i, id)| (id.as_str(), i))\n            .collect();\n\n        let common: Vec<&str> = pos1\n            .keys()\n            .filter(|k| pos2.contains_key(*k))\n            .map(|k| *k)\n            .collect();\n\n        if common.len() < 2 {\n            return 0.0;\n        }\n\n        let r2_positions: Vec<usize> = common.iter().map(|id| pos2[id]).collect();\n        let (_, discordant) = count_inversions(&r2_positions);\n        let n = common.len();\n        let total = n * (n - 1) / 2;\n        let concordant = total - discordant;\n        (concordant as f64 - discordant as f64) / total as f64\n    }\n\n    fn count_inversions(arr: &[usize]) -> (Vec<usize>, usize) {\n        if arr.len() <= 1 {\n            return (arr.to_vec(), 0);\n        }\n        let mid = arr.len() / 2;\n        let (left, inv_l) = count_inversions(&arr[..mid]);\n        let (right, inv_r) = count_inversions(&arr[mid..]);\n\n        let mut merged = Vec::with_capacity(arr.len());\n        let mut inv = inv_l + inv_r;\n        let (mut i, mut j) = (0, 0);\n\n        while i < left.len() && j < right.len() {\n            if left[i] <= right[j] {\n                merged.push(left[i]);\n                i += 1;\n            } else {\n                merged.push(right[j]);\n                inv += left.len() - i;\n                j += 1;\n            }\n        }\n        merged.extend_from_slice(&left[i..]);\n        merged.extend_from_slice(&right[j..]);\n        (merged, inv)\n    }\n\n    /// End-to-end validation: RRF merge on skewed shards produces τ < 0.95\n    /// against ground truth (single-index ranking).\n    ///\n    /// This is a scaled-down version of the 10K-query Python benchmark.\n    #[test]\n    fn test_rrf_skewed_shards_tau_below_threshold() {\n        let k = DEFAULT_RRF_K;\n\n        // Build 5 shards with skewed sizes: [100, 500, 2000, 5000, 10000]\n        // Ground truth: all 17600 docs in one index, sorted by score.\n        let mut all_docs: Vec<(String, f64)> = Vec::new();\n        let mut shard_docs: Vec<Vec<(String, f64)>> = vec![vec![], vec![], vec![], vec![], vec![]];\n        let shard_sizes = [100, 500, 2000, 5000, 10000];\n\n        let mut rng = simple_rng(42);\n        for (shard_id, &size) in shard_sizes.iter().enumerate() {\n            for i in 0..size {\n                // Deterministic pseudo-random scores\n                let score = fake_bm25_score(shard_id, i, &mut rng);\n                let doc_id = format!(\"s{}-d{:06}\", shard_id, i);\n                all_docs.push((doc_id.clone(), score));\n                shard_docs[shard_id].push((doc_id, score));\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Ground truth: global sort by score descending\n        all_docs.sort_by(|a, b| b.1.partial_cmp(&a.1).unwrap_or(std::cmp::Ordering::Equal));\n        let ground_truth: Vec<String> = all_docs.iter().take(100).map(|(id, _)| id.clone()).collect();\n\n        // Per-shard: sort locally (simulates local BM25 with local IDF)\n        for docs in &mut shard_docs {\n            docs.sort_by(|a, b| b.1.partial_cmp(&a.1).unwrap_or(std::cmp::Ordering::Equal));\n        }\n\n        // RRF merge using the actual Rust merger\n        let shard_pages: Vec<ShardHitPage> = shard_docs\n            .iter()\n            .map(|docs| {\n                let hits: Vec<Value> = docs\n                    .iter()\n                    .take(200)\n                    .map(|(id, score)| {\n                        json!({\n                            \"id\": id,\n                            \"_rankingScore\": score,\n                        })\n                    })\n                    .collect();\n                ShardHitPage {\n                    body: json!({\n                        \"hits\": hits,\n                        \"estimatedTotalHits\": docs.len(),\n                        \"processingTimeMs\": 10,\n                    }),\n                }\n            })\n            .collect();\n\n        let strategy = RrfStrategy::new(k);\n        let result = strategy\n            .merge(MergeInput {\n                shard_hits: shard_pages,\n                offset: 0,\n                limit: 100,\n                client_requested_score: true,\n                facets: None,\n        failed_shards: Vec::new(),\n            })\n            .unwrap();\n\n        let rrf_ranking: Vec<String> = result\n            .hits\n            .iter()\n            .filter_map(|h| h.get(\"id\").and_then(|v| v.as_str()).map(String::from))\n            .collect();\n\n        let tau = kendall_tau(&ground_truth, &rrf_ranking);\n\n        // RRF with skewed shards should produce τ well below 0.95.\n        assert!(\n            tau < 0.95,\n            \"RRF tau = {:.4}, expected < 0.95 with skewed shards\",\n            tau,\n        );\n    }\n\n    /// Simple deterministic PRNG for reproducible test scores.\n    fn simple_rng(seed: u64) -> impl FnMut() -> f64 {\n        let mut state = seed;\n        move || {\n            state = state.wrapping_mul(6_364_136_223_846_793_005).wrapping_add(1);\n            (state >> 33) as f64 / (1u64 << 31) as f64\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Simulates a BM25-like score with shard-dependent IDF skew.\n    fn fake_bm25_score(shard_id: usize, _doc_idx: usize, rng: &mut impl FnMut() -> f64) -> f64 {\n        let tf = 1.0 + rng() * 10.0;\n        // Larger shards have lower IDF for common terms (simulating skew)\n        let shard_weight = match shard_id {\n            0 => 0.3,\n            1 => 0.5,\n            2 => 0.7,\n            3 => 0.9,\n            4 => 1.0,\n            _ => 0.5,\n        };\n        tf * shard_weight + rng() * 0.5\n    }\n}\n","numLines":2018,"startLine":1,"totalLines":2018}}}

sk.primary_key\n432\t        } else {\n433\t            // No scoped key yet — fall back to node_master_key for initial setup\n434\t            state.config.node_master_key.clone()\n435\t        }\n436\t    } else {\n437\t        // No Redis store — fall back to node_master_key (single-pod dev mode)\n438\t        state.config.node_master_key.clone()\n439\t    };\n440\t\n441\t    // Extract X-Miroir-Min-Settings-Version header (plan §13.5)\n442\t    let min_settings_version = headers\n443\t        .get(\"X-Miroir-Min-Settings-Version\")\n444\t        .and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok())\n445\t        .and_then(|s| s.parse::<u64>().ok());\n446\t\n447\t    // Use live topology from shared state (updated by health checker)\n448\t    let topo = state.topology.read().await;\n449\t    let policy = match state.config.scatter.unavailable_shard_policy.as_str() {\n450\t        \"partial\" => UnavailableShardPolicy::Partial,\n451\t        \"error\" => UnavailableShardPolicy::Error,\n452\t        \"fallback\" => UnavailableShardPolicy::Fallback,\n453\t        _ => return Response::builder()\n454\t            .status(StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR)\n455\t            .body(axum::body::Body::empty())\n456\t            .unwrap(),\n457\t    };\n458\t\n459\t    // Plan scatter using live topology (span for plan construction)\n460\t    let plan = {\n461\t        let _plan_span = tracing::info_span!(\n462\t            \"scatter_plan\",\n463\t            replica_groups = state.config.replica_groups,\n464\t            shards = state.config.shards,\n465\t            rf = state.config.replication_factor,\n466\t            min_settings_version,\n467\t            pinned_group = ?pinned_group,\n468\t            strategy = %state.config.replica_selection.strategy,\n469\t        ).entered();\n470\t\n471\t        // Determine if we should use adaptive selection\n472\t        let use_adaptive = state.config.replica_selection.strategy == \"adaptive\";\n473\t        let replica_selector_ref = if use_adaptive {\n474\t            Some(state.replica_selector.as_ref())\n475\t        } else {\n476\t            None\n477\t        };\n478\t\n479\t        // Session pinning: if pinned_group is set, use group-specific planning\n480\t        if let Some(group) = pinned_group {\n481\t            match plan_search_scatter_for_group(\n482\t                &topo,\n483\t                0,\n484\t                state.config.replication_factor as usize,\n485\t                state.config.shards,\n486\t                group,\n487\t                replica_selector_ref,\n488\t            ).await {\n489\t                Some(p) => p,\n490\t                None => {\n491\t                    // Pinned group not available - fall back to normal planning\n492\t                    // This can happen if the pinned group has failed\n493\t                    warn!(\n494\t                        pinned_group = group,\n495\t                        \"pinned group unavailable, falling back to normal routing\"\n496\t                    );\n497\t                    plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, state.config.replication_factor as usize, state.config.shards, replica_selector_ref).await\n498\t                }\n499\t            }\n500\t        } else if let Some(floor) = min_settings_version {\n501\t            // If client provided a min settings version floor, use version-filtered planning\n502\t            // Clone the settings broadcast for version checking\n503\t            let settings_broadcast = state.settings_broadcast.clone();\n504\t            let plan_result = plan_search_scatter_with_version_floor(\n505\t                &topo,\n506\t                0,\n507\t                state.config.replication_factor as usize,\n508\t                state.config.shards,\n509\t                &index,\n510\t                floor,\n511\t                &move |idx, node_id| {\n512\t                    // Use a blocking task wrapper since we're in a sync context\n513\t                    let sb = settings_broadcast.clone();\n514\t                    let idx = idx.to_string();\n515\t                    let node_id = node_id.to_string();\n516\t                    tokio::task::block_in_place(|| {\n517\t                        tokio::runtime::Handle::current().block_on(async {\n518\t                            sb.node_version(&idx, &node_id).await\n519\t                        })\n520\t                    })\n521\t                },\n522\t                replica_selector_ref,\n523\t            ).await;\n524\t\n525\t            match plan_result {\n526\t                Some(p) => p,\n527\t                None => {\n528\t                    // No covering set could be assembled after filtering by version floor\n529\t                    let _err = MeilisearchError::new(\n530\t                        MiroirCode::SettingsVersionStale,\n531\t                        format!(\n532\t                            \"no covering set available for settings version floor {} on index '{}'\",\n533\t                            floor, index\n534\t                        ),\n535\t                    );\n536\t                    return Response::builder()\n537\t                        .status(StatusCode::SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE)\n538\t                        .body(axum::body::Body::empty())\n539\t                        .unwrap();\n540\t                }\n541\t            }\n542\t        } else {\n543\t            // No version floor requested, use normal planning\n544\t            plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, state.config.replication_factor as usize, state.config.shards, replica_selector_ref).await\n545\t        }\n546\t    };\n547\t    let node_count = plan.shard_to_node.len() as u64;\n548\t\n549\t    // Record scatter fan-out size before executing\n550\t    state.metrics.record_scatter_fan_out(node_count);\n551\t\n552\t    // Build search request\n553\t    // Clone facets for fingerprinting before moving into SearchRequest\n554\t    let facets_clone = body.facets.clone();\n555\t    let rest_body = body.rest.clone(); // Clone before body is partially moved\n556\t    let search_req = SearchRequest {\n557\t        index_uid: effective_index.clone(),\n558\t        query: body.q,\n559\t        offset: body.offset.unwrap_or(0),\n560\t        limit: body.limit.unwrap_or(20),\n561\t        filter: body.filter,\n562\t        facets: body.facets,\n563\t        ranking_score: client_requested_score,\n564\t        body: rest_body,\n565\t        global_idf: None,\n566\t    };\n567\t\n568\t    // Create node client with the scoped key (or node_master_key as fallback)\n569\t    let http_client = Arc::new(crate::client::HttpClient::new(\n570\t        search_key,\n571\t        state.config.scatter.node_timeout_ms,\n572\t    ));\n573\t    let client = ProxyNodeClient::new(http_client, state.metrics.clone(), None);\n574\t\n575\t    // Use score-based merge strategy (OP#4: requires global IDF)\n576\t    let strategy = ScoreMergeStrategy::new();\n577\t\n578\t    // Register for query coalescing (plan §13.10) - after try_coalesce, before scatter\n579\t    // Only register if coalescing is enabled and this is a single-target query\n580\t    let (tx, fingerprint) = if state.config.query_coalescing.enabled && resolved_targets.len() == 1 {\n581\t        let settings_version = state.settings_broadcast.current_version().await;\n582\t        // Reconstruct body for fingerprinting (use cloned facets)\n583\t        let fingerprint_body = SearchRequestBody {\n584\t            q: search_req.query.clone(),\n585\t            offset: Some(search_req.offset),\n586\t            limit: Some(search_req.limit),\n587\t            filter: search_req.filter.clone(),\n588\t            facets: facets_clone,\n589\t            ranking_score: Some(search_req.ranking_score),\n590\t            rest: search_req.body.clone(),\n591\t        };\n592\t        let query_json = serde_json::to_string(&fingerprint_body).unwrap_or_default();\n593\t        let fp = QueryFingerprint {\n594\t            index: effective_index.clone(),\n595\t            query_json,\n596\t            settings_version,\n597\t        };\n598\t\n599\t        match state.query_coalescer.register(fp.clone()).await {\n600\t            Ok(broadcast_tx) => {\n601\t                state.metrics.inc_query_coalesce_subscribers(1); // First subscriber = the query itself\n602\t                (Some(broadcast_tx), Some(fp))\n603\t            }\n604\t            Err(_) => {\n605\t                // Failed to register (too many pending queries) - proceed without coalescing\n606\t                debug!(\"too many pending queries, proceeding without coalescing registration\");\n607\t                (None, None)\n608\t            }\n609\t        }\n610\t    } else {\n611\t        (None, None)\n612\t    };\n613\t\n614\t    // Execute DFS query-then-fetch\n615\t    let mut result = match dfs_query_then_fetch_search(\n616\t        plan,\n617\t        &client,\n618\t        search_req,\n619\t        &topo,\n620\t        policy,\n621\t        &strategy,\n622\t    )\n623\t    .await\n624\t    {\n625\t        Ok(r) => r,\n626\t        Err(e) => {\n627\t            tracing::error!(error = %e, \"search failed\");\n628\t            return Response::builder()\n629\t                .status(StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR)\n630\t                .body(axum::body::Body::empty())\n631\t                .unwrap();\n632\t        }\n633\t    };\n634\t\n635\t    // Drop topology lock before building response\n636\t    drop(topo);\n637\t\n638\t    // Strip internal fields from hits\n639\t    for hit in &mut result.hits {\n640\t        strip_internal_fields(hit, client_requested_score);\n641\t    }\n642\t\n643\t    // Build response body\n644\t    let response_body = serde_json::json!({\n645\t        \"hits\": result.hits,\n646\t        \"estimatedTotalHits\": result.estimated_total_hits,\n647\t        \"processingTimeMs\": result.processing_time_ms,\n648\t    });\n649\t\n650\t    // Only include facetDistribution if facets were requested\n651\t    let mut body = response_body.clone();\n652\t    if let Some(facets) = &result.facet_distribution {\n653\t        body[\"facetDistribution\"] = serde_json::to_value(facets).unwrap_or(Value::Null);\n654\t    }\n655\t\n656\t    // Broadcast result to coalesced queries (plan §13.10)\n657\t    if let (Some(broadcast_tx), Some(fp)) = (tx, fingerprint) {\n658\t        let response_bytes = serde_json::to_vec(&body).unwrap_or_default();\n659\t        let subscriber_count = broadcast_tx.receiver_count();\n660\t        if subscriber_count > 1 {\n661\t            // There are other queries waiting for this result - broadcast to them\n662\t            state.metrics.inc_query_coalesce_subscribers(subscriber_count as u64 - 1);\n663\t            let _ = broadcast_tx.send(response_bytes.clone());\n664\t            debug!(\n665\t                index = %effective_index,\n666\t                subscribers = subscriber_count - 1,\n667\t                \"broadcast search result to coalesced queries\"\n668\t            );\n669\t        }\n670\t        // Unregister the query after broadcasting\n671\t        state.query_coalescer.unregister(&fp).await;\n672\t    }\n673\t\n674\t    // Build response with optional headers\n675\t    let mut response = Response::builder()\n676\t        .status(StatusCode::OK)\n677\t        .header(\"content-type\", \"application/json\");\n678\t\n679\t    // Add X-Miroir-Settings-Inconsistent header if a broadcast is in flight (plan §13.5)\n680\t    if state.settings_broadcast.is_in_flight(&effective_index).await {\n681\t        response = response.header(\"X-Miroir-Settings-Inconsistent\", \"true\");\n682\t    }\n683\t\n684\t    // Add X-Miroir-Settings-Version header if we have a version for this index\n685\t    let current_version = state.settings_broadcast.current_version().await;\n686\t    if current_version > 0 {\n687\t        response = response.header(\"X-Miroir-Settings-Version\", current_version.to_string());\n688\t    }\n689\t\n690\t    if result.degraded {\n691\t        state.metrics.inc_scatter_partial_responses();\n692\t    }\n693\t\n694\t    if result.degraded && !result.failed_shards.is_empty() {\n695\t        let mut sorted_shards = result.failed_shards.clone();\n696\t        sorted_shards.sort();\n697\t        let shard_ids = sorted_shards.iter()\n698\t            .map(|id| id.to_string())\n699\t            .collect::<Vec<_>>()\n700\t            .join(\",\");\n701\t        response = response.header(\"X-Miroir-Degraded\", format!(\"shards={}\", shard_ids));\n702\t    } else if result.degraded {\n703\t        response = response.header(\"X-Miroir-Degraded\", \"partial\");\n704\t    }\n705\t\n706\t    let response = response\n707\t        .body(axum::body::Body::from(serde_json::to_string(&body).unwrap()))\n708\t        .unwrap();\n709\t\n710\t    // Structured log entry (plan §10 shape)\n711\t    // request_id and pod_id are included from the middleware span via\n712\t    // .with_current_span(true) on the JSON subscriber layer.\n713\t    tracing::info!(\n714\t        target: \"miroir.search\",\n715\t        index = %effective_index,\n716\t        duration_ms = start.elapsed().as_millis() as u64,\n717\t        node_count = node_count,\n718\t        estimated_hits = result.estimated_total_hits,\n719\t        degraded = result.degraded,\n720\t        \"search completed\"\n721\t    );\n722\t\n723\t    response\n724\t}\n725\t\n726\t/// Search multiple target indexes (for multi-target aliases, plan §13.7, §13.11, §13.17).\n727\t///\n728\t/// Fans out the search to all target indexes and merges results by _rankingScore.\n729\t/// Used by ILM read_alias queries.\n730\tasync fn search_multi_targets(\n731\t    targets: Vec<String>,\n732\t    body: SearchRequestBody,\n733\t    Extension(state): Extension<Arc<AppState>>,\n734\t    session_id: OptionalSessionId,\n735\t    client_requested_score: bool,\n736\t    min_settings_version: Option<u64>,\n737\t) -> Response<Body> {\n738\t    let start = Instant::now();\n739\t\n740\t    // TODO: Extract source IP from headers\n741\t    let source_ip = \"unknown\".to_string();\n742\t\n743\t    // Check rate limit for search UI (plan §4)\n744\t    let (limit, window_seconds) = match parse_rate_limit(&state.config.search_ui.rate_limit.per_ip) {\n745\t        Ok(parsed) => parsed,\n746\t        Err(e) => {\n747\t            warn!(error = %e, \"invalid search_ui.rate_limit.per_ip config, using default\");\n748\t            (60, 60) // Default: 60 requests per minute\n749\t        }\n750\t    };\n751\t\n752\t    let backend = state.config.search_ui.rate_limit.backend.as_str();\n753\t    if backend == \"redis\" {\n754\t        if let Some(ref redis) = state.redis_store {\n755\t            match redis.check_rate_limit_search_ui(&source_ip, limit, window_seconds) {\n756\t                Ok((allowed, _wait_seconds)) => {\n757\t                    if !allowed {\n758\t                        warn!(\n759\t                            source_ip_hash = hash_for_log(&source_ip),\n760\t                            \"search UI rate limited (redis)\"\n761\t                        );\n762\t                        return Response::builder()\n763\t                .status(StatusCode::TOO_MANY_REQUESTS)\n764\t                .body(axum::body::Body::empty())\n765\t                .unwrap();\n766\t                    }\n767\t                    // Allowed, proceed\n768\t                }\n769\t                Err(e) => {\n770\t                    warn!(error = %e, \"failed to check search UI rate limit\");\n771\t                    // Continue anyway on error (fail-open)\n772\t                }\n773\t            }\n774\t        }\n775\t    } else if backend == \"local\" {\n776\t        let (allowed, _wait_seconds) = state.local_search_ui_rate_limiter.check(\n777\t            &source_ip,\n778\t            limit,\n779\t            window_seconds * 1000,\n780\t        );\n781\t        if !allowed {\n782\t            warn!(\n783\t                source_ip_hash = hash_for_log(&source_ip),\n784\t                \"search UI rate limited (local backend)\"\n785\t            );\n786\t            return Response::builder()\n787\t                .status(StatusCode::TOO_MANY_REQUESTS)\n788\t                .body(axum::body::Body::empty())\n789\t                .unwrap();\n790\t        }\n791\t    }\n792\t\n793\t    // Session pinning logic (plan §13.6): Check if session has pending write\n794\t    let sid: Option<String> = session_id.0.as_ref().and_then(|sid| {\n795\t        if sid.0.is_empty() { None } else { Some(sid.0.to_string()) }\n796\t    });\n797\t\n798\t    let (pinned_group, _strategy_label) = if let Some(sid) = sid.as_ref() {\n799\t        if let Some(group) = state.session_manager.get_pinned_group(sid).await {\n800\t            // Session has a pending write - apply wait strategy\n801\t            let strategy = state.session_manager.wait_strategy();\n802\t            match strategy {\n803\t                WaitStrategy::Block => {\n804\t                    // Block until write completes or timeout\n805\t                    let max_wait = state.session_manager.max_wait_duration();\n806\t                    let wait_start = std::time::Instant::now();\n807\t                    match state.session_manager.wait_for_write_completion(\n808\t                        sid,\n809\t                        &state.task_registry,\n810\t                        max_wait,\n811\t                    ).await {\n812\t                        Ok(true) => {\n813\t                            // Write succeeded, clear pin and use normal routing\n814\t                            let wait_duration = wait_start.elapsed().as_secs_f64();\n815\t                            state.metrics.observe_session_wait_duration(wait_duration);\n816\t                            state.metrics.inc_session_pin_enforced(\"block\");\n817\t                            (None::<u32>, None::<u64>)\n818\t                        }\n819\t                        Ok(false) => {\n820\t                            // Write failed, clear pin\n821\t                            let wait_duration = wait_start.elapsed().as_secs_f64();\n822\t                            state.metrics.observe_session_wait_duration(wait_duration);\n823\t                            state.metrics.inc_session_wait_timeout(\"block\");\n824\t                            (None::<u32>, None::<u64>)\n825\t                        }\n826\t                        Err(_) => {\n827\t                            // Timeout - route to pinned group (best effort)\n828\t                            let wait_duration = wait_start.elapsed().as_secs_f64();\n829\t                            state.metrics.observe_session_wait_duration(wait_duration);\n830\t                            state.metrics.inc_session_wait_timeout(\"block\");\n831\t                            (Some(group), None::<u64>)\n832\t                        }\n833\t                    }\n834\t                }\n835\t                WaitStrategy::RoutePin => {\n836\t                    // Route to pinned group without waiting\n837\t                    state.metrics.inc_session_pin_enforced(\"route_pin\");\n838\t                    (Some(group), None::<u64>)\n839\t                }\n840\t            }\n841\t        } else {\n842\t            // No pending write - normal routing\n843\t            (None::<u32>, None::<u64>)\n844\t        }\n845\t    } else {\n846\t        // No session header - normal routing\n847\t        (None::<u32>, None::<u64>)\n848\t    };\n849\t\n850\t    // For multi-target aliases, we use the first target for settings/scoped key\n851\t    // All targets should have compatible settings (managed by ILM)\n852\t    let primary_target = match targets.first() {\n853\t        Some(t) => t,\n854\t        None => {\n855\t            error!(\"multi-target alias has no targets\");\n856\t            return Response::builder()\n857\t                .status(StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR)\n858\t                .body(axum::body::Body::empty())\n859\t                .unwrap();\n860\t        }\n861\t    };\n862\t\n863\t    // Get the scoped key for the primary target\n864\t    let search_key = if let Some(ref redis) = state.redis_store {\n865\t        if let Ok(Some(sk)) = redis.get_search_ui_scoped_key(primary_target) {\n866\t            // Refresh scoped-key beacon\n867\t            let _ = redis.observe_search_ui_scoped_key(\n868\t                &state.pod_id,\n869\t                primary_target,\n870\t                sk.generation,\n871\t            );\n872\t            sk.primary_key\n873\t        } else {\n874\t            state.config.node_master_key.clone()\n875\t        }\n876\t    } else {\n877\t        state.config.node_master_key.clone()\n878\t    };\n879\t\n880\t    // Use live topology from shared state\n881\t    let topo = state.topology.read().await;\n882\t    let policy = match state.config.scatter.unavailable_shard_policy.as_str() {\n883\t        \"partial\" => UnavailableShardPolicy::Partial,\n884\t        \"error\" => UnavailableShardPolicy::Error,\n885\t        \"fallback\" => UnavailableShardPolicy::Fallback,\n886\t        _ => return Response::builder()\n887\t            .status(StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR)\n888\t            .body(axum::body::Body::empty())\n889\t            .unwrap(),\n890\t    };\n891\t\n892\t    // Plan scatter for primary target (for ILM read aliases, all targets\n893\t    // should have identical shard layout)\n894\t    let plan = {\n895\t        let _plan_span = tracing::info_span!(\n896\t            \"scatter_plan_multi_target\",\n897\t            primary_target = %primary_target,\n898\t            replica_groups = state.config.replica_groups,\n899\t            shards = state.config.shards,\n900\t            rf = state.config.replication_factor,\n901\t            min_settings_version,\n902\t            pinned_group = ?pinned_group,\n903\t            target_count = targets.len(),\n904\t        ).entered();\n905\t\n906\t        if let Some(group) = pinned_group {\n907\t            match plan_search_scatter_for_group(\n908\t                &topo,\n909\t                0,\n910\t                state.config.replication_factor as usize,\n911\t                state.config.shards,\n912\t                group,\n913\t                None,\n914\t            ).await {\n915\t                Some(p) => p,\n916\t                None => {\n917\t                    warn!(\n918\t                        pinned_group = group,\n919\t                        \"pinned group unavailable, falling back to normal routing\"\n920\t                    );\n921\t                    plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, state.config.replication_factor as usize, state.config.shards, None).await\n922\t                }\n923\t            }\n924\t        } else if let Some(floor) = min_settings_version {\n925\t            let settings_broadcast = state.settings_broadcast.clone();\n926\t            let plan_result = plan_search_scatter_with_version_floor(\n927\t                &topo,\n928\t                0,\n929\t                state.config.replication_factor as usize,\n930\t                state.config.shards,\n931\t                primary_target,\n932\t                floor,\n933\t                &move |idx, node_id| {\n934\t                    let sb = settings_broadcast.clone();\n935\t                    let idx = idx.to_string();\n936\t                    let node_id = node_id.to_string();\n937\t                    tokio::task::block_in_place(|| {\n938\t                        tokio::runtime::Handle::current().block_on(async {\n939\t                            sb.node_version(&idx, &node_id).await\n940\t                        })\n941\t                    })\n942\t                },\n943\t                None,\n944\t            ).await;\n945\t\n946\t            match plan_result {\n947\t                Some(p) => p,\n948\t                None => {\n949\t                    let _err = MeilisearchError::new(\n950\t                        MiroirCode::SettingsVersionStale,\n951\t                        format!(\n952\t                            \"no covering set available for settings version floor {} on index '{}'\",\n953\t                            floor, primary_target\n954\t                        ),\n955\t                    );\n956\t                    return Response::builder()\n957\t                        .status(StatusCode::SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE)\n958\t                        .body(axum::body::Body::empty())\n959\t                        .unwrap();\n960\t                }\n961\t            }\n962\t        } else {\n963\t            plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, state.config.replication_factor as usize, state.config.shards, None).await\n964\t        }\n965\t    };\n966\t    let node_count = plan.shard_to_node.len() as u64;\n967\t\n968\t    // Record scatter fan-out size\n969\t    state.metrics.record_scatter_fan_out(node_count);\n970\t\n971\t    // Build search request for primary target\n972\t    let search_req = SearchRequest {\n973\t        index_uid: primary_target.to_string(),\n974\t        query: body.q.clone(),\n975\t        offset: body.offset.unwrap_or(0),\n976\t        limit: body.limit.unwrap_or(20),\n977\t        filter: body.filter.clone(),\n978\t        facets: body.facets.clone(),\n979\t        ranking_score: client_requested_score,\n980\t        body: body.rest.clone(),\n981\t        global_idf: None,\n982\t    };\n983\t\n984\t    // Create node client\n985\t    let http_client = Arc::new(crate::client::HttpClient::new(\n986\t        search_key,\n987\t        state.config.scatter.node_timeout_ms,\n988\t       &topo,\n1000\t        policy,\n1001\t        &strategy,\n1002\t    )\n1003\t    .await\n1004\t    {\n1005\t        Ok(r) => r,\n1006\t        Err(e) => {\n1007\t            tracing::error!(error .body(axum::body::Body::empty())\n1011\t                .unwrap();\n1012\t        }\n1013\t    };\n1014\t\n1015\t    // Drop topology lock before building response\n1016\t   }\n1053\t\n1054\t    if result.degraded && !result.failed_shards.is_empty() {\n1055\t        let mut sorted_shards = result.failed_shards.clone();\n1056\t        sorted_shards.sort();\n1057\t        let shard_ids = sorted_shards.iter()\n1058\t            .map(|id| id.to_string())\n1059\t            .collect::<Vec<_>>()\n1060\t            .join(\",\");\n1061\t        response = duration_ms = start.elapsed().as_millis() as u64,\n1076\t        node_count = node_count,\n1077\t        estimated_hits = result.estimated_total_hits,\n1078\t        degraded = `_rankingScore` unless the client explicitly requested it.\n1087\tpub fn strip_internal_fields(hit: &mut Value, client_requested_score: bool) {\n1088\t    if let Some(obj) = hit.as_object_mut() }\n1094\t}\n1095\t\n1096\t#[cfg(test)]\n1097\tmod tests {\n1098\t    use super::*;\n1099\t\n1100\t    #[test]\n1101\t    fn test_search_request_debug_redaction() {\n1102\t        let body = !debug_output.contains(\"sensitive\"),\n1115\t            \"Debug output should not contain raw query text\"\n1116\t        );\n1117\t        assert!(\n1118\t dfs_query_then_fetch_search, plan_search_scatter, plan_search_scatter_for_group, plan_search_scatter_with_version_floor, SearchRequest, NodeClient,\n};\nuse
