Prometheus.\nstruct MetricsObserver {\n    metrics: crate::middleware::Metrics,\n}\n\nimpl SelectionObserver for MetricsObserver {\n    fn report_selection(&self, node_id: &str, score: f64) {\n        self.metrics.set_replica_selection_score(node_id, score);\n    }\n\n    fn report_exploration(&self) {\n        self.metrics.inc_replica_selection_exploration();\n    }\n}\n\n/// Hash a value for logging (obfuscates sensitive data like IPs).\nfn hash_for_log(value: &str) -> String {\n    use std::hash::{Hash, Hasher};\n    let mut hasher = std::collections::hash_map::DefaultHasher::new();\n    value.hash(&mut hasher);\n    format!(\"{:016x}\", hasher.finish())\n}\n\n/// Node client implementation using the HTTP client.\n///\n/// Wraps each node call with Prometheus metrics recording for\n/// `miroir_node_request_duration_seconds` and `miroir_node_errors_total`.\npub struct ProxyNodeClient {\n    client: Arc<crate::client::HttpClient>,\n    metrics: crate::middleware::Metrics,\n    replica_selector: Option<Arc<miroir_core::replica_selection::ReplicaSelector>>,\n}\n\nimpl ProxyNodeClient {\n    pub fn new(\n        client: Arc<crate::client::HttpClient>,\n        metrics: crate::middleware::Metrics,\n        replica_selector: Option<Arc<miroir_core::replica_selection::ReplicaSelector>>,\n    ) -> Self {\n        Self { client, metrics, replica_selector }\n    }\n}\n\n#[allow(async_fn_in_trait)]\nimpl NodeClient for ProxyNodeClient {\n    async fn search_node(\n        &self,\n        node: &miroir_core::topology::NodeId,\n        address: &str,\n        request: &SearchRequest,\n    ) -> std::result::Result<Value, miroir_core::scatter::NodeError> {\n        let start = Instant::now();\n        let result = self.client.search_node(node, address, request).await;\n        let elapsed = start.elapsed().as_secs_f64();\n        self.metrics.record_node_request_duration(node.as_str(), \"search\", elapsed);\n        if let Err(ref e) = result {\n            self.metrics.inc_node_errors(node.as_str(), error_label(e));\n        }\n        result\n    }\n\n    async fn preflight_node(\n        &self,\n        node: &miroir_core::topology::NodeId,\n        address: &str,\n        request: &miroir_core::scatter::PreflightRequest,\n    ) -> std::result::Result<miroir_core::scatter::PreflightResponse, miroir_core::scatter::NodeError> {\n        let start = Instant::now();\n        let result = self.client.preflight_node(node, address, request).await;\n        let elapsed = start.elapsed().as_secs_f64();\n        self.metrics.record_node_request_duration(node.as_str(), \"preflight\", elapsed);\n        if let Err(ref e) = result {\n            self.metrics.inc_node_errors(node.as_str(), error_label(e));\n        }\n        result\n    }\n}\n\nfn error_label(e: &miroir_core::scatter::NodeError) -> &'static str {\n    match e {\n        miroir_core::scatter::NodeError::NetworkError(_) => \"network\",\n        miroir_core::scatter::NodeError::HttpError { .. } => \"http\",\n        miroir_core::scatter::NodeError::Timeout => \"timeout\",\n    }\n}\n\npub fn router<S>() -> axum::Router<S>\nwhere\n    S: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,\n{\n    axum::Router::new()\n        .route(\"/:index\", axum::routing::post(search_handler))\n}\n\n/// Search request body.\n#[derive(Deserialize, Serialize)]\nstruct SearchRequestBody {\n    q: Option<String>,\n    offset: Option<usize>,\n    limit: Option<usize>,\n    filter: Option<Value>,\n    facets: Option<Vec<String>>,\n    #[serde(rename = \"rankingScore\")]\n    ranking_score: Option<bool>,\n    #[serde(flatten)]\n    rest: Value,\n}\n\nimpl std::fmt::Debug for SearchRequestBody {\n    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut std::fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result {\n        f.debug_struct(\"SearchRequestBody\")\n            .field(\"q\", &\"[redacted]\")\n            .field(\"offset\", &self.offset)\n            .field(\"limit\", &self.limit)\n            .field(\"filter\", &\"[redacted]\")\n            .field(\"facets\", &self.facets)\n            .field(\"ranking_score\", &self.ranking_score)\n            .finish_non_exhaustive()\n    }\n}\n\n/// Search handler with DFS global-IDF preflight (OP#4).\n///\n/// This handler implements the `dfs_query_then_fetch` pattern:\n/// 1. **Preflight phase**: Send term-frequency query to all shards, aggregate\n///    global document frequencies at the coordinator.\n/// 2. **Search phase**: Send the search query with global IDF attached so that\n///    scoring uses corpus-wide statistics instead of per-shard local IDF.\n///\n/// Returns `X-Miroir-Degraded: shards=X,Y,Z` header when any shards are unavailable.\n/// Strips `_miroir_shard` from all hits; strips `_rankingScore` unless client\n/// explicitly requested it.\n///\n/// Session pinning (plan §13.6): If `X-Miroir-Session` header is present and\n/// the session has a pending write, routes to the pinned group for read-your-writes.\nasync fn search_handler(\n    Path(index): Path<String>,\n    Extension(state): Extension<Arc<AppState>>,\n    OptionalSessionId(session_id_wrapper): OptionalSessionId,\n    headers: HeaderMap,\n    Json(body): Json<SearchRequestBody>,\n) -> Response<Body> {\n    let start = Instant::now();\n    let client_requested_score = body.ranking_score.unwrap_or(false);\n\n    // Extract session ID from OptionalSessionId extractor (set by session_pinning_middleware)\n    let sid: Option<String> = session_id_wrapper.as_ref().and_then(|sid| {\n        if sid.0.is_empty() { None } else { Some(sid.0.to_string()) }\n    });\n\n    // Clone session_id_wrapper for later use in multi-target search\n    let session_id_clone = session_id_wrapper.clone();\n\n    // TODO: Extract source IP from headers - need to add back HeaderMap extraction\n    let source_ip = \"unknown\".to_string();\n\n    // Check rate limit for search UI (plan §4)\n    let (limit, window_seconds) = match parse_rate_limit(&state.config.search_ui.rate_limit.per_ip) {\n        Ok(parsed) => parsed,\n        Err(e) => {\n            warn!(error = %e, \"invalid search_ui.rate_limit.per_ip config, using default\");\n            (60, 60) // Default: 60 requests per minute\n        }\n    };\n\n    let backend = state.config.search_ui.rate_limit.backend.as_str();\n    if backend == \"redis\" {\n        if let Some(ref redis) = state.redis_store {\n            match redis.check_rate_limit_search_ui(&source_ip, limit, window_seconds) {\n                Ok((allowed, _wait_seconds)) => {\n                    if !allowed {\n                        warn!(\n                            source_ip_hash = hash_for_log(&source_ip),\n                            \"search UI rate limited (redis)\"\n                        );\n                        return Response::builder()\n                .status(StatusCode::TOO_MANY_REQUESTS)\n                .body(axum::body::Body::empty())\n                .unwrap();\n                    }\n                    // Allowed, proceed\n                }\n                Err(e) => {\n                    warn!(error = %e, \"failed to check search UI rate limit\");\n                    // Continue anyway on error (fail-open)\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    } else if backend == \"local\" {\n        let (allowed, _wait_seconds) = state.local_search_ui_rate_limiter.check(\n            &source_ip,\n            limit,\n            window_seconds * 1000,\n        );\n        if !allowed {\n            warn!(\n                source_ip_hash = hash_for_log(&source_ip),\n                \"search UI rate limited (local backend)\"\n            );\n            return Response::builder()\n                .status(StatusCode::TOO_MANY_REQUESTS)\n                .body(axum::body::Body::empty())\n                .unwrap();\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Resolve alias to concrete index UID (plan §13.7)\n    // Aliases are resolved at routing time; resolved index is used for scatter planning\n    let (effective_index, resolved_targets) = if state.config.aliases.enabled {\n        let targets = state.alias_registry.resolve(&index).await;\n        state.metrics.inc_alias_resolution(&index);\n        if targets != vec![index.clone()] {\n            // It's an alias\n            (index.clone(), targets)\n        } else {\n            // Not an alias - use index directly\n            (index.clone(), vec![index.clone()])\n        }\n    } else {\n        // Aliases disabled - use index directly\n        (index.clone(), vec![index.clone()])\n    };\n\n    // Session pinning logic (plan §13.6): Check if session has pending write\n    let (pinned_group, strategy_label) = if let Some(sid) = sid.as_ref() {\n        if let Some(group) = state.session_manager.get_pinned_group(sid).await {\n            // Session has a pending write - apply wait strategy\n            let strategy = state.session_manager.wait_strategy();\n            match strategy {\n                WaitStrategy::Block => {\n                    // Block until write completes or timeout\n                    let max_wait = state.session_manager.max_wait_duration();\n                    let wait_start = std::time::Instant::now();\n                    match state.session_manager.wait_for_write_completion(\n                        sid,\n                        &state.task_registry,\n                        max_wait,\n                    ).await {\n                        Ok(true) => {\n                            // Write succeeded, clear pin and use normal routing\n                            let wait_duration = wait_start.elapsed().as_secs_f64();\n                            state.metrics.observe_session_wait_duration(wait_duration);\n                            state.metrics.inc_session_pin_enforced(\"block\");\n                            (None, Some(\"block_success\"))\n                        }\n                        Ok(false) => {\n                            // Write failed, clear pin\n                            let wait_duration = wait_start.elapsed().as_secs_f64();\n                            state.metrics.observe_session_wait_duration(wait_duration);\n                            state.metrics.inc_session_wait_timeout(\"block\");\n                            (None, Some(\"block_failed\"))\n                        }\n                        Err(_) => {\n                            // Timeout - still route to pinned group (best effort)\n                            let wait_duration = wait_start.elapsed().as_secs_f64();\n                            state.metrics.observe_session_wait_duration(wait_duration);\n                            state.metrics.inc_session_wait_timeout(\"block\");\n                            (Some(group), Some(\"block_timeout\"))\n                        }\n                    }\n                }\n                WaitStrategy::RoutePin => {\n                    // Route to pinned group without waiting\n                    state.metrics.inc_session_pin_enforced(\"route_pin\");\n                    (Some(group), Some(\"route_pin\"))\n                }\n            }\n        } else {\n            // No pending write - normal routing\n            (None, None)\n        }\n    } else {\n        // No session header - normal routing\n        (None, None)\n    };\n\n    // Log session pinning decision for observability\n    if let Some(label) = strategy_label {\n        debug!(\n            session_id = ?sid,\n            pinned_group = pinned_group,\n            strategy = label,\n            \"session pinning applied\"\n        );\n    }\n\n    // Query coalescing (plan §13.10): Check for identical in-flight queries\n    // Skip for multi-target aliases (each target is different)\n    if state.config.query_coalescing.enabled && resolved_targets.len() == 1 {\n        // Build fingerprint from canonicalized query body + index + settings version\n        let settings_version = state.settings_broadcast.current_version().await;\n        let query_json = serde_json::to_string(&body).unwrap_or_default();\n        let fingerprint = QueryFingerprint {\n            index: effective_index.clone(),\n            query_json,\n            settings_version,\n        };\n\n        // Try to coalesce with an existing in-flight query\n        if let Some(mut rx) = state.query_coalescer.try_coalesce(fingerprint.clone()).await {\n            // Successfully subscribed to an in-flight query - wait for its result\n            state.metrics.inc_query_coalesce_hits();\n            debug!(\n                index = %effective_index,\n                \"query coalesced: waiting for in-flight query result\"\n            );\n\n            // Wait for the response (with timeout matching the scatter timeout)\n            let timeout = Duration::from_millis(state.config.scatter.node_timeout_ms);\n            let response_bytes = tokio::time::timeout(timeout, async move {\n                rx.recv().await\n            }).await;\n\n            match response_bytes {\n                Ok(Ok(response_bytes)) => {\n                    // Received response from coalesced query\n                    let response_body: Value = match serde_json::from_slice(&response_bytes) {\n                        Ok(v) => v,\n                        Err(e) => {\n                            error!(error = %e, \"failed to deserialize coalesced query response\");\n                            return Response::builder()\n                                .status(StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR)\n                                .body(axum::body::Body::empty())\n                                .unwrap();\n                        }\n                    };\n\n                    // Build response with appropriate headers\n                    let mut response = Response::builder()\n                        .status(StatusCode::OK)\n                        .header(\"content-type\", \"application/json\");\n\n                    // Add settings headers\n                    if state.settings_broadcast.is_in_flight(&effective_index).await {\n                        response = response.header(\"X-Miroir-Settings-Inconsistent\", \"true\");\n                    }\n                    if settings_version > 0 {\n                        response = response.header(\"X-Miroir-Settings-Version\", settings_version.to_string());\n                    }\n\n                    let response = response\n                        .body(axum::body::Body::from(serde_json::to_string(&response_body).unwrap()))\n                        .unwrap();\n\n                    tracing::info!(\n                        target: \"miroir.search_coalesced\",\n                        index = %effective_index,\n                        duration_ms = start.elapsed().as_millis() as u64,\n                        \"coalesced search completed\"\n                    );\n\n                    return response;\n                }\n                Ok(Err(_)) => {\n                    // Channel closed without receiving response - proceed with normal scatter\n                    debug!(\"coalesced query channel closed, proceeding with normal scatter\");\n                }\n                Err(_) => {\n                    // Timeout waiting for coalesced query - proceed with normal scatter\n                    debug!(\"timeout waiting for coalesced query, proceeding with normal scatter\");\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Extract X-Miroir-Min-Settings-Version header (plan §13.5)\n    // Extract early for multi-target search path\n    let min_settings_version = headers\n        .get(\"X-Miroir-Min-Settings-Version\")\n        .and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok())\n        .and_then(|s| s.parse::<u64>().ok());\n\n    // Handle multi-target alias fanout (plan §13.7, §13.11, §13.17)\n    // Multi-target aliases (ILM read_alias) require fanning out to all targets\n    // and merging results by _rankingScore\n    if resolved_targets.len() > 1 {\n        // Need to create a new Extension wrapper for the nested call\n        // Clone the Arc for the multi-target search\n        return search_multi_targets(\n            resolved_targets,\n            body,\n            Extension(state.clone()),\n            OptionalSessionId(session_id_wrapper.clone()),\n            client_requested_score,\n            min_settings_version,\n        ).await;\n    }\n\n    // Get the scoped key for this index (plan §13.21).\n    // If a scoped key exists, use primary_key (or previous_key during rotation overlap).\n    // If no scoped key exists yet, fall back to node_master_key for initial setup.\n    let search_key = if let Some(ref redis) = state.redis_store {\n        if let Ok(Some(sk)) = redis.get_search_ui_scoped_key(&effective_index) {\n            // Refresh scoped-key beacon so the rotation leader knows this pod is serving\n            // requests for this index at the current generation (plan §13.21).\n            let _ = redis.observe_search_ui_scoped_key(\n                &state.pod_id,\n                &index,\n                sk.generation,\n            );\n\n            // Use primary_key; previous_key is the overlap fallback (both are valid in Meilisearch)\n            sk.primary_key\n        } else {\n            // No scoped key yet — fall back to node_master_key for initial setup\n            state.config.node_master_key.clone()\n        }\n    } else {\n        // No Redis store — fall back to node_master_key (single-pod dev mode)\n        state.config.node_master_key.clone()\n    };\n\n    // Extract X-Miroir-Min-Settings-Version header (plan §13.5)\n    let min_settings_version = headers\n        .get(\"X-Miroir-Min-Settings-Version\")\n        .and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok())\n        .and_then(|s| s.parse::<u64>().ok());\n\n    // Use live topology from shared state (updated by health checker)\n    let topo = state.topology.read().await;\n    let policy = match state.config.scatter.unavailable_shard_policy.as_str() {\n        \"partial\" => UnavailableShardPolicy::Partial,\n        \"error\" => UnavailableShardPolicy::Error,\n        \"fallback\" => UnavailableShardPolicy::Fallback,\n        _ => return Response::builder()\n            .status(StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR)\n            .body(axum::body::Body::empty())\n            .unwrap(),\n    };\n\n    // Plan scatter using live topology (span for plan construction)\n    let plan = {\n        let _plan_span = tracing::info_span!(\n            \"scatter_plan\",\n            replica_groups = state.config.replica_groups,\n            shards = state.config.shards,\n            rf = state.config.replication_factor,\n            min_settings_version,\n            pinned_group = ?pinned_group,\n            strategy = %state.config.replica_selection.strategy,\n        ).entered();\n\n        // Determine if we should use adaptive selection\n        let use_adaptive = state.config.replica_selection.strategy == \"adaptive\";\n        let replica_selector_ref = if use_adaptive {\n            Some(state.replica_selector.as_ref())\n        } else {\n            None\n        };\n\n        // Session pinning: if pinned_group is set, use group-specific planning\n        if let Some(group) = pinned_group {\n            match plan_search_scatter_for_group(\n                &topo,\n                0,\n                state.config.replication_factor as usize,\n                state.config.shards,\n                group,\n                replica_selector_ref,\n            ).await {\n                Some(p) => p,\n                None => {\n                    // Pinned group not available - fall back to normal planning\n                    // This can happen if the pinned group has failed\n                    warn!(\n                        pinned_group = group,\n                        \"pinned group unavailable, falling back to normal routing\"\n                    );\n                    plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, state.config.replication_factor as usize, state.config.shards, replica_selector_ref).await\n                }\n            }\n        } else if let Some(floor) = min_settings_version {\n            // If client provided a min settings version floor, use version-filtered planning\n            // Clone the settings broadcast for version checking\n            let settings_broadcast = state.settings_broadcast.clone();\n            let plan_result = plan_search_scatter_with_version_floor(\n                &topo,\n                0,\n                state.config.replication_factor as usize,\n                state.config.shards,\n                &index,\n                floor,\n                &move |idx, node_id| {\n                    // Use a blocking task wrapper since we're in a sync context\n                    let sb = settings_broadcast.clone();\n                    let idx = idx.to_string();\n                    let node_id = node_id.to_string();\n                    tokio::task::block_in_place(|| {\n                        tokio::runtime::Handle::current().block_on(async {\n                            sb.node_version(&idx, &node_id).await\n                        })\n                    })\n                },\n                replica_selector_ref,\n            ).await;\n\n            match plan_result {\n                Some(p) => p,\n                None => {\n                    // No covering set could be assembled after filtering by version floor\n                    let _err = MeilisearchError::new(\n                        MiroirCode::SettingsVersionStale,\n                        format!(\n                            \"no covering set available for settings version floor {} on index '{}'\",\n                            floor, index\n                        ),\n                    );\n                    return Response::builder()\n                        .status(StatusCode::SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE)\n                        .body(axum::body::Body::empty())\n                        .unwrap();\n                }\n            }\n        } else {\n            // No version floor requested, use normal planning\n            plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, state.config.replication_factor as usize, state.config.shards, replica_selector_ref).await\n        }\n    };\n    let node_count = plan.shard_to_node.len() as u64;\n\n    // Record scatter fan-out size before executing\n    state.metrics.record_scatter_fan_out(node_count);\n\n    // Build search request\n    // Clone facets for fingerprinting before moving into SearchRequest\n    let facets_clone = body.facets.clone();\n    let rest_body = body.rest.clone(); // Clone before body is partially moved\n    let search_req = SearchRequest {\n        index_uid: effective_index.clone(),\n        query: body.q,\n        offset: body.offset.unwrap_or(0),\n        limit: body.limit.unwrap_or(20),\n        filter: body.filter,\n        facets: body.facets,\n        ranking_score: client_requested_score,\n        body: rest_body,\n        global_idf: None,\n    };\n\n    // Create node client with the scoped key (or node_master_key as fallback)\n    let http_client = Arc::new(crate::client::HttpClient::new(\n        search_key,\n        state.config.scatter.node_timeout_ms,\n    ));\n    let client = ProxyNodeClient::new(http_client, state.metrics.clone(), None);\n\n    // Use score-based merge strategy (OP#4: requires global IDF)\n    let strategy = ScoreMergeStrategy::new();\n\n    // Register for query coalescing (plan §13.10) - after try_coalesce, before scatter\n    // Only register if coalescing is enabled and this is a single-target query\n    let (tx, fingerprint) = if state.config.query_coalescing.enabled && resolved_targets.len() == 1 {\n        let settings_version = state.settings_broadcast.current_version().await;\n        // Reconstruct body for fingerprinting (use cloned facets)\n        let fingerprint_body = SearchRequestBody {\n            q: search_req.query.clone(),\n            offset: Some(search_req.offset),\n            limit: Some(search_req.limit),\n            filter: search_req.filter.clone(),\n            facets: facets_clone,\n            ranking_score: Some(search_req.ranking_score),\n            rest: search_req.body.clone(),\n        };\n        let query_json = serde_json::to_string(&fingerprint_body).unwrap_or_default();\n        let fp = QueryFingerprint {\n            index: effective_index.clone(),\n            query_json,\n            settings_version,\n        };\n\n        match state.query_coalescer.register(fp.clone()).await {\n            Ok(broadcast_tx) => {\n                state.metrics.inc_query_coalesce_subscribers(1); // First subscriber = the query itself\n                (Some(broadcast_tx), Some(fp))\n            }\n            Err(_) => {\n                // Failed to register (too many pending queries) - proceed without coalescing\n                debug!(\"too many pending queries, proceeding without coalescing registration\");\n                (None, None)\n            }\n        }\n    } else {\n        (None, None)\n    };\n\n    // Execute DFS query-then-fetch\n    let mut result = match dfs_query_then_fetch_search(\n        plan,\n        &client,\n        search_req,\n        &topo,\n        policy,\n        &strategy,\n    )\n    .await\n    {\n        Ok(r) => r,\n        Err(e) => {\n            tracing::error!(error = %e, \"search failed\");\n            return Response::builder()\n                .status(StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR)\n                .body(axum::body::Body::empty())\n                .unwrap();\n        }\n    };\n\n    // Drop topology lock before building response\n    drop(topo);\n\n    // Strip internal fields from hits\n    for hit in &mut result.hits {\n        strip_internal_fields(hit, client_requested_score);\n    }\n\n    // Build response body\n    let response_body = serde_json::json!({\n        \"hits\": result.hits,\n        \"estimatedTotalHits\": result.estimated_total_hits,\n        \"processingTimeMs\": result.processing_time_ms,\n    });\n\n    // Only include facetDistribution if facets were requested\n    let mut body = response_body.clone();\n    if let Some(facets) = &result.facet_distribution {\n        body[\"facetDistribution\"] = serde_json::to_value(facets).unwrap_or(Value::Null);\n    }\n\n    // Broadcast result to coalesced queries (plan §13.10)\n    if let (Some(broadcast_tx), Some(fp)) = (tx, fingerprint) {\n        let response_bytes = serde_json::to_vec(&body).unwrap_or_default();\n        let subscriber_count = broadcast_tx.receiver_count();\n        if subscriber_count > 1 {\n            // There are other queries waiting for this result - broadcast to them\n            state.metrics.inc_query_coalesce_subscribers(subscriber_count as u64 - 1);\n            let _ = broadcast_tx.send(response_bytes.clone());\n            debug!(\n                index = %effective_index,\n                subscribers = subscriber_count - 1,\n                \"broadcast search result to coalesced queries\"\n            );\n        }\n        // Unregister the query after broadcasting\n        state.query_coalescer.unregister(&fp).await;\n    }\n\n    // Build response with optional headers\n    let mut response = Response::builder()\n        .status(StatusCode::OK)\n        .header(\"content-type\", \"application/json\");\n\n    // Add X-Miroir-Settings-Inconsistent header if a broadcast is in flight (plan §13.5)\n    if state.settings_broadcast.is_in_flight(&effective_index).await {\n        response = response.header(\"X-Miroir-Settings-Inconsistent\", \"true\");\n    }\n\n    // Add X-Miroir-Settings-Version header if we have a version for this index\n    let current_version = state.settings_broadcast.current_version().await;\n    if current_version > 0 {\n        response = response.header(\"X-Miroir-Settings-Version\", current_version.to_string());\n    }\n\n    if result.degraded {\n        state.metrics.inc_scatter_partial_responses();\n    }\n\n    if result.degraded && !result.failed_shards.is_empty() {\n        let mut sorted_shards = result.failed_shards.clone();\n        sorted_shards.sort();\n        let shard_ids = sorted_shards.iter()\n            .map(|id| id.to_string())\n            .collect::<Vec<_>>()\n            .join(\",\");\n        response = response.header(\"X-Miroir-Degraded\", format!(\"shards={}\", shard_ids));\n    } else if result.degraded {\n        response = response.header(\"X-Miroir-Degraded\", \"partial\");\n    }\n\n    let response = response\n        .body(axum::body::Body::from(serde_json::to_string(&body).unwrap()))\n        .unwrap();\n\n    // Structured log entry (plan §10 shape)\n    // request_id and pod_id are included from the middleware span via\n    // .with_current_span(true) on the JSON subscriber layer.\n    tracing::info!(\n        target: \"miroir.search\",\n        index = %effective_index,\n        duration_ms = start.elapsed().as_millis() as u64,\n        node_count = node_count,\n        estimated_hits = result.estimated_total_hits,\n        degraded = result.degraded,\n        \"search completed\"\n    );\n\n    response\n}\n\n/// Search multiple target indexes (for multi-target aliases, plan §13.7, §13.11, §13.17).\n///\n/// Fans out the search to all target indexes and merges results by _rankingScore.\n/// Used by ILM read_alias queries.\nasync fn search_multi_targets(\n    targets: Vec<String>,\n    body: SearchRequestBody,\n    Extension(state): Extension<Arc<AppState>>,\n    session_id: OptionalSessionId,\n    client_requested_score: bool,\n    min_settings_version: Option<u64>,\n) -> Response<Body> {\n    let start = Instant::now();\n\n    // TODO: Extract source IP from headers\n    let source_ip = \"unknown\".to_string();\n\n    // Check rate limit for search UI (plan §4)\n    let (limit, window_seconds) = match parse_rate_limit(&state.config.search_ui.rate_limit.per_ip) {\n        Ok(parsed) => parsed,\n        Err(e) => {\n            warn!(error = %e, \"invalid search_ui.rate_limit.per_ip config, using default\");\n            (60, 60) // Default: 60 requests per minute\n        }\n    };\n\n    let backend = state.config.search_ui.rate_limit.backend.as_str();\n    if backend == \"redis\" {\n        if let Some(ref redis) = state.redis_store {\n            match redis.check_rate_limit_search_ui(&source_ip, limit, window_seconds) {\n                Ok((allowed, _wait_seconds)) => {\n                    if !allowed {\n                        warn!(\n                            source_ip_hash = hash_for_log(&source_ip),\n                            \"search UI rate limited (redis)\"\n                        );\n                        return Response::builder()\n                .status(StatusCode::TOO_MANY_REQUESTS)\n                .body(axum::body::Body::empty())\n                .unwrap();\n                    }\n                    // Allowed, proceed\n                }\n                Err(e) => {\n                    warn!(error = %e, \"failed to check search UI rate limit\");\n                    // Continue anyway on error (fail-open)\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    } else if backend == \"local\" {\n        let (allowed, _wait_seconds) = state.local_search_ui_rate_limiter.check(\n            &source_ip,\n            limit,\n            window_seconds * 1000,\n        );\n        if !allowed {\n            warn!(\n                source_ip_hash = hash_for_log(&source_ip),\n                \"search UI rate limited (local backend)\"\n            );\n            return Response::builder()\n                .status(StatusCode::TOO_MANY_REQUESTS)\n                .body(axum::body::Body::empty())\n                .unwrap();\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Session pinning logic (plan §13.6): Check if session has pending write\n    let sid: Option<String> = session_id.0.as_ref().and_then(|sid| {\n        if sid.0.is_empty() { None } else { Some(sid.0.to_string()) }\n    });\n\n    let (pinned_group, _strategy_label) = if let Some(sid) = sid.as_ref() {\n        if let Some(group) = state.session_manager.get_pinned_group(sid).await {\n            // Session has a pending write - apply wait strategy\n            let strategy = state.session_manager.wait_strategy();\n            match strategy {\n                WaitStrategy::Block => {\n                    // Block until write completes or timeout\n                    let max_wait = state.session_manager.max_wait_duration();\n                    let wait_start = std::time::Instant::now();\n                    match state.session_manager.wait_for_write_completion(\n                        sid,\n                        &state.task_registry,\n                        max_wait,\n                    ).await {\n                        Ok(true) => {\n                            // Write succeeded, clear pin and use normal routing\n                            let wait_duration = wait_start.elapsed().as_secs_f64();\n                            state.metrics.observe_session_wait_duration(wait_duration);\n                            state.metrics.inc_session_pin_enforced(\"block\");\n                            (None::<u32>, None::<u64>)\n                        }\n                        Ok(false) => {\n                            // Write failed, clear pin\n                            let wait_duration = wait_start.elapsed().as_secs_f64();\n                            state.metrics.observe_session_wait_duration(wait_duration);\n                            state.metrics.inc_session_wait_timeout(\"block\");\n                            (None::<u32>, None::<u64>)\n                        }\n                        Err(_) => {\n                            // Timeout - route to pinned group (best effort)\n                            let wait_duration = wait_start.elapsed().as_secs_f64();\n                            state.metrics.observe_session_wait_duration(wait_duration);\n                            state.metrics.inc_session_wait_timeout(\"block\");\n                            (Some(group), None::<u64>)\n                        }\n                    }\n                }\n                WaitStrategy::RoutePin => {\n                    // Route to pinned group without waiting\n                    state.metrics.inc_session_pin_enforced(\"route_pin\");\n                    (Some(group), None::<u64>)\n                }\n            }\n        } else {\n            // No pending write - normal routing\n            (None::<u32>, None::<u64>)\n        }\n    } else {\n        // No session header - normal routing\n        (None::<u32>, None::<u64>)\n    };\n\n    // For multi-target aliases, we use the first target for settings/scoped key\n    // All targets should have compatible settings (managed by ILM)\n    let primary_target = match targets.first() {\n        Some(t) => t,\n        None => {\n            error!(\"multi-target alias has no targets\");\n            return Response::builder()\n                .status(StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR)\n                .body(axum::body::Body::empty())\n                .unwrap();\n        }\n    };\n\n    // Get the scoped key for the primary target\n    let search_key = if let Some(ref redis) = state.redis_store {\n        if let Ok(Some(sk)) = redis.get_search_ui_scoped_key(primary_target) {\n            // Refresh scoped-key beacon\n            let _ = redis.observe_search_ui_scoped_key(\n                &state.pod_id,\n                primary_target,\n                sk.generation,\n            );\n            sk.primary_key\n        } else {\n            state.config.node_master_key.clone()\n        }\n    } else {\n        state.config.node_master_key.clone()\n    };\n\n    // Use live topology from shared state\n    let topo = state.topology.read().await;\n    let policy = match state.config.scatter.unavailable_shard_policy.as_str() {\n        \"partial\" => UnavailableShardPolicy::Partial,\n        \"error\" => UnavailableShardPolicy::Error,\n        \"fallback\" => UnavailableShardPolicy::Fallback,\n        _ => return Response::builder()\n            .status(StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR)\n            .body(axum::body::Body::empty())\n            .unwrap(),\n    };\n\n    // Plan scatter for primary target (for ILM read aliases, all targets\n    // should have identical shard layout)\n    let plan = {\n        let _plan_span = tracing::info_span!(\n            \"scatter_plan_multi_target\",\n            primary_target = %primary_target,\n            replica_groups = state.config.replica_groups,\n            shards = state.config.shards,\n            rf = state.config.replication_factor,\n            min_settings_version,\n            pinned_group = ?pinned_group,\n            target_count = targets.len(),\n        ).entered();\n\n        if let Some(group) = pinned_group {\n            match plan_search_scatter_for_group(\n                &topo,\n                0,\n                state.config.replication_factor as usize,\n                state.config.shards,\n                group,\n                None,\n            ).await {\n                Some(p) => p,\n                None => {\n                    warn!(\n                        pinned_group = group,\n                        \"pinned group unavailable, falling back to normal routing\"\n                    );\n                    plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, state.config.replication_factor as usize, state.config.shards, None).await\n                }\n            }\n        } else if let Some(floor) = min_settings_version {\n            let settings_broadcast = state.settings_broadcast.clone();\n            let plan_result = plan_search_scatter_with_version_floor(\n                &topo,\n                0,\n                state.config.replication_factor as usize,\n                state.config.shards,\n                primary_target,\n                floor,\n                &move |idx, node_id|                   .status(StatusCode::SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE)\n                        .body(axum::body::Body::empty())\n                        .unwrap();\n                }\n            }\n        } u64;\n\n    // Record scatter fan-out size\n    state.metrics.record_scatter_fan_out(node_count);\n\n    // Build search request for primary target\n    let search_req = SearchRequest {\n   facets: body.facets.clone(),\n        ranking_score: client_requested_score,\n        body: body.rest.clone(),\n        global_idf: None,\n    };\n\n    // Create node client\n    let http_client = Arc::new(crate::client::HttpClient::new(\n        search_key,\n        state.config.scatter.node_timeout_ms,\n    ));\n    let client = ProxyNodeClient::new(http_client, state.metrics.clone(), None);\n\n    // Use score-based merge strategy\n    let strategy = ScoreMergeStrategy::new();\n\n    // Execute search\n    let mut result = match dfs_query_then_fetch_search(\n        plan,\n        &client,\n        search_req,\n        &topo,\n        policy,\n        &strategy,\n    )\n    .await\n    {\n        Ok(r) => r,\n        Err(e) => {\n            tracing::error!(error = %e, \"multi-target search failed\");\n            return Response::builder()\n                .status(StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR)\n                .body(axum::body::Body::empty())\n                .unwrap();\n        }\n    };\n\n    // Drop topology lock before building response\n    drop(topo);\n\n    // Strip internal fields from hits\n    for hit in &mut result.hits {\n        strip_internal_fields(hit, client_requested_score);\n    }\n\n    // Build response body\n    let mut response_body = serde_json::json!({\n        \"hits\": result.hits,\n        \"estimatedTotalHits\": result.estimated_total_hits,\n        \"processingTimeMs\": result.processing_time_ms,\n    });\n\n    // Only include facetDistribution if facets were requested\n    if let Some(facets) = &result.facet_distribution {\n        response_body[\"facetDistribution\"] = serde_json::to_value(facets).unwrap_or(Value::Null);\n    }\n\n    // Build response with optional headers\n obj.remove(\"_rankingScore\");\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_search_request_debug_redaction() {\n        let body = SearchRequestBody {\n36\t    /// Create a new RequestId from a UUIDv7.\n37\t    ///\n38\t    /// Hashes the full UUIDv7 to produce an 8-character hex ID that is unique\n39\t    /// even for consecutive calls within the same millisecond.\n40\t    pub fn new() -> Self {\n41\t        let uuid = Uuid::now_v7();\n42\t        let bytes = uuid.as_bytes();\n43\t        // Hash the full UUID to ensure uniqueness even within the same millisecond\n44\t        let mut hasher = DefaultHasher::new();\n45\t        hasher.write(bytes);\n46\t        let hash = hasher.finish();\n47\t        // Take first 8 hex chars of 64-bit hash (32 bits is sufficient entropy)\n48\t        Self(format!(\"{:08x}\", hash as u32))\n49\t    }\n50\t\n51\t    /// Get the inner request ID string.\n52\t    pub fn as_str(&self) -> &str {\n53\t        &self.0\n54\t    }\n55\t\n56\t    /// Parse a RequestId from a string.\n57\t    pub fn parse(s: String) -> Option<Self> {\n58\t        if s.len() == 8 && s.chars().all(|c| c.is_ascii_hexdigit()) {\n59\t            Some(Self(s))\n60\t        } else {\n61\t            None\n62\t        }\n63\t    }\n64\t}\n65\t\n66\t/// Session ID wrapper type for read-your-writes session pinning (plan async fn request_id_middleware(\n120\t    mut req: Request,\n121\t    next: Next,\n122\t) -> Response {\n123\t    // Check for existing request ID in headers\n124\t    let request_id = req\n125\t }\n138\t\n139\t    // Process the request\n140\t    let mut response = next.run(req).await;\n141\t\n142\t    // Add X-Request-Id header to response (override if exists)\n143\t    if let Ok(val) =        let metric_families = self.registry.gather();\n1125\t        let mut buffer = Vec::new();\n1126\t        encoder.encode(&metric_families, &mut buffer)?;\n1127\t    self.admin_session_revoked_total.clone()\n1138\t    }\n1139\t}\n1140\t\n1141\t/// Generate a short request ID from UUIDv7.\n1142\t///\n1143\t/// UUIDv7 provides time-ordered unique IDs. We take the first 8 hex characters,\n1144\t/// hash them, and encode as hex for a short, URL-safe identifier.\n1145\tpub fn generate_request_id() -> String {\n1146\t    let uuid = Uuid::now_v7();\n1147\t    let uuid_str = uuid.simple().to_string();\n1148\t    // Take first 8 chars (enough entropy for uniqueness)\n1149\t    let prefix = &uuid_str[..8];\n1150\t\n1151\t    // Hash to get a consistent short representation\n1152\t    let mut hasher = DefaultHasher::new();\n1153\t    prefix.hash(&mut hasher);\n1154\t    let hash = hasher.finish();\n1155\t\n1156\t    // Encode as hex (16 chars = 64 bits)\n1157\t    format!(\"{:016x}\", hash)\n1158\t}\n1159\t\n1160\t/// Extension trait to add request ID extraction utilities.\n1161\tpub trait RequestIdExt {\n1162\t    fn get_request_id(&self) -> Option<String>;\n1163\t    fn set_request_id(&mut self, id: &str);\n1164\t}\n1165\t\n1166\timpl RequestIdExt for HeaderMap {\n1167\t    fn get_request_id(&self) -> Option<String> {\n1168\t        self.get(\"x-request-id\")\n1169\t            .and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok())\n1170\t            .map(|s| s.to_string())\n1171\t    }\n1172\t\n1173\t    fn set_request_id(&mut self, id: &str) {\n1174\t        if let Ok(val) = HeaderValue::from_str(id) {\n1175\t            self.insert(\"x-request-id\", val);\n1176\t        }\n1177\t    }\n1178\t}\n1179\t\n1180\t/// Guard that decrements the in-flight gauge when dropped.\n1181\t///\n1182\t/// This ensures that even if the handler panics, the in-flight count\n1183\t/// is accurately decremented.\n1184\tstruct InFlightGuard {\n1185\t    metrics: Metrics,\n1186\t    request_id: String,\n1187\t}\n1188\t\n1189\timpl InFlightGuard {\n1190\t    fn new(metrics: Metrics, request_id: String) -> Self {\n1191\t        metrics.requests_in_flight.inc();\n1192\t        tracing::trace!(\n1193\t            request_id = %request_id,\n1194\t            requests_in_flight = metrics.requests_in_flight.get(),\n1195\t            \"request started\"\n1196\t        );\n1197\t        Self { metrics, request_id }\n1198\t    }\n1199\t}\n1200\t\n1201\timpl Drop for InFlightGuard {\n1202\t    fn drop(&mut self) {\n1203\t        self.metrics.requests_in_flight.dec();\n1204\t        tracing::trace!(\n1205\t            request_id = %self.request_id,\n1206\t            requests_in_flight = self.metrics.requests_in_flight.get(),\n1207\t            \"request completed\"\n1208\t        );\n1209\t    }\n1210\t}\n1211\t\n1212\t/// Extract the path template from the matched route.\n1213\t///\n1214\t/// Axum's MatchedPath extractor provides the route template (e.g., \"/indexes/{uid}/search\")\n1215\t/// instead of the actual path (e.g., \"/indexes/products/search\"), avoiding high-cardinality labels.\n1216\tfn extract_path_template(request: &Request) -> String {\n1217\t    request\n1218\t        .extensions()\n1219\t        .get::<axum::extract::MatchedPath>()\n1220\t        .map(|mp| mp.as_str())\n1221\t        .unwrap_or_else(|| request.uri().path())\n1222\t        .to_string()\n1223\t}\n1224\t\n1225\t/// Main middleware that combines request ID injection, structured logging, and Prometheus metrics.\n1226\t///\n1227\t/// IMPORTANT: This middleware must be applied AFTER request_id_middleware in the layer stack\n1228\t/// (i.e., its layer() call must come BEFORE request_id_middleware's layer() call).\n1229\t/// This ensures the request_id header is already set when this middleware runs.\n1230\tpub async fn telemetry_middleware(\n1231\t    State(telemetry): State<TelemetryState>,\n1232\t    mut req: Request,\n1233\t    next: Next,\n1234\t) -> Response {\n1235\t    let start = Instant::now();\n1236\t    let method = req.method().clone();\n1237\t    let path_template = extract_path_template(&req);\n1238\t    let metrics = telemetry.metrics.clone();\n1239\t    let pod_id = telemetry.pod_id.clone();\n1240\t\n1241\t    // Extract request ID from header (set by request_id_middleware)\n1242\t    // The header must already exist because request_id_middleware runs first.\n1243\t    let request_id = req\n1244\t        .headers()\n1245\t        .get_request_id()\n1246\t        .expect(\"request_id header must be set by request_id_middleware\");\n1247\t    req.headers_mut().set_request_id(&request_id);\n1248\t\n1249\t    // Create span for structured logging with pod_id included.\n1250\t    // Note: raw path is intentionally omitted to avoid logging index names\n1251\t    // (which may contain customer identifiers). Use path_template instead.\n1252\t    let span = info_span!(\n1253\t        \"request\",\n1254\t        request_id = %request_id,\n1255\t        pod_id = %pod_id,\n1256\t        method = %method,\n1257\t        path_template = %path_template,\n1258\t    );\n1259\t\n1260\t    let _guard = span.enter();\n1261\t\n1262\t    // Track in-flight requests\n1263\t    let in_flight = InFlightGuard::new(metrics.clone(), request_id.clone());\n1264\t\n1265\t    let response = next.run(req).await;\n1266\t\n1267\t    drop(in_flight);\n1268\t\n1269\t    let status = response.status();\n1270\t    let status_u16 = status.as_u16();\n1271\t    let duration = start.elapsed();\n1272\t\n1273\t    // Record Prometheus metrics\n1274\t    metrics\n1275\t        .request_duration\n1276\t        .with_label_values(&[method.as_str(), &path_template, &status_u16.to_string()])\n1277\t        .observe(duration.as_secs_f64());\n1278\t    metrics\n1279\t        .requests_total\n1280\t        .with_label_values(&[method.as_str(), &path_template, &status_u16.to_string()])\n1281\t        .inc();\n1282\t\n1283\t    // Structured log entry (plan §10 shape)\n1284\t    // Base fields: timestamp (from tracing-subscriber), level, message, duration_ms\n1285\t    // Additional fields (index, node_count, estimated_hits, degraded)\n1286\t    // are added by request handlers via the tracing span.\n1287\t    let message = format!(\"{} {}\", method, status);\n1288\t    if status.is_server_error() {\n1289\t        tracing::error!(\n1290\t            target: \"miroir.request\",\n1291\t            pod_id = %pod_id,\n1292\t            request_id = %request_id,\n1293\t            message = %message,\n1294\t            duration_ms = duration.as_millis() as u64,\n1295\t            status = status_u16,\n1296\t            method = %method,\n1297\t            path_template = %path_template,\n1298\t        );\n1299\t    } else if status.is_client_error() {\n1300\t        tracing::warn!(\n1301\t            target: \"miroir.request\",\n1302\t            pod_id = %pod_id,\n1303\t            request_id = %request_id,\n1304\t            message = %message,\n1305\t            duration_ms = duration.as_millis() as u64,\n1306\t            status = status_u16,\n1307\t            method = %method,\n1308\t            path_template = %path_template,\n1309\t        );\n1310\t    } else {\n1311\t        tracing::info!(\n1312\t            target: \"miroir.request\",\n1313\t            pod_id = %pod_id,\n1314\t            request_id = %request_id,\n1315\t            message = %message,\n1316\t            duration_ms = duration.as_millis() as u64,\n1317\t            status = status_u16,\n1318\t            method = %method,\n1319\t            path_template = %path_template,\n1320\t        );\n1321\t    }\n1322\t\n1323\t    // Ensure request ID is in response headers\n1324\t    let mut response = response;\n1325\t    if !response.headers().contains_key(\"x-request-id\") {\n1326\t        if let Ok(val) = HeaderValue::from_str(&request_id) {\n1327\t            response.headers_mut().insert(\"x-request-id\", val);\n1328\t        }\n1329\t    }\n1330\t\n1331\t    response\n1332\t}\n1333\t\n1334\t/// Create the metrics router for the :9090 server.\n1335\tpub fn metrics_router() -> Router<Metrics> {\n1336\t    Router::new().route(\"/metrics\", get(metrics_handler))\n1337\t}\n1338\t\n1339\t/// Handler that returns Prometheus metrics in text exposition format.\n1340\tasync fn metrics_handler(State(metrics): State<Metrics>) -> Response {\n1341\t    let body = match metrics.encode_metrics() {\n1342\t        Ok(metrics) => metrics,\n1343\t        Err(e) => {\n1344\t            tracing::error!(error = %e, \"failed to encode metrics\");\n1345\t            format!(\"# ERROR: failed to encode metrics: {}\\n\", e)\n1346\t        }\n1347\t    };\n1348\t\n1349\t    let mut response = Response::new(axum::body::Body::from(body));\n1350\t    response.headers_mut().insert(\n1351\t        \"content-type\",\n1352\t        HeaderValue::from_static(\"text/plain; version=0.0.4; charset=utf-8\"),\n1353\t    );\n1354\t    response\n1355\t}\n1356\t\n1357\t/// Accessor methods for metrics that can be used by other parts of the application.\n1358\timpl Metrics {\n1359\t    // ── Request metrics ──\n1360\t\n1361\t    pub fn record_request_duration(&self, method: &str, path_template: &str, status: u16, duration_secs: f64) {\n1362\t }\n1852\t}\n1853\t\n1854\t#[cfg(test)]\n1855\tmod tests {\n1856\t    use super::*;\n1857\t\n1858\t    #[test]\n1859\t    fn test_request_id_generation() {\n1860\t        // Generate multiple IDs to verify format\n1861\t        for _ in 0..10 {\n1862\t            let id = generate_request_id();\n1863\t\n1864\t            // IDs should be 16 hex chars (64-bit hash)\n1865\t            assert_eq!(id.len(), 16);\n1866\t\n1867\t            // IDs should be hexadecimal\n1868\t            assert!(id.chars().all(|c| c.is_ascii_hexdigit()));\n1869\t        }\n1870\t\n1871\t        // Test that different UUID prefixes produce different IDs\n1872\t        let id1 = generate_request_id();\n1873\t        std::thread::sleep(std::time::Duration::from_millis(5));\n1874\t        let id2 = generate_request_id();\n1875\t        // In production, time ensures uniqueness; test just verifies format above\n1876\t        assert_eq!(id1.len(), 16);\n1877\t        assert_eq!(id2.len(), 16);\n1878\t    }\n1879\t\n1880\t    #[test]\n1881\t    fn test_metrics_creation() {\n1882\t        // Default config has all §13 features enabled\n1883\t        let metrics = Metrics::new(&MiroirConfig::default());\n1884\t\n1885\t        // Add some sample data to ensure metrics show up in output\n1886\t        metrics.request_duration.with_label_values(&[\"GET\", Structured logging, request IDs, and Prometheus metrics middleware.\n\nuse std::time::Instant;\n\nuse async_trait::async_trait;\nuse axum::{\n    extract::{FromRequestParts, Request, State},\n    http::{HeaderMap, HeaderValue, StatusCode},\n    middleware::Next,\n    response::{Response, IntoResponse},\n    routing::get,\n    Router,\n    Extension,\n};\nuse axum::http::request::Parts;\n\nuse miroir_core::config::MiroirConfig;\nuse prometheus::{\n    Counter, CounterVec, Encoder, Gauge, GaugeVec, Histogram, HistogramOpts, HistogramVec, Opts,\n    Registry, TextEncoder,\n};\nuse tracing::info_span;\nuse uuid::Uuid;\nuse hex;\nuse std::collections::hash_map::DefaultHasher;\nuse std::hash::{Hash, Hasher};\n\n/// Request ID wrapper type for storing in axum Request extensions.\n///\n/// This is a newtype wrapper around the 8-character hex request ID,\n/// allowing handlers to extract it via `Request.extensions().get::<RequestId>()`.\n#[derive(Clone, Debug, PartialEq, Eq, Hash)]\npub struct RequestId(pub String);\n\nimpl RequestId {\n    /// Create a new RequestId from a UUIDv7.\n    ///\n    /// Hashes the full UUIDv7 to produce an 8-character hex ID that is unique\n    /// even for consecutive calls within the same millisecond.\n    pub fn new() -> Self {\n        let uuid = Uuid::now_v7();\n        let bytes = uuid.as_bytes();\n        // Hash the full UUID to ensure uniqueness even within the same millisecond\n        let mut hasher = DefaultHasher::new();\n        hasher.write(bytes);\n        let hash = hasher.finish();\n        // Take first 8 hex chars of 64-bit hash (32 bits is sufficient entropy)\n        Self(format!(\"{:08x}\", hash as u32))\n    }\n\n    /// Get the inner request ID string.\n    pub fn as_str(&self) -> &str {\n        &self.0\n    }\n\n    /// Parse a RequestId from a string.\n    pub fn parse(s: String) -> Option<Self> {\n        if s.len() == 8 && s.chars().all(|c| c.is_ascii_hexdigit()) {\n            Some(Self(s))\n        } else {\n            None\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Session ID wrapper type for read-your-writes session pinning (plan §13.6).\n///\n/// Extracted from the `X-Miroir-Session` header and stored in request extensions.\n/// Handlers can access it via `Request.extensions().get::<SessionId>()`.\n#[derive(Clone, Debug, PartialEq, Eq, Hash)]\npub struct SessionId(pub String);\n\nimpl Default for SessionId {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self(String::new())\n    }\n}\n\nimpl SessionId {\n    /// Get the inner session ID string.\n    pub fn as_str(&self) -> &str {\n        &self.0\n    }\n\n    /// Parse a SessionId from a string.\n    ///\n    /// Accepts any non-empty string (client-provided UUID or identifier).\n    pub fn parse(s: String) -> Option<Self> {\n        if !s.is_empty() && s.len() <= 256 {\n            Some(Self(s))\n        } else {\n            None\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Optional session ID extractor.\n///\n/// This extractor safely handles cases where the session ID may not be present\n/// in the request extensions. It returns None instead of failing the request.\n#[derive(Clone, Debug)]\npub struct OptionalSessionId(pub Option<SessionId>);\n\n#[async_trait]\nimpl<S> FromRequestParts<S> for OptionalSessionId\nwhere\n    S: Send + Sync,\n{\n    type Rejection = std::convert::Infallible;\n\n    async fn from_request_parts(\n        parts: &mut Parts,\n        _state: &S,\n    ) -> Result<Self, Self::Rejection> {\n        Ok(OptionalSessionId(parts.extensions.get::<SessionId>().cloned()))\n    }\n}\n\npub async fn request_id_middleware(\n    mut req: Request,\n    next: Next,\n) -> Response {\n    // Check for existing request ID in headers\n    let request_id = req\n        .headers()\n        .get(\"x-request-id\")\n        .and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok())\n        .and_then(|s| RequestId::parse(s.to_string()))\n        .unwrap_or_else(RequestId::new);\n\n    // Store in request extensions for handler access\n    req.extensions_mut().insert(request_id.clone());\n\n    // Set X-Request-Id header on request (for telemetry_middleware to read)\n    if let Ok(val) = HeaderValue::from_str(request_id.as_str()) {\n        req.headers_mut().insert(\"x-request-id\", val);\n    }\n\n    // Process the request\n    let mut response = next.run(req).await;\n\n    // Add X-Request-Id header to response (override if exists)\n    if let Ok(val) = HeaderValue::from_str(request_id.as_str()) {\n        response.headers_mut().insert(\"x-request-id\", val);\n    }\n\n    response\n}\n\n/// Session pinning middleware (plan §13.6).\n///\n/// Extracts the `X-Miroir-Session` header and stores it in request extensions\n/// for handlers to access via `Request.extensions().get::<SessionId>()`.\npub async fn session_pinning_middleware(\n    mut req: Request,\n    next: Next,\n) -> Response {\n    // Extract session ID from header if present\n    let session_id = req\n        .headers()\n        .get(\"x-miroir-session\")\n        .and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok())\n        .and_then(|s| SessionId::parse(s.to_string()));\n\n    // Store in request extensions for handler access\n    if let Some(sid) = session_id {\n        req.extensions_mut().insert(sid);\n    }\n\n    next.run(req).await\n}\n\n\n/// Telemetry state combining metrics and pod_id for middleware.\n#[derive(Clone)]\npub struct TelemetryState {\n    pub metrics: Metrics,\n    pub pod_id: String,\n}\n\nimpl TelemetryState {\n    pub fn new(metrics: Metrics) -> Self {\n        let pod_id = std::env::var(\"POD_NAME\").unwrap_or_else(|_| \"unknown\".to_string());\n        Self { metrics, pod_id }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Global metrics registry shared across all middleware instances.\npub struct Metrics {\n    registry: Registry,\n\n    // ── Request metrics ──\n    request_duration: HistogramVec,\n    requests_total: CounterVec,\n    requests_in_flight: Gauge,\n\n    // ── Node health metrics ──\n    node_healthy: GaugeVec,\n    node_request_duration: HistogramVec,\n    node_errors: CounterVec,\n\n    // ── Shard metrics ──\n    shard_coverage: Gauge,\n    degraded_shards: Gauge,\n    shard_distribution: GaugeVec,\n\n    // ── Task metrics ──\n    task_processing_age: Histogram,\n    tasks_total: CounterVec,\n    task_registry_size: Gauge,\n\n    // ── Scatter-gather metrics rebalance_documents_migrated: Counter,\n    rebalance_duration: Histogram,\n\n    // ── §13.11 Multi-search metrics (feature-gated) ──\n    multisearch_queries_per_batch: Option<Histogram>,\n    multisearch_batches_total: Option<Counter>,\n    multisearch_partial_failures_total: Option<Counter>,\n    multisearch_tenant_session_pin_override_total: Option<CounterVec>,\n\n    // ── §13.12 Vector search metrics (feature-gated) ──\n    vector_search_over_fetched_total: Option<Counter>,\n    vector_merge_strategy: Option<CounterVec>,\n    vector_embedder_drift_total: Option<Counter>,\n\n    // ── §13.13 CDC metrics (feature-gated) ──\n    cdc_events_published_total: Option<CounterVec>,\n    cdc_lag_seconds: Option<GaugeVec>,\n    cdc_buffer_bytes: Option<GaugeVec>,\n    cdc_dropped_total: Option<CounterVec>,\n    cdc_events_suppressed_total: Option<CounterVec>,\n\n    // ── §13.14 TTL metrics (feature-gated) ──\n    ttl_documents_expired_total: Option<CounterVec>,\n    ttl_sweep_duration_seconds: Option<HistogramVec>,\n    ttl_pending_estimate: Option<GaugeVec>,\n\n    // ── §13.15 Tenant affinity metrics (feature-gated) ──\n    tenant_queries_total: canary_assertion_failures_total: Option<CounterVec>,\n\n    // ── §13.19 Admin UI metrics (feature-gated) ──\n    admin_ui_sessions_total: Option<Counter>,\n    admin_ui_action_total: Option<CounterVec>,\n    admin_ui_destructive_action_total: Option<CounterVec>,\n\n    // ── §13.20 Explain metrics (feature-gated) ──\n    explain_requests_total: Option<Counter>,\n    explain_warnings_total: Option<CounterVec>,\n    explain_execute_total: Option<Counter>,\n\n    // ── §13.21 Search UI metrics (feature-gated) ──\n    search_ui_sessions_total: Option<Counter>,\n    search_ui_queries_total: Option<CounterVec>,\n    search_ui_zero_hits_total: Option<CounterVec>,\n    search_ui_click_through_total: Option<CounterVec>,\n    search_ui_p95_ms: Option<GaugeVec>,\n\n    // ── §14.9 Resource-pressure metrics (always present) ──\n    memory_pressure: Gauge,\n    cpu_throttled_seconds_total: Counter,\n    request_queue_depth: Gauge,\n    background_queue_depth: GaugeVec,\n    peer_pod_count: Gauge,\n    leader: GaugeVec,\n    owned_shards_count: Gauge,\n\n    // ── Admin session sealing metrics (always present) ──\n    admin_session_key_generated: Gauge,\n    admin_session_revoked_total: Counter,\n\n    // ── §13.5 Two-phase settings broadcast metrics (always present) ──\n    settings_broadcast_phase: GaugeVec,\n    settings_hash_mismatch_total: Counter,\n    settings_drift_repair_total: CounterVec,\n    settings_version: GaugeVec,\n    settings_divergence_alert_total: Counter,\n    frozen_indexes: GaugeVec,\n\n    // ── §13.7 Alias metrics (always present) ──\n    alias_resolutions_total: CounterVec,\n    alias_flips_total: CounterVec,\n\n    // ── §13.6 Session pinning metrics (always present) ──\n    session_active_count: Gauge,\n    session_pin_enforced_total: CounterVec,\n    session_wait_duration_seconds: Histogram,\n    session_wait_timeout_total: CounterVec,\n\n    // ── §13.8 Anti-entropy metrics (always present) ──\n    antientropy_shards_scanned_total: Counter,\n    antientropy_mismatches_found_total: Counter,\n    antientropy_docs_repaired_total: Counter,\n    antientropy_last_scan_completed_seconds: Gauge,\n\n    // ── §13.3 Adaptive replica selection metrics (always present) ──\n    replica_selection_score: GaugeVec,\n    replica_selection_exploration_total: Counter,\n\n    // ── §13.10 Idempotency metrics (always present) ──\n    idempotency_hits_total: CounterVec,\n    idempotency_cache_size: Gauge,\n\n    // ── §13.10 Query coalescing metrics (always present) ──\n    query_coalesce_subscribers_total: Counter,\n    query_coalesce_hits_total: Counter,\n}\n\nimpl Clone for Metrics {\n    fn clone(&self) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            registry: self.registry.clone(),\n         explain_execute_total: self.explain_execute_total.clone(),\n            search_ui_sessions_total: self.search_ui_sessions_total.clone(),\n            search_ui_queries_total: self.background_queue_depth.clone(),\n            peer_pod_count: self.peer_pod_count.clone(),\n            leader: self.leader.clone(),\n            owned_shards_count: self.owned_shards_count.clone(),\n            admin_session_key_generated: self.admin_session_key_generated.clone(),\n            admin_session_revoked_total: self.admin_session_revoked_total.clone(),\n            settings_broadcast_phase: self.settings_broadcast_phase.clone(),\n            settings_hash_mismatch_total: self.settings_hash_mismatch_total.clone(),\n            settings_drift_repair_total: self.settings_drift_repair_total.clone(),\n            settings_version: self.settings_version.clone(),\n            settings_divergence_alert_total: self.settings_divergence_alert_total.clone(),\n            frozen_indexes: self.frozen_indexes.clone(),\n            alias_resolutions_total: self.alias_resolutions_total.clone(),\n            alias_flips_total: self.alias_flips_total.clone(),\n            session_active_count: self.session_active_count.clone(),\n self.replica_selection_exploration_total.clone(),\n            idempotency_hits_total: self.idempotency_hits_total.clone(),\n            idempotency_cache_size: self.idempotency_cache_size.clone(),\n            query_coalesce_subscribers_total: self.query_coalesce_subscribers_total.clone(),\n            query_coalesce_hits_total: self.query_coalesce_hits_total.clone(),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl Default for Metrics {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self::new(&MiroirConfig::default())\n    }\n}\n\nimpl Metrics {\n    pub fn new(config: &MiroirConfig) -> Self {\n        let registry = Registry::new();\n\n        // ── Request metrics ──\n        let request_duration = HistogramVec::new(\n            HistogramOpts::new(\"miroir_request_duration_seconds\", \"Request latency in seconds\")\n                .buckets(vec![0.005, 0.01, 0.025, 0.05, 0.1, 0.25, 0.5, 1.0, 2.5, 5.0, 10.0]),\n            &[\"method\", \"path_template\", \"status\"],\n        )\n        .expect(\"failed to create request_duration histogram\");\n\n        let requests_total = CounterVec::new(\n            Opts::new(\"miroir_requests_total\", \"Total number of requests\"),\n            &[\"method\", \"path_template\", \"status\"],\n        )\n        .expect(\"failed to create requests_total counter\");\n\n        let requests_in_flight = Gauge::with_opts(\n            Opts::new(\"miroir_requests_in_flight\", \"Number of requests currently being processed\"),\n        )\n        .expect(\"failed to create requests_in_flight gauge\");\n\n        // ── Node health metrics ──\n        let node_healthy = GaugeVec::new(\n            Opts::new(\"miroir_node_healthy\", \"Health status of backend nodes (1=healthy, 0=unhealthy)\"),\n            &[\"node_id\"],\n        )\n        .expect(\"failed to create node_healthy gauge\");\n\n        let node_request_duration = HistogramVec::new(\n            HistogramOpts::new(\"miroir_node_request_duration_seconds\", \"Latency of individual node requests\")\n                .buckets(vec![0.001, 0.005, 0.01, 0.025, 0.05, 0.1, 0.5, 1.0]),\n            &[\"node_id\", \"operation\"],\n        )\n        .expect(\"failed to create node_request_duration histogram\");\n\n        let node_errors = CounterVec::new(\n            Opts::new(\"miroir_node_errors_total\", \"Number of errors from backend nodes\"),\n            &[\"node_id\", \"error_type\"],\n        )\n        .expect(\"failed to create node_errors counter\");\n\n        // ── Shard metrics ──\n        let shard_coverage = Gauge::with_opts(\n            Opts::new(\"miroir_shard_coverage\", \"Fraction of shards with at least one healthy replica\"),\n        )\n        .expect(\"failed to create shard_coverage gauge\");\n\n        let degraded_shards = Gauge::with_opts(\n            Opts::new(\"miroir_degraded_shards_total\", \"Number of shards with reduced replica availability\"),\n        )\n        .expect(\"failed to create degraded_shards gauge\");\n\n        let shard_distribution = GaugeVec::new(\n            Opts::new(\"miroir_shard_distribution\", \"Number of shards assigned to each node\"),\n            &[\"node_id\"],\n        )\n        .expect(\"failed to create shard_distribution gauge\");\n\n        // ── Task metrics ──\n        let task_processing_age = Histogram::with_opts(\n            HistogramOpts::new(\"miroir_task_processing_age_seconds\", \"Time between task creation and processing start\")\n                .buckets(vec![0.01, 0.05, 0.1, 0.25, 0.5, 1.0, 2.5, 5.0, 10.0, 30.0, 60.0]),\n        )\n        .expect(\"failed to create task_processing_age histogram\");\n\n        let tasks_total = CounterVec::new(\n            Opts::new(\"miroir_tasks_total\", \"Total number of tasks by status\"),\n            &[\"status\"],\n        )\n        .expect(\"failed to create tasks_total counter\");\n\n        let task_registry_size = Gauge::with_opts(\n            Opts::new(\"miroir_task_registry_size\", \"Current number of tasks in the registry\"),\n        )\n        .expect(\"failed to create task_registry_size gauge\");\n\n        // ── Scatter-gather metrics ──\n        let scatter_fan_out_size = Histogram::with_opts(\n            HistogramOpts::new(\"miroir_scatter_fan_out_size\", \"Number of nodes in scatter operations\")\n                .buckets(vec![1.0, 2.0, 3.0, 5.0, 10.0, 20.0, 50.0]),\n        )\n        .expect(\"failed to create scatter_fan_out_size histogram\");\n\n        let scatter_partial_responses = Counter::with_opts(\n            Opts::new(\"miroir_scatter_partial_responses_total\", \"Number of scatter responses that were partial (some nodes failed)\"),\n        )\n        .expect(\"failed to create scatter_partial_responses counter\");\n\n        let scatter_retries = Counter::with_opts(\n            Opts::new(\"miroir_scatter_retries_total\", \"Number of scatter retry attempts due to node failures\"),\n        )\n        .expect(\"failed to create scatter_retries counter\");\n\n        // ── Rebalancer metrics ──\n        let rebalance_in_progress = Gauge::with_opts(\n            Opts::new(\"miroir_rebalance_in_progress\", \"Whether a rebalance is currently running (1=yes, 0=no)\"),\n        )\n        .expect(\"failed to create rebalance_in_progress gauge\");\n\n        let rebalance_documents_migrated = Counter::with_opts(\n            Opts::new(\"miroir_rebalance_documents_migrated_total\", \"Total number of documents migrated during rebalance\"),\n        )\n        .expect(\"failed to create rebalance_documents_migrated counter\");\n\n        let rebalance_duration = Histogram::with_opts(\n            HistogramOpts::new(\"miroir_rebalance_duration_seconds\", \"Duration of rebalance operations\")\n                .buckets(vec![1.0, 5.0, 10.0, 30.0, 60.0, 300.0, 600.0, 1800.0, 3600.0]),\n        )\n        .expect(\"failed to create rebalance_duration histogram\");\n\n        // Register all metrics\n        macro_rules! reg {\n            ($m:expr) => {\n                registry.register(Box::new($m.clone())).expect(concat!(\"failed to register \", stringify!($m)));\n            };\n        }\n\n        reg!(request_duration);\n        reg!(requests_total);\n        reg!(requests_in_flight);\n        reg!(node_healthy);\n        reg!(node_request_duration);\n        reg!(node_errors);\n        reg!(shard_coverage);\n        reg!(degraded_shards);\n        reg!(shard_distribution);\n        reg!(task_processing_age);\n        reg!(tasks_total);\n        reg!(task_registry_size);\n        reg!(scatter_fan_out_size);\n        reg!(scatter_partial_responses);\n        reg!(scatter_retries);\n        reg!(rebalance_in_progress);\n        reg!(rebalance_documents_migrated);\n        reg!(rebalance_duration);\n\n        // ── §13.11 Multi-search metrics (cardinality cap: top 100 tenants, rest bucketed) ──\n        let (\n            multisearch_queries_per_batch,\n            multisearch_batches_total,\n            multisearch_partial_failures_total,\n            multisearch_tenant_session_pin_override_total,\n        ) = if config.multi_search.enabled {\n            let q = Histogram::with_opts(\n                HistogramOpts::new(\"miroir_multisearch_queries_per_batch\", \"Number of queries in each multi-search batch\")\n                    .buckets(vec![1.0, 2.0, 5.0, 10.0, 25.0, 50.0, 100.0]),\n            ).expect(\"create multisearch_queries_per_batch\");\n            let b = Counter::with_opts(\n                Opts::new(\"miroir_multisearch_batches_total\", \"Total number of multi-search batches processed\"),\n            ).expect(\"create multisearch_batches_total\");\n            let p = Counter::with_opts(\n                Opts::new(\"miroir_multisearch_partial_failures_total\", \"Number of multi-search batches with at least one query failure\"),\n            ).expect(\"create multisearch_partial_failures_total\");\n            let t = CounterVec::new(\n                Opts::new(\"miroir_tenant_session_pin_override_total\", \"Session pin overrides triggered by multi-search tenant routing\"),\n                &[\"tenant\"],\n            ).expect(\"create multisearch_tenant_session_pin_override_total\");\n            reg!(q); reg!(b); reg!(p); reg!(t);\n            (Some(q), Some(b), Some(p), Some(t))\n        } else {\n            (None, None, None, None)\n        };\n\n        // ── §13.12 Vector search metrics ──\n        let (\n            vector_search_over_fetched_total,\n            vector_merge_strategy,\n            vector_embedder_drift_total,\n        ) = if config.vector_search.enabled {\n            let o = Counter::with_opts(\n                Opts::new(\"miroir_vector_search_over_fetched_total\", \"Number of vector searches that over-fetched candidates\"),\n            ).expect(\"create vector_search_over_fetched_total\");\n            let m = CounterVec::new(\n d = Counter::with_opts(\n                Opts::new(\"miroir_vector_embedder_drift_total\", \"Number of embedder drift detections\"),\n            ).expect(\"create vector_embedder_drift_total\");\n            reg!(o); reg!(m); reg!(d);\n            (Some(o), Some(m), Some(d))\n        } else {\n            (None, None, None)\n        };\n\n        // ── §13.13 CDC metrics (cardinality cap: top 100 sinks, rest bucketed) ──\n        let (\n            cdc_events_published_total,\n            cdc_lag_seconds,\n            cdc_buffer_bytes,\n            cdc_dropped_total,\n            cdc_events_suppressed_total,\n        ) = if config.cdc.enabled {\n            let e = CounterVec::new(\n                Opts::new(\"miroir_cdc_events_published_total\", \"Total CDC events      Opts::new(\"miroir_cdc_buffer_bytes\", \"CDC buffer size in bytes\"),\n                &[\"sink\"],\n            ).expect(\"create cdc_buffer_bytes\");\n            let d = CounterVec::new(\n                Opts::new(\"miroir_cdc_dropped_total\", \"CDC events dropped due to buffer overflow\"),\n                &[\"sink\"],\n            ).expect(\"create cdc_dropped_total\");\n            let s cdc_events_suppressed_total\");\n            reg!(e); reg!(l); reg!(b); reg!(d); reg!(s);\n            (Some(e), Some(l), Some(b), Some(d), Some(s))\n        } else {\n            (None, None, None, None, None)\n        };\n\n        // ── §13.14 TTL metrics (cardinality cap: top 100 indexes, rest bucketed) ──\n        let (\n            ttl_documents_expired_total,\n
