      }\n    }\n\n    pub fn encode_metrics(&self) -> Result<String, prometheus::Error> {\n        let encoder = TextEncoder::new();\n        let metric_families = self.registry.gather();\n        let mut buffer = Vec::new();\n        encoder.encode(&metric_families, &mut buffer)?;\n        Ok(String::from_utf8(buffer).map_err(|e| {\n            prometheus::Error::Msg(format!(\"failed to convert metrics to UTF-8: {}\", e))\n        })?)\n    }\n\n    pub fn admin_session_key_generated(&self) -> Gauge {\n        self.admin_session_key_generated.clone()\n    }\n\n    pub fn admin_session_revoked_total(&self) -> Counter {\n        self.admin_session_revoked_total.clone()\n    }\n}\n\n/// Generate a short request ID from UUIDv7.\n///\n/// UUIDv7 provides time-ordered unique IDs. We take the first 8 hex characters,\n/// hash them, and encode as hex for a short, URL-safe identifier.\npub fn generate_request_id() -> String {\n    let uuid = Uuid::now_v7();\n    let uuid_str = uuid.simple().to_string();\n    // Take first 8 chars (enough entropy for uniqueness)\n    let prefix = &uuid_str[..8];\n\n    // Hash to get a consistent short representation\n    let mut hasher = DefaultHasher::new();\n    prefix.hash(&mut hasher);\n    let hash = hasher.finish();\n\n    // Encode as hex (16 chars = 64 bits)\n    format!(\"{:016x}\", hash)\n}\n\n/// Extension trait to add request ID extraction utilities.\npub trait RequestIdExt {\n    fn get_request_id(&self) -> Option<String>;\n    fn set_request_id(&mut self, id: &str);\n}\n\nimpl RequestIdExt for HeaderMap {\n    fn get_request_id(&self) -> Option<String> {\n        self.get(\"x-request-id\")\n            .and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok())\n            .map(|s| s.to_string())\n    }\n\n    fn set_request_id(&mut self, id: &str) {\n        if let Ok(val) = HeaderValue::from_str(id) {\n            self.insert(\"x-request-id\", val);\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Guard that decrements the in-flight gauge when dropped.\n///\n/// This ensures that even if the handler panics, the in-flight count\n/// is accurately decremented.\nstruct InFlightGuard {\n    metrics: Metrics,\n    request_id: String,\n}\n\nimpl InFlightGuard {\n    fn new(metrics: Metrics, request_id: String) -> Self {\n        metrics.requests_in_flight.inc();\n        tracing::trace!(\n            request_id = %request_id,\n            requests_in_flight = metrics.requests_in_flight.get(),\n            \"request started\"\n        );\n        Self { metrics, request_id }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl Drop for InFlightGuard {\n    fn drop(&mut self) {\n        self.metrics.requests_in_flight.dec();\n        tracing::trace!(\n            request_id = %self.request_id,\n            requests_in_flight = self.metrics.requests_in_flight.get(),\n            \"request completed\"\n        );\n    }\n}\n\n/// Extract the path template from the matched route.\n///\n/// Axum's MatchedPath extractor provides the route template (e.g., \"/indexes/{uid}/search\")\n/// instead of the actual path (e.g., \"/indexes/products/search\"), avoiding high-cardinality labels.\nfn extract_path_template(request: &Request) -> String {\n    request\n        .extensions()\n        .get::<axum::extract::MatchedPath>()\n        .map(|mp| mp.as_str())\n        .unwrap_or_else(|| request.uri().path())\n        .to_string()\n}\n\n/// Main middleware that combines request ID injection, structured logging, and Prometheus metrics.\n///\n/// IMPORTANT: This middleware must be applied AFTER request_id_middleware in the layer stack\n/// (i.e., its layer() call must come BEFORE request_id_middleware's layer() call).\n/// This ensures the request_id header is already set when this middleware runs.\npub async fn telemetry_middleware(\n    State(telemetry): State<TelemetryState>,\n    mut req: Request,\n    next: Next,\n) -> Response {\n    let start = Instant::now();\n    let method = req.method().clone();\n    let path_template = extract_path_template(&req);\n    let metrics = telemetry.metrics.clone();\n    let pod_id = telemetry.pod_id.clone();\n\n    // Extract request ID from header (set by request_id_middleware)\n    // The header must already exist because request_id_middleware runs first.\n    let request_id = req\n        .headers()\n        .get_request_id()\n        .expect(\"request_id header must be set by request_id_middleware\");\n    req.headers_mut().set_request_id(&request_id);\n\n    // Create span for structured logging with pod_id included.\n    // Note: raw path is intentionally omitted to avoid logging index names\n    // (which may contain customer identifiers). Use path_template instead.\n    let span = info_span!(\n        \"request\",\n        request_id = %request_id,\n        pod_id = %pod_id,\n        method = %method,\n        path_template = %path_template,\n    );\n\n    let _guard = span.enter();\n\n    // Track in-flight requests\n    let in_flight = InFlightGuard::new(metrics.clone(), request_id.clone());\n\n    let response = next.run(req).await;\n\n    drop(in_flight);\n\n    let status = response.status();\n    let status_u16 = status.as_u16();\n    let duration = start.elapsed();\n\n    // Record Prometheus metrics\n    metrics\n        .request_duration\n        .with_label_values(&[method.as_str(), &path_template, &status_u16.to_string()])\n        .observe(duration.as_secs_f64());\n    metrics\n        .requests_total\n        .with_label_values(&[method.as_str(), &path_template, &status_u16.to_string()])\n        .inc();\n\n    // Structured log entry (plan §10 shape)\n    // Base fields: timestamp (from tracing-subscriber), level, message, duration_ms\n    // Additional fields (index, node_count, estimated_hits, degraded)\n    // are added by request handlers via the tracing span.\n    let message = format!(\"{} {}\", method, status);\n    if status.is_server_error() {\n        tracing::error!(\n            target: \"miroir.request\",\n            pod_id = %pod_id,\n            request_id = %request_id,\n            message = %message,\n            duration_ms = duration.as_millis() as u64,\n            status = status_u16,\n            method = %method,\n            path_template = %path_template,\n        );\n    } else if status.is_client_error() {\n        tracing::warn!(\n            target: \"miroir.request\",\n            pod_id = %pod_id,\n            request_id = %request_id,\n            message = %message,\n            duration_ms = duration.as_millis() as u64,\n            status = status_u16,\n            method = %method,\n            path_template = %path_template,\n        );\n    } else {\n        tracing::info!(\n            target: \"miroir.request\",\n            pod_id = %pod_id,\n            request_id = %request_id,\n            message = %message,\n            duration_ms = duration.as_millis() as u64,\n            status = status_u16,\n            method = %method,\n            path_template = %path_template,\n        );\n    }\n\n    // Ensure request ID is in response headers\n    let mut response = response;\n    if !response.headers().contains_key(\"x-request-id\") {\n        if let Ok(val) = HeaderValue::from_str(&request_id) {\n            response.headers_mut().insert(\"x-request-id\", val);\n        }\n    }\n\n    response\n}\n\n/// Create the metrics router for the :9090 server.\npub fn metrics_router() -> Router<Metrics> {\n    Router::new().route(\"/metrics\", get(metrics_handler))\n}\n\n/// Handler that returns Prometheus metrics in text exposition format.\nasync fn metrics_handler(State(metrics): State<Metrics>) -> Response {\n    let body = match metrics.encode_metrics() {\n        Ok(metrics) => metrics,\n        Err(e) => {\n            tracing::error!(error = %e, \"failed to encode metrics\");\n            format!(\"# ERROR: failed to encode metrics: {}\\n\", e)\n        }\n    };\n\n    let mut response = Response::new(axum::body::Body::from(body));\n    response.headers_mut().insert(\n        \"content-type\",\n        HeaderValue::from_static(\"text/plain; version=0.0.4; charset=utf-8\"),\n    );\n    response\n}\n\n/// Accessor methods for metrics that can be used by other parts of the application.\nimpl Metrics {\n    // ── Request metrics ──\n\n    pub fn record_request_duration(&self, method: &str, path_template: &str, status: u16, duration_secs: f64) {\n        self.request_duration.with_label_values(&[method, path_template, &status.to_string()]).observe(duration_secs);\n    }\n\n    pub fn inc_requests_total(&self, method: &str, path_template: &str, status: u16) {\n        self.requests_total.with_label_values(&[method, path_template, &status.to_string()]).inc();\n    }\n\n    // ── Scatter-gather ──\n\n    pub fn record_scatter_fan_out(&self, size: u64) {\n        self.scatter_fan_out_size.observe(size as f64);\n    }\n\n    pub fn inc_scatter_partial_responses(&self) {\n        self.scatter_partial_responses.inc();\n    }\n\n    pub fn inc_scatter_retries(&self) {\n        self.scatter_retries.inc();\n    }\n\n    // ── Node health ──\n\n    pub fn set_node_healthy(&self,  // ── §13.10 Query coalescing metrics ──\n\n    pub fn inc_query_coalesce_subscribers(&self, count: u64) {\n        self.query_coalesce_subscribers_total.inc_by(count as f64);\n    }\n\n    pub fn #[test]\n    fn test_request_id_format() {\n        let id = RequestId::new();\n        // RequestId should be exactly 8 hex characters\n        assert_eq!(id.as_str().len(), 8);\n        assert!(id.as_str().chars().all(|c| c.is_ascii_hexdigit()));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_request_id_parse_valid() {\n        // Valid 8-char hex string\n        let valid = \"abcd1234\";\n        let parsed = RequestId::parse(valid.to_string());\n        assert!(parsed.is_some());\n        assert_eq!(parsed.unwrap().as_str(), valid);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_request_id_parse_invalid_wrong_length() {\n        // Wrong length (too short)\n        assert!(RequestId::parse(\"abc123\".to_string()).is_none());\n        // Wrong length (too long)\n        assert!(RequestId::parse(\"abcd12345678\".to_string()).is_none());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_request_id_parse_invalid_non_hex() {\n        // Contains non-hex characters\n      // Generate two consecutive IDs - they should be different\n        // due to UUIDv7's timestamp component\n        let id1 = RequestId::new();\n        std::thread::sleep(std::time::Duration::from_millis(5));\n        let id2 = RequestId::new();\n\n        assert_ne!(id1, id2);\n        assert_ne!(id1.as_str(), id2.as_str());\n    }\n\n    // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n    // Integration tests for request_id_middleware\n    // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_request_id_middleware_adds_header() {\n        use axum::{routing::get, Router};\n        use http_body_util::Full;\n        use tower::ServiceExt;\n\n        // Build a simple router with the request ID middleware\n        let app = Router::new()\n            .route(\"/test\", get(|| async { \"OK\" }))\n            .layer(axum::middleware::from_fn(request_id_middleware));\n\n        // Create a test request\n        let request = Request::builder()\n            .uri(\"/test\")\n            .body(Full::default())\n            .unwrap();\n\n        // Send the request\n        let response = app.oneshot(request).await.unwrap();\n\n        // Verify X-Request-Id header is present\n        let header = response\n            .headers()\n            .get(\"x-request-id\")\n            .expect(\"X-Request-Id header should be present\");\n        let header_value = header.to_str().unwrap();\n\n        // Verify it's 8 hex characters\n        assert_eq!(\n            header_value.len(),\n            8,\n            \"X-Request-Id should be 8 characters\"\n        );\n        assert!(\n            header_value.chars().all(|c| c.is_ascii_hexdigit()),\n            \"X-Request-Id should be hexadecimal\"\n        );\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_request_id_middleware_unique_per_request() {\n        use axum::{routing::get, Router};\n        use http_body_util::Full;\n        use tower::ServiceExt;\n\n        // Build a simple router with the request ID middleware\n        let app = Router::new()\n            .route(\"/test\", get(|| async { \"OK\" }))\n            .layer(axum::middleware::from_fn(request_id_middleware));\n\n        // Create two identical requests\n        let request1 = Request::builder()\n            .uri(\"/test\")\n            .body(Full::default())\n            .unwrap();\n\n        let request2 = Request::builder()\n            .uri(\"/test\")\n            .body(Full::default())\n            .unwrap();\n\n        // Send both requests\n        let response1 = app.clone().oneshot(request1).await.unwrap();\n        let response2 = app.oneshot(request2).await.unwrap();\n\n        // Extract headers\n        let id1 = response1\n            .headers()\n            .get(\"x-request-id\")\n            .unwrap()\n            .to_str()\n            .unwrap();\n        let id2 = response2\n            .headers()\n            .get(\"x-request-id\")\n            .unwrap()\n            .to_str()\n            .unwrap();\n\n        // Verify IDs are different (UUIDv7 timestamp ensures this)\n        assert_ne!(\n            id1, id2,\n            \"Two consecutive requests should have different request IDs\"\n        );\n\n        // Both should still be valid 8-char hex\n        assert_eq!(id1.len(), 8);\n        assert_eq!(id2.len(), 8);\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_request_id_middleware_preserves_existing_header() {\n        use axum::{routing::get, Router};\n        use http_body_util::Full;\n        use tower::ServiceExt;\n\n        // Build a simple router with the request ID middleware\n        let app = Router::new()\n            .route(\"/test\", get(|| async { \"OK\" }))\n            .layer(axum::middleware::from_fn(request_id_middleware));\n\n        // Create a request with a pre-existing X-Request-Id header\n        let existing_id = \"deadbeef\";\n        let request = Request::builder()\n            .uri(\"/test\")\n            .header(\"x-request-id\", existing_id)\n            .body(Full::default())\n            .unwrap();\n\n        // Send the request\n        let response = app.oneshot(request).await.unwrap();\n\n        // Verify the header is preserved\n        let header = response\n            .headers()\n            .get(\"x-request-id\")\n            .expect(\"X-Request-Id header should be present\");\n        let header_value = header.to_str().unwrap();\n\n        assert_eq!(\n            header_value, existing_id,\n            \"Existing X-Request-Id should be preserved\"\n        );\n    }\n}\n","numLines":2333,"startLine":1,"totalLines":2333}}}

Integration Tests\n\nThis directory contains integration tests for Miroir that run against a real Docker Compose stack.\n\n## Prerequisites\n\nStart the Docker Compose development stack:\n\n```bash\ndocker compose -f examples/docker-compose-dev.yml up -d\n```\n\nWait for all containers to be healthy (may take 30-60 seconds):\n\n```bash\ndocker compose -f examples/docker-compose-dev.yml ps\n```\n\n## Running Tests\n\nRun all integration tests:\n\n```bash\ncargo test -p miroir-proxy --test docker_compose_integration -- --test-threads=1\n```\n\nRun a specific test:\n\n```bash\ncargo test -p miroir-proxy --test docker_compose_integration test_document_round_trip -- --exact\n```\n\nRun tests with output:\n\n```bash\ncargo test -p miroir-proxy --test docker_compose_integration -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture\n```\n\n## Test Coverage\n\n| Test | Description | AC Reference |\n|------|-------------|--------------|\n| `test_document_round_trip` | Index and retrieve 1000 documents | §8.1 |\n| `test_search_shard_coverage` | Verify search hits all 16 shards | §8.2 |\n| `test_facet_aggregation` | Verify facet counts sum correctly | §8.3 |\n| `test_offset_limit_paging` | Test pagination with offset/limit | §8.4 |\n| `test_settings_broadcast` | Verify settings propagate to all nodes | §8.5 |\n| `test_task_polling` | Test task status polling | §8.6 |\n| `test_health_check` | Verify health endpoint responds | §8.7 |\n| `test_direct_meilisearch_access` | Verify direct node access for debugging | §8.7 |\n| `test_node_failure_rf2` | Test graceful degradation with node failure | §8.8 |\n\n## RF=2 / High Availability Tests\n\nThe `test_node_failure_rf2` test requires the RF=2 docker-compose stack:\n\n```bash\n# Start the RF=2 stack (6 Meilisearch nodes, RF=2, RG=2)\ndocker compose -f examples/docker-compose-dev-rf2.yml up -d\n\n# Run the node failure test\ncargo test -p miroir-proxy --test docker_compose_integration test_node_failure_rf2 -- --ignored\n\n# Clean up\ndocker compose -f examples/docker-compose-dev-rf2.yml down -v\n```\n\nThe node failure test:\n1. Indexes documents with RF=2 (each document replicated to both replica groups)\n2. Stops a Meilisearch node mid-test (`docker stop miroir-meili-1`)\n3. Verifies that searches still work using remaining replicas\n4. Restarts the node and verifies recovery\n\n## Cleanup\n\nStop and remove containers:\n\n```bash\ndocker compose -f examples/docker-compose-dev.yml down -v\n```\n\nFor RF=2 tests:\n\n```bash\ndocker compose -f examples/docker-compose-dev-rf2.yml down -v\n```\n\n## Troubleshooting\n\n### Containers not starting\n\nCheck container status:\n\n```bash\ndocker compose -f examples/docker-compose-dev.yml ps\n```\n\nCheck logs:\n\n```bash\ndocker compose -f examples/docker-compose-dev.yml logs miroir\ndocker compose -f examples/docker-compose-dev.yml logs meili-0\n```\n\n### Port conflicts\n\nIf ports 7700-7703 are already in use, modify the port mappings in `examples/docker-compose-dev.yml`.\n\nFor RF=2 tests, ensure ports 7710-7716 and 6379 are available.\n\n### Tests failing with connection refused\n\nEnsure the Docker Compose stack is running:\n\n```bash\ndocker compose -f examples/docker-compose-dev.yml ps\n```\n\nWait for all containers to be healthy before running tests.\n\n### Node failure test not working\n\nThe RF=2 test requires:\n1. The RF=2 docker-compose stack to be running\n2. Sufficient memory for 6 Meilisearch containers + Redis\n3. The `docker` command to be accessible from the test environment\n\nRun with `--ignored` flag:\n```bash\ncargo test --test integration test_node_failure_rf2 -- --ignored\n```\n","numLines":132,"startLine":1,"totalLines":132}}}

Scatter orchestration: fan-out logic and covering set builder.\n2\t\n3\tuse crate::config::UnavailableShardPolicy;\n4\tuse crate::replica_selection::ReplicaSelector;\n5\tuse tracing::{instrument, std::collections::HashMap;\n13\t\n14\t/// Scatter plan: the exact shard→node mapping for a search query.\n15\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n16\tpub struct ScatterPlan {\n17\t    pub chosen_group: u32,\n18\t    pub target_shards: Vec<u32>,\n19\t    pub shard_to_node: HashMap<u32, NodeId>,\n20\t    pub deadline_ms: u32,\n21\t    pub hedging_eligible: bool,\n22\t}\n23\t\n24\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n25\t// §15 OP#4: Global-IDF preflight (dfs_query_then_fetch pattern)\n26\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n27\t\n28\t/// Per-term document frequency from a single shard.\n29\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n30\tpub struct TermStats {\n31\t    pub df: u64,\n32\t}\n33\t\n34\t/// Preflight request: gather term-frequency statistics from a shard.\n35\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n36\tpub struct PreflightRequest {\n37\t    pub index_uid: String,\n38\t    pub terms: Vec<String>,\n39\t    pub filter: Option<Value>,\n40\t}\n41\t\n42\t/// Response from a shard's preflight query.\n43\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n44\tpub struct PreflightResponse {\n45\t    pub total_docs: u64,\n46\t    pub avg_doc_length: f64,\n47\t    pub term_stats: HashMap<String, TermStats>,\n48\t}\n49\t\n50\t/// Aggregated global term statistics after coordinator aggregation.\n51\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n52\tpub struct GlobalTermStats {\n53\t    pub df: u64,\n54\t    pub idf: f64,\n55\t}\n56\t\n57\t/// Aggregated global IDF data computed at the coordinator.\n58\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n59\tpub struct GlobalIdf {\n60\t    pub total_docs: u64,\n61\t    pub avg_doc_length: f64,\n62\t    pub terms: HashMap<String, GlobalTermStats>,\n63\t}\n64\t\n65\timpl GlobalIdf {\n66\t    /// Aggregate per-shard preflight responses into global IDF.\n67\t    pub fn from_preflight_responses(responses: &[PreflightResponse]) -> Self {\n68\t        let mut total_docs = 0u64;\n69\t        let mut total_length = 0.0f64;\n70\t        let mut term_df: HashMap<String, u64> = HashMap::new();\n71\t\n72\t        for resp in responses {\n73\t            total_docs += resp.total_docs;\n74\t            total_length += resp.avg_doc_length * resp.total_docs as f64;\n75\t            for (term, stats) in &resp.term_stats {\n76\t                *term_df.entry(term.clone()).or_insert(0) += stats.df;\n77\t            }\n78\t        }\n79\t\n80\t        let avg_doc_length = if total_docs > 0 {\n81\t            total_length / total_docs as f64\n82\t        } else {\n83\t            0.0\n84\t        };\n85\t\n86\t        let n = total_docs as f64;\n87\t        let terms = term_df\n88\t            .into_iter()\n89\t            .map(|(term, df)| {\n90\t                let idf = if df == 0 {\n91\t                    0.0\n92\t                } else {\n93\t                    ((n - df as f64 + 0.5) / (df as f64 + 0.5)).ln() + 1.0\n94\t                };\n95\t                (term, GlobalTermStats { df, idf })\n96\t            })\n97\t            .collect();\n98\t\n99\t        Self { total_docs, avg_doc_length, terms }\n100\t    }\n101\t}\n102\t\n103\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n104\t// NodeClient trait\n105\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n106\t\n107\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n108\t// Write path: document operations (P2.2)\n109\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n110\t\n111\t/// Request to add/replace documents on a node.\n112\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n113\tpub struct WriteRequest {\n114\t    pub index_uid: String,\n115\t    pub documents: Vec<Value>,\n116\t    pub primary_key: Option<String>,\n117\t    /// Internal origin tag for CDC event suppression (plan §13.13).\n118\t    /// - None = client write (always emitted to CDC)\n119\t    /// - Some(\"antientropy\") = anti-entropy repair write (suppressed unless emit_internal_writes)\n120\t    /// - Some(\"reshard_backfill\") = reshard backfill write (suppressed unless emit_internal_writes)\n121\t    /// - Some(\"rollover\") = ILM rollover write (suppressed unless emit_internal_writes)\n122\t    /// - Some(\"ttl_expire\") = TTL expiration delete (suppressed unless emit_ttl_deletes)\n123\t    ///\n124\t    /// This field is NEVER stored on documents and never returned to clients.\n125\t    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n126\t    pub origin: Option<String>,\n127\t}\n128\t\n129\t/// Response from a single node's document write operation.\n130\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n131\tpub struct WriteResponse {\n132\t    pub success: bool,\n133\t    pub task_uid: Option<u64>,\n134\t    pub message: ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/// Per-term document frequency from a single shard.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct TermStats {\n    pub df: u64,\n}\n\n/// Preflight request: gather term-frequency statistics from a shard.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct PreflightRequest {\n    pub index_uid: String,\n    pub terms: Vec<String>,\n    pub filter: Option<Value>,\n}\n\n/// Response from a shard's preflight query.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct PreflightResponse {\n    pub total_docs: u64,\n    pub avg_doc_length: f64,\n    pub term_stats: HashMap<String, TermStats>,\n}\n\n/// Aggregated global term statistics after coordinator aggregation.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct GlobalTermStats {\n    pub df: u64,\n    pub idf: f64,\n}\n\n/// Aggregated global IDF data computed at the coordinator.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct GlobalIdf {\n    pub total_docs: u64,\n    pub avg_doc_length: f64,\n    pub terms: HashMap<String, GlobalTermStats>,\n}\n\nimpl GlobalIdf {\n    /// Aggregate per-shard preflight responses into global IDF.\n    pub fn from_preflight_responses(responses: &[PreflightResponse]) -> Self {\n        let mut total_docs = 0u64;\n        let mut total_length = 0.0f64;\n        let mut term_df: HashMap<String, u64> = HashMap::new();\n\n        for resp in responses {\n            total_docs += resp.total_docs;\n            total_length += resp.avg_doc_length * resp.total_docs as f64;\n            for (term, stats) in &resp.term_stats {\n                *term_df.entry(term.clone()).or_insert(0) += stats.df;\n            }\n        }\n\n        let avg_doc_length = if total_docs > 0 {\n            total_length / total_docs as f64\n        } else {\n            0.0\n        };\n\n        let n = total_docs as f64;\n        let terms = term_df\n            .into_iter()\n            .map(|(term, df)| {\n                let idf = if df == 0 {\n                    0.0\n                } else {\n                    ((n - df as f64 + 0.5) / (df as f64 + 0.5)).ln() + 1.0\n                };\n                (term, GlobalTermStats { df, idf })\n            })\n            .collect();\n\n        Self { total_docs, avg_doc_length, terms }\n    }\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// NodeClient trait\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Write path: document operations (P2.2)\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/// Request to add/replace documents on a node.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct WriteRequest {\n    pub index_uid: String,\n    pub documents: Vec<Value>,\n    pub primary_key: Option<String>,\n    /// Internal origin tag for CDC event suppression (plan §13.13).\n    /// - None = client write (always emitted to CDC)\n    /// - Some(\"antientropy\") = anti-entropy repair write (suppressed unless emit_internal_writes)\n    /// - Some(\"reshard_backfill\") = reshard backfill write (suppressed unless emit_internal_writes)\n    /// - Some(\"rollover\") = ILM rollover write (suppressed unless emit_internal_writes)\n    /// - Some(\"ttl_expire\") = TTL expiration delete (suppressed unless emit_ttl_deletes)\n    ///\n    /// This field is NEVER stored on documents and never returned to clients.\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    pub origin: Option<String>,\n}\n\n/// Response from a single node's document write operation.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct WriteResponse {\n    pub success: bool,\n    pub #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    pub origin: Option<String>,\n}\n\n/// Request to delete all documents matching a filter.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub   }\n255\t    }\n256\t\n257\t    let colors = merged.get(\"color\").unwrap();\n258\t    assert_eq!(*colors.get(\"red\").unwrap(), 17, \"red: 10+7=17\");\n259\t    assert_eq!(*colors.get(\"green\").unwrap(), 17, \"green: 5+12=17\");\n260\t    assert_eq!(*colors.get(\"blue\").unwrap(), 11, \"blue: 3+8=11\");\n261\t}\n262\t\n263\t//  index_uid: \"test\".to_string(),\n305\t        query: Some(\"test\".to_string()),\n306\t        offset: 0,\n307\t        limit: 5,\n308\t        filter: None,\n309\t        facets: None,\n310\t        ranking_score: true,\n311\t        body: json!({}),\n312\t        global_idf: None,\n313\t    };\n314\t    let result1 = dfs_query_then_fetch_search(\n315\t        plan1, &mock, req1, &topo,\n316\t        miroir_core::config::UnavailableShardPolicy::Partial, &strategy,\n317\t    ).await.unwrap();\n318\t\n319\t    // Page 2: offset=5, limit=5 (different query_seq to get different covering set)\n320\t    let plan2 = plan_search_scatter(&topo, 1, 3, shards);\n321\t    // Re-use same mock responses since the node set is the same for this topology\n322\t    let req2 = SearchRequest {\n323\t        index_uid: \"test\".to_string(),\n324\t        query: Some(\"test\".to_string()),\n325\t        offset: 5,\n326\t        limit: 5,\n327\t        filter: None,\n328\t        facets: None,\n329\t        ranking_score: true,\n330\t        body: json!({}),\n331\t        global_idf: None,\n332\t    };\n333\t    let result2 = dfs_query_then_fetch_search(\n334\t        plan2, &mock, req2, &topo,\n335\t        miroir_core::config::UnavailableShardPolicy::Partial, &strategy,\n336\t    ).await.unwrap();\n337\t\n338\t    // Pages should not overlap\n339\t    let page1_ids: std::collections::HashSet<String> = result1.hits.iter()\n340\t        .filter_map(|h| h[\"id\"].as_str().map(|s| s.to_string()))\n341\t        .collect();\n342\t    let page2_ids: std::collections::HashSet<String> = result2.hits.iter()\n343\t        .filter_map(|h| h[\"id\"].as_str().map(|s| s.to_string()))\n344\t        .collect();\n345\t\n346\t    let overlap: std::collections::HashSet<_> = page1_ids.intersection(&page2_ids).collect();\n347\t    assert!(overlap.is_empty(), \"pages should not overlap, but found: {:?}\", overlap);\n348\t\n349\t    // Combined hits\");\n352\t\n353\t    // Verify global ordering: page 1 scores >= page 2 scores\n354\t    let page1_max_score = result1.hits.last()\n355\t        .and_then(|h| h[\"_rankingScore\"].as_f64())\n356\t        .unwrap_or(0.0);\n357\t    let page2_min_score = result2.hits.first()\n358\t        .and_then(|h| h[\"_rankingScore\"].as_f64())\n359\t        .unwrap_or(1.0);\n360\t    assert!(\n361\t        page1_max_score >= page2_min_score,\n362\t        \"page 1 min score ({}) should be >= page 2 max score ({})\",\n363\t        page1_max_score, page2_min_score\n364\t    );\n365\t}\n366\t\n367\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n368\t// DoD 5: write with one group completely down still succeeds + X-Miroir-Degraded\n369\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n370\t\n371\t#[test]\n372\tfn test_degraded_write_one_group_down() {\n373\t    let shards = 4u32;\n374\t    let mut topo = two_group_topology(shards);\n375\t\n376\t    // Take down all nodes in group 1\n377\t    for i in 2..4u32 {\n378\t        topo.node_mut(&NodeId::new(format!(\"node-{i}\")))\n379\t            .unwrap()\n380\t            .transition_to(NodeStatus::Failed)\n381\t            .unwrap();\n382\t    }\n383\t\n384\t    // Verify group 1 nodes are not healthy\n385\t    let node_map = topo.node_map();\n386\t    for group in topo.groups() {\n387\t        if group.id == 1 {\n388\t            let healthy = one group can still accept writes\n394\t    for shard_id in 0..shards {\n395\t        let targets = write_targets(shard_id, &topo);\n396\t        let group0_targets: Vec<_> = targets.iter()\n397\t            .filter(|node_id| {\n398\t                topo.node(node_id).map(|n| n.replica_group == 0).unwrap_or(false)\n399\t            })\n400\t            .collect();\n401\t        assert!(!group0_targets.is_empty(), \"shard {} should have group 0 targets\", shard_id);\n402\t    }\n403\t}\n404\t\n405\t#[test]\n406\tfn test_quorum_logic_group_down() {\n407\t    // Simulate: 2 groups, RF=2. Group 1 is completely down.\n408\t    // Quorum per group = floor(2/2) + 1 = 2.\n409\t    // Group 0: 2 of 2 nodes succeed → quorum met.\n410\t    // Group 1: 0 of 2 nodes succeed → quorum not met.\n411\t    // Overall: at least one group met quorum → write succeeds, degraded header.\n412\t\n413\t    let rf = 2usize;\n414\t    let replica_group_count = 2u32;\n415\t    let quorum_per_group = (rf / 2) + 1; // = 2\n416\t\n417\t    // Group 0: 2 ACKs\n418\t    let group0_acks = 2usize;\n419\t    assert!(group0_acks >= quorum_per_group, \"group 0 met quorum\");\n420\t\n421\t    // Group 1: 0 ACKs\n422\t    let group1_acks = 0usize;\n423\t    assert!(group1_acks < quorum_per_group, \"group 1 missed quorum\");\n424\t\n425\t    // At least one group met quorum → write should succeed\n426\t    let quorum_groups = (group0_acks >= quorum_per_group) as usize\n427\t        + (group1_acks >= quorum_per_group) as usize;\n428\t    assert!(quorum_groups >= 1, \"at least one group met quorum\");\n429\t\n430\t    // Degraded header should be set because group 1 missed quorum\n431\t    let degraded_groups = replica_group_count - quorum_groups as u32;\n432\t    assert_eq!(degraded_groups, 1, \"one group is degraded\");\n433\t}\n434\t\n435\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n436\t// DoD 6: error-format parity with Meilisearch\n437\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n438\t\n439\t#[test]\n440\tfn test_error_shape_byte_for_byte_parity() {\n441\t    // Every MiroirCode Phase 2 DoD integration tests.\n//!\n//! Tests covering all Definition of Done criteria:\n//! - 1000 documents indexed across 3 nodes, each retrievable by ID\n//! - Unique-keyword search finds every doc exactly once\n//! - Facet aggregation across 3 color values sums correctly\n//! - Offset/limit paging preserves global ordering\n//! - Write with one group completely down still succeeds + X-Miroir-Degraded\n//! - Error-format parity: every error matches Meilisearch shape\n//! - GET /_miroir/topology matches plan §10 shape\n\nuse miroir_core::api_error::{ErrorType, MeilisearchError, MiroirCode};\nuse miroir_core::merger::ScoreMergeStrategy;\nuse miroir_core::router::{shard_for_key, write_targets};\nuse miroir_core::scatter::{\n    MockNodeClient, SearchRequest,\n    dfs_query_then_fetch_search, plan_search_scatter,\n};\nuse miroir_core::topology::{Node, NodeId, NodeStatus, Topology};\nuse serde_json::{json, Value};\nuse std::collections::HashMap;\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Helper: build a 3-node, 1 replica group topology\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nfn three_node_topology(shards: u32) -> Topology {\n    let mut topo = Topology::new(shards, 1, 3);\n    for i in 0..3u32 {\n        let node = Node::new(\n            NodeId::new(format!(\"node-{i}\")),\n            format!(\"http://localhost:810{i}\"),\n            0,\n        );\n        topo.add_node(node);\n        topo.node_mut(&NodeId::new(format!(\"node-{i}\")))\n            .unwrap()\n            .transition_to(NodeStatus::Active)\n            .unwrap();\n    }\n    topo\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Helper: build a 2-group, 2-node-per-group topology (4 nodes)\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nfn two_group_topology(shards: u32) -> Topology {\n    let mut topo = Topology::new(shards, 2, 2);\n    for i in 0..2u32 {\n        let node = Node::new(\n            NodeId::new(format!(\"node-{i}\")),\n            format!(\"http://localhost:810{i}\"),\n            0,\n        );\n        topo.add_node(node);\n        topo.node_mut(&NodeId::new(format!(\"node-{i}\")))\n            .unwrap()\n            .transition_to(NodeStatus::Active)\n            .unwrap();\n    }\n    for i in 2..4u32 {\n        let node = Node::new(\n            NodeId::new(format!(\"node-{i}\")),\n            format!(\"http://localhost:810{i}\"),\n            1,\n        );\n        topo.add_node(node);\n        topo.node_mut(&NodeId::new(format!(\"node-{i}\")))\n            .unwrap()\n            .transition_to(NodeStatus::Active)\n            .unwrap();\n    }\n    topo\n}\n\n// All MiroirCode variants for iteration\nconst ALL_CODES: [MiroirCode; 10] = [\n    MiroirCode::PrimaryKeyRequired,\n    MiroirCode::NoQuorum,\n    MiroirCode::ShardUnavailable,\n    MiroirCode::ReservedField,\n    MiroirCode::IdempotencyKeyReused,\n    MiroirCode::SettingsVersionStale,\n    MiroirCode::MultiAliasNotWritable,\n    MiroirCode::JwtInvalid,\n    MiroirCode::JwtScopeDenied,\n    MiroirCode::InvalidAuth,\n];\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// DoD 1: 1000 documents indexed across 3 nodes, each retrievable by ID\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n#[test]\nfn test_1000_docs_shard_assignment_coverage() {\n    let shards = 8u32;\n    let topo = three_node_topology(shards);\n\n    let mut shard_counts: HashMap<u32, Vec<String>> = HashMap::new();\n    for i in 0..1000u32 {\n        let pk = format!(\"doc-{i}\");\n        let shard = shard_for_key(&pk, shards);\n        shard_counts.entry(shard).or_default().push(pk);\n    }\n\n    // Every shard should have at least one document\n    assert_eq!(shard_counts.len(), shards as usize, \"all shards should receive documents\");\n\n    // All write targets should be reachable\n    for shard_id in 0..shards {\n        let targets = write_targets(shard_id, &topo);\n        assert_eq!(targets.len(), 3, \"RF=3 means 3 write targets per shard\");\n        for node_id in &targets {\n            assert!(topo.node(node_id).is_some(), \"target node {} should exist\", node_id);\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Total count should be 1000\n    let total: usize = shard_counts.values().map(|v| v.len()).sum();\n    assert_eq!(total, 1000);\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// DoD 2: unique-keyword search finds every doc exactly once\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn test_unique_keyword_search_deduplication() {\n    let shards = 4u32;\n    let topo = two_group_topology(shards);\n\n    let mut mock = MockNodeClient::default();\n\n    // Compute covering set for query_seq=0\n    let plan = plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, 2, shards);\n\n    // Build per-node responses by accumulating all docs for each node.\n    // Multiple shards may map to the same node; a real Meilisearch node\n    // returns all its matching docs in a single response.\n    let mut node_hits: HashMap<NodeId, Vec<Value>> = HashMap::new();\n    let mut node_totals: HashMap<NodeId, u64> = HashMap::new();\n\n    for (shard_id, node_id) in &plan.shard_to_node {\n        let doc1 = json!({\n            \"id\": format!(\"doc-{}-a\", shard_id),\n            \"title\": format!(\"unique-keyword-{}\", shard_id * 2),\n            \"_miroir_shard\": shard_id,\n            \"_rankingScore\": 0.95,\n        });\n        let doc2 = json!({\n            \"id\": format!(\"doc-{}-b\", shard_id),\n            \"title\": format!(\"unique-keyword-{}\", shard_id * 2 + 1),\n            \"_miroir_shard\": shard_id,\n            \"_rankingScore\": 0.90,\n        });\n\n        let hits = node_hits.entry(node_id.clone()).or_default();\n        hits.push(doc1);\n        hits.push(doc2);\n        *node_totals.entry(node_id.clone()).or_insert(0) += 2;\n\n        mock.preflight_responses.insert(node_id.clone(), miroir_core::scatter::PreflightResponse {\n            total_docs: 100,\n            avg_doc_length: 500.0,\n            term_stats: HashMap::new(),\n        });\n    }\n\n    // Now build one response per node with all its accumulated docs\n    for (node_id, hits) in node_hits {\n        let total = node_totals.remove(&node_id).unwrap();\n        mock.responses.insert(node_id, json!({\n            \"hits\": hits,\n            \"estimatedTotalHits\": total,\n            \"processingTimeMs\": 5,\n        }));\n    }\n\n    let strategy = ScoreMergeStrategy::new();\n    let req = SearchRequest {\n        index_uid: \"test-index\".to_string(),\n        query: Some(\"unique-keyword\".to_string()),\n        offset: 0,\n        limit: 100,\n        filter: None,\n        facets: None,\n        ranking_score: false,\n        body: json!({}),\n        global_idf: None,\n    };\n\n    let result = dfs_query_then_fetch_search(\n        plan,\n        &mock,\n        req,\n        &topo,\n        miroir_core::config::UnavailableShardPolicy::Partial,\n        &strategy,\n    )\n    .await\n    .unwrap();\n\n    // Every document should appear exactly once (no duplicates)\n    let mut seen_ids: HashMap<String, usize> = HashMap::new();\n    for hit in &result.hits {\n        let id = hit[\"id\"].as_str().unwrap().to_string();\n        *seen_ids.entry(id).or_insert(0) += 1;\n    }\n\n    for (id, count) in &seen_ids {\n        assert_eq!(*count, 1, \"doc {} appeared {} times, expected 1\", id, count);\n    }\n\n    // Should find all 8 docs (2 per shard × 4 shards)\n    assert_eq!(result.hits.len(), 8, \"expected 8 unique hits across 4 shards\");\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// DoD 3: facet aggregation across 3 color values sums correctly\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n#[test]\nfn test_facet_aggregation_sums_correctly() {\n    // Test the facet merge logic directly using the same algorithm the merger uses.\n    // Since merge_facets is private, we replicate the merge logic here to validate\n    // the aggregation contract.\n    use std::collections::BTreeMap;\n\n    // Shard 0: red=10, green=5, blue=3\n    let mut shard0: BTreeMap<String, BTreeMap<String, u64>> = BTreeMap::new();\n    let mut colors0: BTreeMap<String, u64> = BTreeMap::new();\n    colors0.insert(\"red\".to_string(), 10);\n    colors0.insert(\"green\".to_string(), 5);\n    colors0.insert(\"blue\".to_string(), 3);\n    shard0.insert(\"color\".to_string(), colors0);\n\n    // Shard 1: red=7, green=12, blue=8\n    let mut shard1: BTreeMap<String, BTreeMap<String, u64>> = BTreeMap::new();\n    let mut colors1: BTreeMap<String, u64> = BTreeMap::new();\n    colors1.insert(\"red\".to_string(), 7);\n    colors1.insert(\"green\".to_string(), 12);\n    colors1.insert(\"blue\".to_string(), 8);\n    shard1.insert(\"color\".to_string(), colors1);\n\n    // Merge: sum per-value facet counts across shards\n    let mut merged: BTreeMap<String, BTreeMap<String, u64>> = BTreeMap::new();\n    for shard_facets in &[&shard0, &shard1] {\n        for (facet_name, values) in *shard_facets {\n            let entry = merged.entry(facet_name.clone()).or_default();\n            for (value, count) in values {\n                *entry.entry(value.clone()).or_insert(0) += count;\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    let colors = merged.get(\"color\").unwrap();\n    assert_eq!(*colors.get(\"red\").unwrap(), 17, \"red: 10+7=17\");\n    assert_eq!(*colors.get(\"green\").unwrap(), 17, \"green: 5+12=17\");\n    assert_eq!(*colors.get(\"blue\").unwrap(), 11, \"blue: 3+8=11\");\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// DoD 4: offset/limit paging preserves global ordering\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn test_paging_preserves_global_ordering() {\n    let shards = 3u32;\n    let topo = three_node_topology(shards);\n\n    // Each shard returns 5 hits with descending scores\n    let mut mock = MockNodeClient::default();\n\n    // Build covering set for page 1\n    let plan1 = plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, 3, shards);\n\n    for (shard_id, node_id) in &plan1.shard_to_node {\n        let mut hits = Vec::new();\n        for i in 0..5u32 {\n            hits.push(json!({\n                \"id\": format!(\"s{}-d{}\", shard_id, i),\n                \"_miroir_shard\": shard_id,\n                \"_rankingScore\": 1.0 - (i as f64 * 0.1) - (*shard_id as f64 * 0.01),\n            }));\n        }\n        let response = json!({\n            \"hits\": hits,\n            \"estimatedTotalHits\": 5,\n            \"processingTimeMs\": 2,\n        });\n        mock.responses.insert(node_id.clone(), response);\n        mock.preflight_responses.insert(node_id.clone(), miroir_core::scatter::PreflightResponse {\n            total_docs: 50,\n            avg_doc_length: 500.0,\n            term_stats: HashMap::new(),\n        });\n    }\n\n    let strategy = ScoreMergeStrategy::new();\n\n    // Page 1: offset=0, limit=5\n    let req1 = SearchRequest {\n        index_uid: \"test\".to_string(),\n        query: Some(\"test\".to_string()),\n        offset: 0,\n        limit: 5,\n        filter: None,\n        facets: None,\n        ranking_score: true,\n        body: json!({}),\n        global_idf: None,\n    };\n    let result1 = dfs_query_then_fetch_search(\n        plan1, &mock, req1, &topo,\n        miroir_core::config::UnavailableShardPolicy::Partial, &strategy,\n    ).await.unwrap();\n\n    // Page 2: offset=5, limit=5 (different query_seq to get different covering set)\n    let plan2 = plan_search_scatter(&topo, 1, 3, shards);\n    // Re-use same mock responses since the node set is the same for this topology\n    let req2 = SearchRequest {\n        index_uid: \"test\".to_string(),\n        query: Some(\"test\".to_string()),\n        offset: 5,\n        limit: 5,\n        filter: None,\n        facets: None,\n        ranking_score: true,\n        body: json!({}),\n        global_idf: None,\n    };\n    let result2 = dfs_query_then_fetch_search(\n        plan2, &mock, req2, &topo,\n        miroir_core::config::UnavailableShardPolicy::Partial, &strategy,\n    ).await.unwrap();\n\n    // Pages should not overlap\n    let page1_ids: std::collections::HashSet<String> = result1.hits.iter()\n        .filter_map(|h| h[\"id\"].as_str().map(|s| s.to_string()))\n        .collect();\n    let page2_ids: std::collections::HashSet<String> = result2.hits.iter()\n        .filter_map(|h| h[\"id\"].as_str().map(|s| s.to_string()))\n        .collect();\n\n    let overlap: std::collections::HashSet<_> = page1_ids.intersection(&page2_ids).collect();\n    assert!(overlap.is_empty(), \"pages should not overlap, but found: {:?}\", overlap);\n\n    // Combined should have 10 hits total (5 per page)\n    assert_eq!(result1.hits.len(), 5, \"page 1 should have 5 hits\");\n    assert_eq!(result2.hits.len(), 5, \"page 2 should have 5 hits\");\n\n    // Verify global ordering: page 1 scores >= page 2 scores\n    let page1_max_score = result1.hits.last()\n        .and_then(|h| h[\"_rankingScore\"].as_f64())\n        .unwrap_or(0.0);\n    let page2_min_score = result2.hits.first()\n        .and_then(|h| h[\"_rankingScore\"].as_f64())\n        .unwrap_or(1.0);\n    assert!(\n        page1_max_score >= page2_min_score,\n        \"page 1 min score ({}) should be >= page 2 max score ({})\",\n        page1_max_score, page2_min_score\n    );\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// DoD 5: write with one group completely down still succeeds + X-Miroir-Degraded\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n#[test]\nfn test_degraded_write_one_group_down() {\n    let shards = 4u32;\n    let mut topo = two_group_topology(shards);\n\n    // Take down all nodes in group 1\n    for i in 2..4u32 {\n        topo.node_mut(&NodeId::new(format!(\"node-{i}\")))\n            .unwrap()\n            .transition_to(NodeStatus::Failed)\n            .unwrap();\n    }\n\n    // Verify group 1 nodes are not healthy\n    let node_map = topo.node_map();\n    for group in topo.groups() {\n        if group.id == 1 {\n            let healthy = group.healthy_nodes(&node_map);\n            assert!(healthy.is_empty(), \"group 1 should have no healthy nodes\");\n        }\n    }\n\n    // For each shard, verify at least one group can still accept writes\n    for shard_id in 0..shards {\n        let targets = write_targets(shard_id, &topo);\n        let group0_targets: Vec<_> = targets.iter()\n            .filter(|node_id| {\n                topo.node(node_id).map(|n| n.replica_group == 0).unwrap_or(false)\n            })\n            .collect();\n        assert!(!group0_targets.is_empty(), \"shard {} should have group 0 targets\", shard_id);\n    }\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_quorum_logic_group_down() {\n    // Simulate: 2 groups, RF=2. Group 1 is completely down.\n    // Quorum per group = floor(2/2) + 1 = 2.\n    // Group 0: 2 of 2 nodes succeed → quorum met.\n    // Group 1: 0 of 2 nodes succeed → quorum not met.\n    // Overall: at least one group met quorum → write succeeds, degraded header.\n\n    let rf = 2usize;\n    let replica_group_count = 2u32;\n    let quorum_per_group = (rf / 2) + 1; // = 2\n\n    // Group 0: 2 ACKs\n    let group0_acks = 2usize;\n    assert!(group0_acks >= quorum_per_group, \"group 0 met quorum\");\n\n    // Group 1: 0 ACKs\n    let group1_acks = 0usize;\n    assert!(group1_acks < quorum_per_group, \"group 1 missed quorum\");\n\n    // At least one group met quorum → write should succeed\n    let quorum_groups = (group0_acks >= quorum_per_group) as usize\n        + (group1_acks >= quorum_per_group) as usize;\n    assert!(quorum_groups >= 1, \"at least one group met quorum\");\n\n    // Degraded header should be set because group 1 missed quorum\n    let degraded_groups = replica_group_count - quorum_groups as u32;\n    assert_eq!(degraded_groups, 1, \"one group is degraded\");\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// DoD P2.2 Write path acceptance tests.\n//!\n//! Tests the complete write path implementation:\n//! - POST /indexes/{uid}/documents - add documents\n//! - PUT /indexes/{uid}/documents - replace documents\n//! - DELETE /indexes/{uid}/documents/{id} - delete by ID\n//! - DELETE /indexes/{uid}/documents - delete by IDs array or filter\n//!\n//! Acceptance criteria:\n//! 1. 1000 docs indexed via POST — every doc fetch-by-id returns the same doc\n//! 2. Docs distribute across all configured nodes (no node holds < 20% under RF=1/3-node)\n//! 3. Batch with one missing primary key → 400 miroir_primary_key_required, no docs written anywhere\n//! 4. Doc containing _miroir_shard → 400 miroir_reserved_field\n//! 5. RG=2, RF=1, 1 group down: write to 1 group succeeds with X-Miroir-Degraded: groups=1\n//! 6. RG=2, RF=1, both groups down: 503 miroir_no_quorum\n//! 7. DELETE by IDs array [docA, docB] with docA on shard 3, docB on shard 7 produces 2 independent per-shard delete calls\n\nuse miroir_core::config::MiroirConfig;\nuse miroir_core::config::NodeConfig;\nuse miroir_core::router::shard_for_key;\nuse serde_json::json;\nuse std::collections::HashMap;\n\nfn make_config(shards: u32, rf: u32, replica_groups: u32, node_addresses: Vec<String>) -> MiroirConfig {\n    let nodes: Vec<NodeConfig> = node_addresses\n        .into_iter()\n        .enumerate()\n        .map(|(i, addr)| NodeConfig {\n            id: format!(\"node-{i}\"),\n            address: addr,\n            replica_group: (i % replica_groups as usize) as u32,\n        })\n        .collect();\n\n    MiroirConfig {\n        master_key: \"test-master-key\".into(),\n        node_master_key: \"test-node-master-key\".into(),\n        shards,\n        replication_factor: rf,\n        replica_groups,\n        nodes,\n        ..Default::default()\n    }\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Acceptance 1: 1000 docs indexed via POST — every doc fetch-by-id returns the same doc\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n#[test]\nfn acceptance_1_1000_docs_indexed_retrievable() {\n    // Verify the orchestrator correctly distributes 1000 documents across shards\n    // and that each document can be retrieved from its assigned shard\n\n    // Track which documents each node receives\n    let mut node1_docs: Vec<serde_json::Value> = Vec::new();\n    let mut node2_docs: Vec<serde_json::Value> = Vec::new();\n    let mut node3_docs: Vec<serde_json::Value> = Vec::new();\n\n    // Create 1000 test documents\n    let documents: Vec<serde_json::Value> = (0..1000)\n        .map(|i| json!({\"id\": format!(\"doc-{i}\"), \"title\": format!(\"Document {i}\")}))\n        .collect();\n\n    // Simulate the orchestrator's shard grouping logic\n    let shard_count = 8u32;\n    let mut shard_documents: HashMap<u32, Vec<serde_json::Value>> = HashMap::new();\n\n    for doc in &documents {\n        let id = doc.get(\"id\").and_then(|v| v.as_str()).unwrap();\n        let shard_id = shard_for_key(id, shard_count);\n        shard_documents.entry(shard_id).or_default().push(doc.clone());\n    }\n\n    // Distribute shards across nodes (round-robin for RF=1)\n    let node_count = 3usize;\n    for (shard_id, docs) in shard_documents {\n        let node_idx = shard_id as usize % node_count;\n        match node_idx {\n            0 => node1_docs.extend(docs),\n            1 => node2_docs.extend(docs),\n            2 => node3_docs.extend(docs),\n            _ => unreachable!(),\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Verify all documents are distributed\n    let total: usize = node1_docs.len() + node2_docs.len() + node3_docs.len();\n    assert_eq!(total, 1000, \"all 1000 documents should be distributed\");\n\n    // Verify each document can be retrieved (simulated by checking presence in node_docs)\n    for doc in &documents {\n        let id = doc.get(\"id\").and_then(|v| v.as_str()).unwrap();\n        let shard_id = shard_for_key(id, shard_count);\n        let node_idx = shard_id as usize % 3;\n\n        let found = match node_idx {\n            0 => node1_docs.iter().any(|d| d.get(\"id\").and_then(|v| v.as_str()) == Some(id)),\n            1 => node2_docs.iter().any(|d| d.get(\"id\").and_then(|v| v.as_str()) == Some(id)),\n            2 => node3_docs.iter().any(|d| d.get(\"id\").and_then(|v| v.as_str()) == Some(id)),\n            _ => false,\n        };\n        assert!(found, \"document {} should be retrievable\", id);\n    }\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Acceptance 2: Docs distribute across all configured nodes (no node < 20%)\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn acceptance_2_distribution_across_all_nodes() {\n    // With 3 nodes, RF=1, each node should get roughly 33% of documents\n    // Minimum 20% means at least 200 docs out of 1000 per node\n\n    let shard_count = 8u32;\n    let node_count = 3usize;\n\n    // Create 1000 test documents\n    let documents: Vec<String> = (0..1000)\n        .map(|i| format!(\"doc-{i}\"))\n        .collect();\n\n    // Track distribution\n    let mut node_counts: HashMap<usize, usize> = HashMap::new();\n\n    for id in &documents {\n        let shard_id = shard_for_key(id, shard_count);\n        let node_idx = shard_id as usize % node_count;\n        *node_counts.entry(node_idx).or_insert(0) += 1;\n    }\n\n    // Verify each node has at least 20% of documents\n    let min_count = (1000.0 * 0.20) as usize;\n    for (node_idx, count) in &node_counts {\n        assert!(\n            *count >= min_count,\n            \"node {} has {} docs, expected at least {} (20%)\",\n            node_idx,\n            count,\n            min_count\n        );\n    }\n\n    // Verify total is 1000\n    let total: usize = node_counts.values().sum();\n    assert_eq!(total, 1000);\n\n    // With good distribution, each node should have roughly 33% ± 10%\n    for (node_idx, count) in &node_counts {\n        let percentage = (*count as f64 / 1000.0) * 100.0;\n        assert!(\n            percentage >= 20.0 && percentage <= 50.0,\n            \"node {} has {:.1}% of documents, expected roughly 33%\",\n            node_idx,\n            percentage\n        );\n    }\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Acceptance 3: Batch with one missing primary key → 400, no docs written\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n#[test]\nfn acceptance_3_batch_missing_primary_key_rejected() {\n    // Create a batch with one document missing the primary key\n    let documents = json!(\n        [\n            {\"id\": \"doc-1\", \"title\": \"Valid doc\"},\n            {\"title\": \"Missing ID\"},  // Missing primary key\n            {\"id\": \"doc-3\", \"title\": \"Another valid doc\"}\n        ]\n    );\n\n    // Simulate validation logic - validation fails before any writes\n    let primary_key = \"id\";\n    let has_missing = documents.as_array()\n        .unwrap()\n        .iter()\n        .any(|doc| doc.get(primary_key).is_none());\n\n    assert!(has_missing, \"batch should have a document missing primary key\");\n\n    // Verify error code would be miroir_primary_key_required\n    use miroir_core::api_error::MiroirCode;\n    let code = MiroirCode::PrimaryKeyRequired;\n    assert_eq!(code.as_str(), \"miroir_primary_key_required\");\n    assert_eq!(code.http_status(), 400);\n\n    // No writes should be attempted - validation happens first\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Acceptance 4: Doc containing _miroir_shard → 400 miroir_reserved_field\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n#[test]\nfn acceptance_4_reserved_field_rejection() {\n    // Document with _miroir_shard field\n    let documents = json!(\n        [\n            {\"id\": \"doc-1\", \"_miroir_shard\": 5, \"title\": \"Invalid doc\"}\n        ]\n    );\n\n    // Simulate validation logic - reserved field check happens before writes\n    let has_reserved = documents.as_array()\n        .unwrap()\n        .iter()\n        .any(|doc| doc.get(\"_miroir_shard\").is_some());\n\n    assert!(has_reserved, \"document should have reserved field _miroir_shard\");\n\n    // Verify error code would be miroir_reserved_field\n    use miroir_core::api_error::MiroirCode;\n    let code = MiroirCode::ReservedField;\n    assert_eq!(code.as_str(), \"miroir_reserved_field\");\n    assert_eq!(code.http_status(), 400);\n\n    // No writes should be attempted - reserved field validation happens first\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Acceptance 5: RG=2, RF=1, 1 group down → success with X-Miroir-Degraded: groups=1\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n#[test]\nfn acceptance_5_degraded_write_one_group_down() {\n    // Simulate quorum calculation with RG=2, RF=1\n    // Per-group quorum = floor(1/2) + 1 = 1 ACK needed per group\n    let _replica_groups = 2u32;\n    let rf = 1usize;\n    let quorum_per_group = (rf / 2) + 1;  // = 1\n\n    // Simulate group 0 ACKs, group 1 down\n    let mut group_acks: HashMap<u32, usize> = HashMap::new();\n    group_acks.insert(0, 1);  // Group 0 met quorum\n    // Group 1 has no ACKs (down)\n\n    // Count groups that met quorum\n    let quorum_groups = group_acks.values().filter(|&&acks| acks >= quorum_per_group).count();\n\n    // Verify: at least 1 group met quorum → write succeeds\n    assert_eq!(quorum_groups, 1, \"at least one group should meet quorum\");\n\n    // The key assertion: write succeeds when at least one group meets quorum\n    // X-Miroir-Degraded header would be set to indicate degraded state\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Acceptance 6: RG=2, RF=1, both groups down → 503 miroir_no_quorum\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n#[test]\nfn acceptance_6_no_quorum_both_groups_down() {\n    // Simulate quorum calculation with RG=2, RF=1\n    let _replica_groups = 2u32;\n    let rf = 1usize;\n    let quorum_per_group = (rf / 2) + 1;  // = 1\n\n    // No groups ACK (both down)\n    let group_acks: HashMap<u32, usize> = HashMap::new();\n\n    // miroir_no_quorum\n    assert_eq!(quorum_groups, 0, \"no groups should meet quorum\");\n\n    // Verify error code\n    use miroir_core::api_error::MiroirCode;\n    let code = MiroirCode::NoQuorum;\n DELETE by IDs array routes each ID to its shard independently\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n#[test]\nfn acceptance_7_delete_by_ids_independent_shard_routing() {\n    // Two documents on different shards\n    let doc_a_id = \"user:123\";  // Will route to some shard\n    let doc_b_id = \"product:456\";  // Will route to different shard\n\n    let shard_count = 8u32;\n    let shard_a = shard_for_key(doc_a_id, shard_count);\n    let shard_b = shard_for_key(doc_b_id, shard_count);\n\n    // Verify they're on different shards for this test\n    assert_ne!(shard_a, shard_b, \"test documents should be on different shards\");\n\n    // Simulate grouping IDs by shard (as done in delete_by_ids_impl)\n    let ids = vec![doc_a_id.to_string(), doc_b_id.to_string()];\n    let mut shard_ids: HashMap<u32, Vec<String>> = HashMap::new();\n\n    for id in &ids {\n        let shard_id = shard_for_key(id, shard_count);\n        shard_ids.entry(shard_id).or_default().push(id.clone());\n    }\n\n    // Verify each shard gets its own delete request\n    assert_eq!(shard_ids.len(), 2, \"should have 2 independent shard delete requests\");\n    assert!(shard_ids.contains_key(&shard_a), \"should have delete request for shard A\");\n    assert!(shard_ids.contains_key(&shard_b), \"should have delete request for shard B\");\n\n    // Verify each shard has the correct document\n    let docs_for_shard_a = shard_ids.get(&shard_a).unwrap();\n    let docs_for_shard_b = shard_ids.get(&shard_b).unwrap();\n    assert_eq!(docs_for_shard_a.len(), 1, \"shard A should have 1 document\");\n    assert_eq!(docs_for_shard_b.len(), 1, \"shard B should have 1 document\");\n    assert_eq!(docs_for_shard_a[0], doc_a_id, \"shard A should have doc A\");\n    assert_eq!(docs_for_shard_b[0], doc_b_id, \"shard B should have doc B\");\n}\n\n// assert!(shard < shard_count, \"shard ID should be within range\");\n    }\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_shard_distribution_evenness() {\n    let shard_count = 8u32;\n    let doc_count = 1000;\n\n    let mut Integration Tests\n\nThis directory contains integration tests for Miroir that run against a real Docker Compose stack.\n\n## Prerequisites\n\nStart the Docker Compose development stack:\n\n```bash\ndocker compose -f examples/docker-compose-dev.yml up -d\n```\n\nWait for all containers to be healthy (may take 30-60 seconds):\n\n```bash\ndocker compose -f examples/docker-compose-dev.yml ps\n```\n\n## Running Tests\n\nRun all integration tests:\n\n```bash\ncargo test -p miroir-proxy --test docker_compose_integration -- --test-threads=1\n```\n\nRun a specific test:\n\n```bash\ncargo test -p miroir-proxy --test docker_compose_integration test_document_round_trip -- --exact\n```\n\nRun tests with output:\n\n```bash\ncargo test -p miroir-proxy --test docker_compose_integration -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture\n```\n\n## Test Coverage\n\n| Test | Description | AC Reference |\n|------|-------------|--------------|\n| `test_document_round_trip` | Index and retrieve 1000 documents | §8.1 |\n| `test_search_shard_coverage` | Verify search hits all 16 shards | §8.2 |\n| `test_facet_aggregation` | Verify facet counts sum correctly | §8.3 |\n| `test_offset_limit_paging` | Test pagination with offset/limit | §8.4 |\n| `test_settings_broadcast` | Verify settings propagate to all nodes | §8.5 |\n| `test_task_polling` | Test task status polling | §8.6 |\n| `test_health_check` | Verify health endpoint responds | §8.7 |\n| `test_direct_meilisearch_access` | Verify direct node access for debugging | §8.7 |\n| `test_node_failure_rf2` | Test graceful degradation with node failure | §8.8 |\n\n## RF=2 / High Availability Tests\n\nThe `test_node_failure_rf2` test requires the RF=2 docker-compose stack:\n\n```bash\n# Start the RF=2 stack (6 Meilisearch nodes, RF=2, RG=2)\ndocker compose -f examples/docker-compose-dev-rf2.yml up -d\n\n# Run the node failure test\ncargo test -p miroir-proxy --test docker_compose_integration test_node_failure_rf2 -- --ignored\n\n# Clean up\ndocker compose -f examples/docker-compose-dev-rf2.yml down -v\n```\n\nThe node failure test:\n1. Indexes documents with RF=2 (each document replicated to both replica groups)\n2. Stops a Meilisearch node mid-test (`docker stop miroir-meili-1`)\n3. Verifies that searches still work using remaining replicas\n4. Restarts the node and verifies recovery\n\n## Cleanup\n\nStop and remove containers:\n\n```bash\ndocker compose -f examples/docker-compose-dev.yml down -v\n```\n\nFor RF=2 tests:\n\n```bash\ndocker compose -f examples/docker-compose-dev-rf2.yml down -v\n```\n\n## Troubleshooting\n\n### Containers not starting\n\nCheck container status:\n\n```bash\ndocker compose -f examples/docker-compose-dev.yml ps\n```\n\nCheck logs:\n\n```bash\ndocker compose -f examples/docker-compose-dev.yml logs miroir\ndocker compose -f examples/docker-compose-dev.yml logs meili-0\n```\n\n### Port conflicts\n\nIf ports 7700-7703 are already in use, modify the port mappings in `examples/docker-compose-dev.yml`.\n\nFor RF=2 tests, ensure ports 7710-7716 and 6379 are available.\n\n### Tests failing with connection refused\n\nEnsure the Docker Compose stack is running:\n\n```bash\ndocker compose -f examples/docker-compose-dev.yml ps\n```\n\nWait for all containers to be healthy before running tests.\n\n### Node failure test not working\n\nThe RF=2 test requires:\n1.
